Under Kublai the fiscal base was stronger than that of most premodern states, yet expenditures were also immense because of war, court spending, relief, canal systems, and capital construction, creating frequent pressure and occasional deficits.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调扩大税基、利用纸钞整合财赋、垄断高收益行业并保障都城与军队供给。忽必烈倾向于用南方经济盈余支撑全国统治，同时通过赦免、蠲税与赈济稳定新附地区；然而频繁工程与远征会削弱财政稳健性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized broadening the tax base, using paper currency to integrate revenue flows, monopolizing high-yield sectors, and securing supplies for the capital and the armies. Kublai sought to use the surplus of the south to sustain empire-wide rule while calming newly conquered regions through remissions and relief, but repeated large projects and overseas expeditions undermined fiscal stability.

description.zh: 帝国核心机动兵力，由蒙古诸部和色目骑兵构成，长于远程机动、复合弓骑射、侦察和快速打击，是边防与远征的精锐骨干。
description.en: The core mobile arm of the empire, composed of Mongol contingents and Inner Asian cavalry, skilled in long-range movement, composite-bow warfare, reconnaissance, and rapid shock operations.

description.zh: 由华北旧金与归附汉军组成，承担城防、驻屯、攻城、后勤护运与地方镇压，是元朝占领中国北方和维持行政秩序的重要力量。
description.en: Drawn largely from north China and former Jin military populations, these troops provided garrison duty, siege operations, convoy protection, and internal security, forming a major pillar of Yuan rule in the north.

description.zh: 主要由原南宋降军编成，数量庞大，熟悉江南水网、舟师与守城事务，在南方驻防和水陆协同作战中不可或缺，但忠诚与战斗力受编制与待遇影响较大。
description.en: Composed largely of former Southern Song troops, this massive force knew the waterways, naval service, and fortress warfare of the south. It was indispensable for southern garrisons and combined land-water operations, though its reliability varied with organization and treatment.

description.zh: 继承并整合宋朝造船和江海作战传统，部署于长江、运河、东南沿海与朝鲜方向，负责运输、巡防、海上远征与登陆支援。
description.en: Built by inheriting and reorganizing former Song naval traditions, the fleet operated on the Yangzi, the canal system, the southeast coast, and the Korean approaches, providing transport, patrol, seaborne expeditions, and landing support.

description.zh: 负责皇帝宿卫、都城防务和关键宫廷警备，由蒙古亲军与精选诸军构成，政治可靠性高于一般驻军。
description.en: Charged with protecting the emperor, defending the capital, and securing the palace, these guards were drawn from Mongol household troops and select formations and were more politically reliable than ordinary garrisons.

commanderInChief.en: Emperor Kublai Khan as Great Khan

technology.zh: 元军综合草原骑射传统与中原攻城技术，广泛使用复合弓、甲骑、攻城塔、回回炮与火药兵器雏形。其优势在战略机动、工程组织和多族兵种协同，但在海上远征和湿热地区作战方面仍有明显限制。
technology.en: The Yuan military combined steppe mounted-archery traditions with Chinese siegecraft, employing composite bows, armored cavalry, siege towers, counterweight artillery, and early gunpowder weapons. Its strengths lay in strategic mobility, engineering, and multiethnic combined operations, though it remained limited in maritime expeditions and campaigning in hot, humid environments.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括日本拒绝臣服、海上远征风险、高丽方向后勤负担、西南山地与云南局部抵抗、草原宗王政治竞争，以及察合台汗国等蒙古诸汗国关系的不确定性。
threats.en: The main threats included Japan's refusal to submit, the risks of overseas expedition, logistical burdens through Korea, resistance in the southwest and Yunnan highlands, political rivalry among steppe princes, and uncertainty in relations with other Mongol khanates such as the Chagatai Khanate.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 全身钢板铠甲（晚期），防护力顶级
description.en: Full plate steel armor (late period), top-tier protection

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

populationDescription.zh: 1280年的元朝人口约8500万，包含原金朝北方、南宋江南与西南新并入地区，是一个人口极为庞大的多民族帝国。人口仍在战后恢复中，北方不少地区尚未完全恢复至宋金盛时水平，而江南稠密农业区则构成全国人口与税赋重心。
populationDescription.en: The Yuan population in 1280 was about 85 million, encompassing the former Jin north, the former Southern Song south, and newly incorporated southwestern regions, making it one of the most populous empires of its age. Recovery from decades of warfare was still under way: many northern areas had not fully returned to earlier Song-Jin levels, while the densely cultivated south had become the main center of population and taxation.

description.zh: 南宋首都，人间天堂
description.en: Southern Song capital, paradise on earth

description.zh: 宋元时期世界最大港口
description.en: World's largest port during Song-Yuan era

socialClasses.zh: 皇室与蒙古贵族居顶层，其次为色目高官与大商人、汉地文武官员与地主士绅、僧道与宗教团体、城市商人与工匠、普通农民与军户、舟户及差役。族群身份、地域来源、职业世袭和与朝廷关系共同决定社会地位。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the imperial house and Mongol nobility, followed by Semuren high officials and major merchants, Chinese civil and military officials and local gentry, Buddhist and Daoist clergy, urban merchants and artisans, ordinary peasants and military households, and transport laborers and servile workers. Social standing depended on ethnicity, regional origin, hereditary occupation, and proximity to the court.

allies.zh: 高丽王朝是最重要的藩属与合作伙伴，在东征日本和东北秩序中扮演关键角色；与伊儿汗国总体维持较友好关系，并通过蒙古帝国网络与中亚、波斯商人及多方政权保持往来。
allies.en: The Goryeo dynasty was the Yuan's most important subordinate ally and partner, especially in the attempted invasions of Japan and in northeastern affairs. Relations with the Ilkhanate were generally favorable, and the Yuan remained connected through the wider Mongol imperial network to Central Asian, Persian, and many other polities.

enemies.zh: 日本是最显著的拒贡对象；局部抵抗势力存在于西南山地、海上与边疆地区。与察合台汗国及其他蒙古宗王之间虽未必持续全面战争，但竞争、使节纠纷与宗主权争议长期存在。
enemies.en: Japan was the most prominent non-submitting opponent. Pockets of resistance remained in the southwestern uplands, maritime zones, and other frontier regions. Relations with the Chagatai Khanate and other Mongol princely powers did not always amount to constant open war, but rivalry, diplomatic disputes, and competing claims to imperial authority persisted.

foreignPolicy.zh: 忽必烈的外交政策以天下宗主权和册封朝贡秩序为核心，同时利用蒙古帝国遗产维持跨欧亚使节与商业联系。对周边国家常先以招谕、册封和贸易诱导，拒绝者则可能施压或远征；对西方诸国则更重视情报、通商与象征性外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Kublai's foreign policy centered on universal overlordship and an investiture-tribute order, while also drawing on the legacy of the Mongol Empire to maintain trans-Eurasian diplomatic and commercial links. Neighboring states were usually approached first through demands for submission, investiture, and trade incentives, with coercion or expedition following refusal; toward more distant western powers, policy placed greater emphasis on intelligence, commerce, and symbolic diplomacy.

era.zh: 13世纪后期蒙古世界帝国时代
era.en: Late 13th-century age of the Mongol world empire

keyInnovations.zh: 纸钞国家化运用、跨区域驿站体系、整合宋代大型造船与水师技术、回回天文历算输入、改良攻城炮与火药器械、都城规划与大规模粮运组织。元朝并非所有技术都原创，但其最大特征是将草原、伊斯兰世界与中国本土技术置于同一帝国体系中加以整合。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included state-scale use of paper currency, a transregional relay-post system, the integration of large Song shipbuilding and naval techniques, the import of Islamic astronomy and mathematical methods, improved siege artillery and gunpowder devices, and sophisticated capital planning and grain-transport organization. Not all of these were originally invented by the Yuan, but its defining achievement was to integrate steppe, Islamic, and Chinese technologies within a single imperial framework.

strengths.zh: 元朝在1280年拥有当时世界最庞大的人口与经济核心之一，统一中国本部，控制草原与农耕交界，兼具蒙古军事传统和中原财政行政能力。大都作为帝国政治中心，江南作为财富腹地，驿站与海陆交通又把其嵌入横跨欧亚的贸易网络，使其综合国力极强。
strengths.en: In 1280 the Yuan possessed one of the world's largest demographic and economic cores, had unified nearly all of China proper, controlled the steppe-agrarian interface, and combined Mongol military traditions with the fiscal and administrative capacities of Chinese statecraft. With Dadu as the political center, the Yangzi south as the wealth base, and relay and maritime routes tying it into Eurasian exchange, its overall power was formidable.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于征服王朝的合法性张力、族群等级造成的社会摩擦、财政对纸钞和高强度征发的依赖、北方恢复不均、地方治理层次复杂，以及海上远征与南方湿热环境中作战的不适应。庞大的宫廷与宗王体系也会持续消耗财政和政治协调能力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in the legitimacy tensions of a conquest dynasty, social frictions produced by ethnic hierarchy, dependence on paper currency and heavy extraction, uneven northern recovery, complex local administration, and poor adaptation to maritime expedition and humid southern campaigning. The vast court and princely structure also consumed fiscal resources and political attention.

outlook.zh: 短期内元朝处于强盛上升阶段，凭借刚完成的全国统一和巨额财政动员能力，仍将保持东亚最强国家地位。但若远征日本继续受挫、财政赤字扩大、纸钞信用波动并激化社会矛盾，其长期统治稳定性将受到挑战。
outlook.en: In the short term the Yuan was in a powerful ascending phase, and with recent unification and immense mobilizational capacity it was likely to remain the strongest state in East Asia. But if the Japanese expeditions continued to fail, fiscal deficits widened, and paper-currency credibility weakened while social tensions deepened, the long-term stability of its rule would come under strain.

description.zh: 忽必烈统治下的元朝控制中国大部并为蒙古帝国最富庶的核心国家。
description.en: Under Kublai Khan, the Yuan ruled most of China and formed the wealthiest core state of the Mongol imperial world.

leaderTitle.en: Emperor of the Great Yuan and Great Khan of the Mongols

ideology.zh: 蒙汉融合治理、佛教护法、帝国大一统
ideology.en: Mongol-Chinese synthesis in governance, Buddhist patronage, imperial unification

relationship.zh: 忽必烈集团是元朝的绝对核心。忽必烈以蒙古大汗兼中原皇帝的双重身份统治帝国，推行蒙汉并用的治国方略，大力扩建大都、疏浚大运河、发行纸钞、护持藏传佛教。他的统治集团融合了蒙古宗王、色目人（西域人）官僚和部分归附的汉人世侯。
relationship.en: Kublai's faction was the absolute center of Yuan power. Ruling as both Great Khan of the Mongols and Emperor of China, Kublai pursued a dual Mongol-Chinese governing strategy, expanding Dadu, dredging the Grand Canal, issuing paper currency, and patronizing Tibetan Buddhism. His ruling circle blended Mongol princes, Semu (Central Asian) bureaucrats, and some Chinese warlord families who had submitted.

description.zh: 忽必烈（1215-1294）是成吉思汗之孙、托雷之子，1260年自立为大汗，1271年正式建立元朝。他是蒙古帝国历史上最具转型意义的统治者，将游牧征服帝国逐步改造为以中国为核心的定居帝国。忽必烈定都大都（今北京），建造壮丽的皇宫和城市规划，并将大运河延伸至大都以便南粮北运。他推行全国性纸币制度（交钞和中统钞），建立行省制度管理广袤领土，并大力扶持藏传佛教，任命八思巴为帝师，创制蒙古新字。然而他两次远征日本均告失败，对缅甸和爪哇的军事行动也收效有限。他的统治面临来自蒙古保守派和汉族士大夫的双重压力，是一位在文明交融中艰难平衡的伟大君主。
description.en: Kublai Khan (1215–1294), grandson of Chinggis Khan and son of Tolui, declared himself Great Khan in 1260 and formally established the Yuan dynasty in 1271. He was the most transformative ruler in Mongol imperial history, gradually converting a nomadic conquest empire into a sedentary state centered on China. Kublai made Dadu (modern Beijing) his capital, constructing magnificent palaces and urban infrastructure, and extended the Grand Canal to transport southern grain northward. He implemented a national paper currency system (Jiaochao and Zhongtong notes), established the provincial system to administer vast territories, and strongly patronized Tibetan Buddhism, appointing Phags-pa as Imperial Preceptor and commissioning a new Mongol script. His two invasions of Japan both ended in failure, and military campaigns against Burma and Java yielded limited results. His rule faced dual pressures from Mongol conservatives and Chinese scholar-officials, making him a great sovereign struggling to balance civilizational synthesis.

role.zh: 元朝创建者，推行蒙汉融合治国
role.en: Founder of the Yuan dynasty, pursued Mongol-Chinese synthesis

role.zh: 藏传佛教萨迦派领袖，创制蒙古新字
role.en: Leader of Sakya Tibetan Buddhism, created the new Mongol script

role.zh: 色目人财政大臣，主管元朝财税改革
role.en: Semu financial minister, oversaw Yuan fiscal reforms

ideology.zh: 维护草原传统与蒙古至上地位
ideology.en: Preserving steppe traditions and Mongol supremacy

relationship.zh: 蒙古军事贵族是元朝统治的武力基石，但他们与忽必烈的汉化路线存在深层矛盾。这些草原传统的捍卫者希望维持蒙古人的特权地位和游牧习俗，对忽必烈采用中原制度、重用色目人和部分汉人深感不满，但在军事利益的纽带下仍与皇权保持合作。
relationship.en: The Mongol military aristocracy was the armed backbone of Yuan rule, yet harbored deep tensions with Kublai's sinicization policies. As defenders of steppe traditions, they sought to maintain Mongol privilege and nomadic customs, resenting Kublai's adoption of Chinese institutions and employment of Semu and some Chinese officials, but remained allied with imperial power through shared military interests.

description.zh: 蒙古军事贵族由成吉思汗以来的黄金家族宗王、千户长后裔和功臣那颜家族构成，是蒙古帝国征服世界的核心力量。在元朝体制下，他们享有法律特权——蒙古人犯罪不受汉法审判，拥有大量牧地和赐田，世袭军事指挥权。许多蒙古贵族仍然保持半游牧生活方式，每年随皇帝在大都和上都之间迁移。他们对忽必烈推行科举、重用儒生和色目官僚的政策心存疑虑，认为这会侵蚀蒙古人的统治根基。早先海都等西北宗王的叛乱正是这种不满的极端表现。蒙古贵族在四等人制度中居于最高等级，但随着帝国日趋定居化和官僚化，他们的传统优势正在被行政能力更强的群体所稀释。
description.en: The Mongol military aristocracy comprised Golden Family princes descended from Chinggis Khan, descendants of commanders of thousands, and meritorious noyan families—the core force behind the Mongol world conquest. Under the Yuan system, they enjoyed legal privileges: Mongols were exempt from Chinese law, possessed vast pastures and granted estates, and held hereditary military commands. Many Mongol nobles maintained semi-nomadic lifestyles, migrating annually with the emperor between Dadu and Shangdu. They viewed Kublai's promotion of civil service examinations, employment of Confucian scholars, and reliance on Semu bureaucrats with suspicion, fearing erosion of Mongol ruling foundations. The earlier rebellion of Qaidu and other northwestern princes exemplified this discontent in extreme form. The Mongol nobility ranked highest in the four-class system, but as the empire became increasingly sedentary and bureaucratized, their traditional advantages were being diluted by groups with stronger administrative capabilities.

role.zh: 灭南宋统帅，蒙古军事传统的杰出代表
role.en: Commander who conquered Southern Song, outstanding representative of Mongol military tradition

role.zh: 忽必烈之子，负责北方草原防务
role.en: Kublai's son, responsible for northern steppe defense

ideology.zh: 儒家治国理政、恢复科举与文官制度
ideology.en: Confucian governance, restoration of civil service examinations

relationship.zh: 汉人儒臣在元朝体制中处于矛盾地位。他们中的一部分人接受了元朝统治的现实，试图通过在体制内服务来推动儒家理念的实施；但他们在四等人制度中地位低下，科举被长期搁置，仕途受阻，对蒙古和色目人的特权深感不平。他们与皇权的关系是被动合作中夹杂着深层抵触。
relationship.en: Chinese Confucian bureaucrats occupied an ambivalent position within the Yuan system. Some accepted Yuan rule as reality and sought to promote Confucian principles through service within the system. Yet they ranked low in the four-class hierarchy, civil service examinations were long suspended, career paths were blocked, and they deeply resented Mongol and Semu privileges. Their relationship with imperial power was one of passive cooperation mixed with underlying resistance.

description.zh: 汉人儒臣集团代表了中国士大夫传统在异族统治下的艰难存续。南宋灭亡后，大量江南文人学者面临是否出仕新朝的道德困境。一些人选择隐居不仕以示忠于旧朝，但更多人在现实压力下逐渐接受了为元朝服务。忽必烈虽然重视儒学的治国功能，先后起用许衡、刘秉忠等汉儒顾问，但始终未能真正恢复唐宋以来的科举取士制度（直到1315年才正式恢复）。儒臣们在中书省和地方行省中担任次要职位，往往受制于蒙古人和色目人上司。他们持续呼吁恢复科举、减轻赋税、抑制色目人财政官僚的权力，并努力在学术和教育领域维系儒学传统。赵孟頫等文人虽在艺术文化上取得杰出成就，但在政治上仍处于边缘化状态。
description.en: The Chinese Confucian bureaucrats represented the difficult survival of China's scholar-official tradition under foreign rule. After the fall of the Southern Song, many Jiangnan literati faced the moral dilemma of whether to serve the new dynasty. Some chose to live in seclusion to demonstrate loyalty to the old order, but more gradually accepted service to the Yuan under practical pressures. Though Kublai valued Confucianism's governing utility and appointed Chinese Confucian advisors like Xu Heng and Liu Bingzhong, he never truly restored the civil service examination system that had been central since the Tang and Song (formal restoration came only in 1315). Confucian officials held subordinate positions in the Central Secretariat and provincial administrations, often under Mongol and Semu superiors. They continuously advocated for restoring examinations, reducing taxes, curbing the power of Semu fiscal bureaucrats, and preserving Confucian scholarly and educational traditions. Literati like Zhao Mengfu achieved outstanding cultural and artistic accomplishments but remained politically marginalized.

role.zh: 元初最重要的儒学顾问，推动儒学教育制度化
role.en: Most important Confucian advisor of early Yuan, promoted institutionalization of Confucian education

role.zh: 宋朝宗室后裔，元朝最杰出的书画家和文臣
role.en: Song imperial descendant, Yuan's most outstanding calligrapher-painter and literary official

dynasty.zh: 安纳托利亚塞尔柱王朝

governmentForm.zh: 受蒙古伊儿汗国宗主权制约的突厥-波斯化君主制苏丹国，中央权威衰弱，地方军政首领与宫廷派系分权明显。
governmentForm.en: A Turkic-Persianate monarchical sultanate under Mongol Ilkhanid suzerainty, with weakened central authority and strong decentralization toward local military lords and court factions.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由宫廷与军政贵族、波斯文书官僚、乌里玛与苏菲团体、城市商人与手工业者、游牧或半游牧突厥部族、以及占人口多数的乡村农民与基督徒、亚美尼亚人、希腊人等地方社群构成。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the court and military aristocracy, Persian chancery bureaucrats, ulama and Sufi circles, urban merchants and artisans, nomadic or semi-nomadic Turkmen tribes, and a majority of rural cultivators and local Christian, Armenian, and Greek communities.

rulingClass.zh: 塞尔柱王族、宫廷埃米尔、突厥军事贵族、波斯化文官集团，以及在蒙古支配下实际掌握地方资源的总督和军阀。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the Seljuk royal house, court amirs, Turkic military aristocrats, Persianized civil administrators, and provincial governors and warlords who controlled resources under Mongol dominance.

succession.zh: 原则上为王朝世袭，但1280年前后继承常受蒙古伊儿汗国批准、宫廷政变与贵族干预影响，实际并不稳定。
succession.en: Succession was nominally dynastic and hereditary, but by around 1280 it was heavily shaped by Ilkhanid approval, court coups, and aristocratic intervention, making it unstable in practice.

structure.zh: 政府沿用塞尔柱-波斯传统，以苏丹宫廷和大维齐尔为核心，下设财政、文书、军务、司法与宗教管理机构；但在1280年，蒙古驻军、贡赋要求和地方贝伊的独立性已严重削弱中央执行力。
structure.en: The government followed Seljuk-Persian traditions, centered on the sultan's court and the grand vizier, with departments for finance, chancery, military affairs, justice, and religious administration; by 1280, however, Mongol garrisons, tribute demands, and the autonomy of local beys had greatly weakened central enforcement.

function.zh: 统筹朝政、协调各部、传达苏丹与宗主蒙古当局的命令。
function.en: Coordinated state affairs, supervised departments, and transmitted the orders of the sultan and the Mongol overlords.

function.zh: 征收地税、商业税、牧税与非常贡赋，管理国库和支出记录。
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial dues, pastoral levies, and extraordinary tribute, and managed the treasury and expenditure records.

function.zh: 起草敕令、保管档案、处理外交文书与省级任命。
function.en: Drafted decrees, kept archives, handled diplomatic correspondence, and issued provincial appointments.

function.zh: 登记军役、发放军饷、维持要塞与调度苏丹直属部队。
function.en: Registered military obligations, distributed pay, maintained fortresses, and directed troops under direct sultanic authority.

function.zh: 任命卡迪、监督教法裁判、管理宗教基金与部分城市事务。
function.en: Appointed qadis, supervised sharia adjudication, and oversaw religious endowments and some urban matters.

function.zh: 维护驿站、道路与信使网络，保障商旅和军政通讯。
function.en: Maintained relay stations, roads, and messenger networks to support trade and administrative communication.

localAdmin.zh: 地方上由省级或地区性埃米尔、总督、要塞长官和税务官管理，许多边区与高原地带实际上由土库曼贝伊与半自治军事家族控制；城市则依赖卡迪、行会首领和地方显贵维持秩序。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled by provincial amirs, governors, fortress commanders, and tax officials, though many frontier and upland areas were effectively controlled by Turkmen beys and semi-autonomous military families; in towns, qadis, guild leaders, and local notables helped maintain order.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合伊斯兰教法、苏丹敕令、部族习惯与地方社群法；穆斯林主要受卡迪法庭管辖，而希腊、亚美尼亚等基督徒社群在婚姻与宗教事务上保留内部习惯法。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic sharia, sultanic decrees, tribal custom, and communal law; Muslims were mainly subject to qadi courts, while Greek, Armenian, and other Christian communities retained internal customary law in marriage and religious matters.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠农业地税、灌溉与牧场税、市场税、关卡税、矿业和盐税、城市商业税，以及向蒙古宗主缴纳的沉重贡赋前提下加征的非常征收；税制在衰退期往往因包税与地方截留而加重百姓负担。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied mainly on agrarian land tax, irrigation and pasture dues, market taxes, customs tolls, mining and salt taxes, urban commercial levies, and extraordinary exactions imposed under the heavy burden of tribute to the Mongol overlords; in the period of decline, tax farming and local diversion often increased the burden on the population.

religion.zh: 官方主导宗教为逊尼派伊斯兰教（哈乃斐学派占优势），同时苏菲教团在安纳托利亚广泛活跃；境内仍有大量希腊正教、亚美尼亚使徒教会与叙利亚基督徒社群。
religion.en: The dominant official religion was Sunni Islam, especially the Hanafi school, while Sufi orders were highly active across Anatolia; the realm also contained large Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, and Syriac Christian communities.

philosophy.zh: 思想文化深受波斯伊斯兰政治伦理、逊尼法学、苏菲神秘主义以及边疆加齐精神影响，兼容部分古典希腊-拜占庭知识传统与地方民间信仰。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was shaped by Persian-Islamic political ethics, Sunni jurisprudence, Sufi mysticism, and frontier ghazi ideals, while also absorbing elements of classical Greek-Byzantine learning and local popular traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 行政与高雅文学主要使用波斯文和阿拉伯字母书写；阿拉伯文用于宗教与法学，突厥语在军旅和民间普遍存在但书面行政地位较弱；希腊文和亚美尼亚文继续在各自社群中使用。
writingSystem.en: Administration and elite literature were conducted mainly in Persian written in the Arabic script; Arabic was used for religion and jurisprudence, while Turkic was widespread in military and popular life but weaker in formal administration; Greek and Armenian continued in their respective communities.

culturalAchievements.zh: 鲁姆塞尔柱时期留下了安纳托利亚最重要的一批中世纪伊斯兰建筑，包括科尼亚、锡瓦斯、开塞利等地的清真寺、经学院、商队驿站与医院；石雕门户、几何装饰与波斯化宫廷文学尤具特色。此时虽已衰落，但此前数十年的建筑和苏菲文化影响仍然深远。
culturalAchievements.en: The Rum Seljuk age produced some of medieval Anatolia's most important Islamic architecture, including mosques, madrasas, caravanserais, and hospitals in Konya, Sivas, Kayseri, and elsewhere; carved stone portals, geometric ornament, and Persianate court literature were especially notable. Though in decline by 1280, the architectural and Sufi cultural legacy of earlier decades remained profound.

languageFamily.zh: 国家文化呈多语状态：统治军事精英以突厥语系为根基，行政文化高度波斯化，宗教学术使用闪含语系的阿拉伯语，地方居民还广泛使用希腊语和亚美尼亚语。
languageFamily.en: The state was multilingual: the ruling military elite was rooted in the Turkic language family, administrative culture was highly Persianized, religious scholarship used Semitic Arabic, and local populations widely spoke Greek and Armenian.

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年的鲁姆苏丹国仍拥有安纳托利亚中部农业、畜牧业、手工业和东西商路的经济基础，但蒙古贡赋、内乱、地方割据和贸易路线不稳定已明显压缩财政与市场活力，整体经济处于中等规模而走向衰退。
gdpDescription.en: In 1280 the Sultanate of Rum still possessed the economic foundations of central Anatolian agriculture, pastoralism, crafts, and east-west transit trade, but Mongol tribute, internal disorder, regional fragmentation, and unstable trade routes had clearly reduced fiscal and market vitality; the overall economy was of moderate scale but in decline.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括小麦和大麦种植、绵羊与马匹畜牧、地毯和毛织品、皮革加工、金属器制造、建筑石材、盐业、铜银等有限矿业，以及服务商队交通的驿站与贸易中介。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included wheat and barley cultivation, sheep and horse pastoralism, carpet and wool textile production, leatherworking, metalware manufacture, building stone, salt extraction, limited mining of copper and silver, and caravanserai and brokerage services tied to overland trade.

tradeGoods.zh: 对内外贸易商品有谷物、羊毛、地毯、皮革、马匹、奴隶、染料、金属制品、木材、盐和干果；输入品包括丝绸、香料、精制布匹、纸张、奢侈品和部分金属货币。
tradeGoods.en: Trade goods included grain, wool, carpets, leather, horses, slaves, dyestuffs, metal goods, timber, salt, and dried fruits; imports included silk, spices, fine textiles, paper, luxury goods, and some metallic coinage.

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备和家用织物上；城市商人与行会工匠财富差异较大，而军政贵族通过采邑、税权和土地控制远比平民富裕。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in access to arable land, livestock, grain stores, and household textiles; urban merchants and guild artisans showed wide variation in means, while military and administrative elites were far wealthier through iqta holdings, tax rights, and land control.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为以农业租税和采邑为基础的前近代混合经济，城市市场、商队贸易和行会手工业发达，但在蒙古宗主压力下愈发依赖包税、贡赋转嫁和地方武装对资源的控制。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a premodern mixed economy based on agrarian taxation and iqta assignments, with developed urban markets, caravan trade, and guild crafts, but under Mongol suzerainty it increasingly relied on tax farming, tribute transfer, and local armed control of resources.

source.en: Agrarian land tax and iqta remittances

source.zh: 市场税、关税与商路收费
source.en: Market taxes, customs, and transit dues
Pastoral dues, salt tax, and mining revenue
Dependent lands and crown estate income

category.zh: 对蒙古宗主的贡赋与附带开支
category.en: Tribute and associated payments to the Mongol overlords

category.zh: 道路、驿站、建筑与宗教基金
category.en: Roads, relay stations, construction, and religious endowments

category.zh: 赈济、外交馈赠与非常支出
category.en: Relief, diplomatic gifts, and extraordinary expenses

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，且易因蒙古征调、宫廷斗争和战时紧急支出被迅速耗尽；较大比例财富也分散在采邑持有者、地方埃米尔与宗教基金手中，中央可直接动员的现金不足。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited in size and could be quickly depleted by Mongol requisitions, court struggles, and wartime emergencies; a large share of wealth was also dispersed among iqta holders, provincial amirs, and religious endowments, leaving the center with insufficient liquid cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持贡赋、军费和最低限度行政运作为先，常通过增税、提前征收和包税弥补赤字，但这进一步削弱农村恢复能力与中央合法性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized tribute, military expenditure, and the bare functioning of government, often covering deficits through increased taxation, advance collection, and tax farming, which further weakened rural recovery and central legitimacy.

description.zh: 由宫廷近卫、采邑骑兵和精锐突厥骑射手组成，是传统塞尔柱军力核心，但在1280年人数和忠诚度均不如盛期。
description.en: Composed of court guards, iqta cavalry, and elite Turkic mounted archers, this was the traditional core of Seljuk military power, though by 1280 both numbers and loyalty were below their former peak.

name.zh: 地方埃米尔与土库曼部族骑兵
name.en: Provincial Amir and Turkmen Tribal Cavalry

description.zh: 依赖地方领主和部族首领征集，机动性强，擅长袭扰和边疆作战，但纪律不稳，常优先服从本地主人而非中央。
description.en: Raised through provincial lords and tribal chiefs, these forces were mobile and adept at raiding and frontier warfare, but unstable in discipline and often more loyal to local patrons than to the center.

description.zh: 包括城市守军、要塞步兵、弓弩手和临时征召部队，主要负责城防、护路和镇压动乱。
description.en: These included urban garrisons, fortress infantry, archers and crossbowmen, and temporary levies, mainly responsible for defense of towns, route protection, and suppression of unrest.

description.zh: 负责攻城器械、运输、辎重、道路修复和军需供应，数量不大但对长期行动必要。
description.en: Responsible for siege engines, transport, baggage, road repair, and provisioning; not numerous, but necessary for sustained operations.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹凯霍斯鲁三世（名义上）与受蒙古影响的宫廷埃米尔集团
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Kaykhusraw III nominally, alongside court amirs operating under Mongol influence

technology.zh: 军事实力仍以复合弓骑兵、长枪骑兵、要塞防御和传统攻城器械为主，盔甲、马具和石工筑城技术成熟；但缺乏能完全独立于蒙古压力的大规模常备军与统一指挥体系。
technology.en: Military capability remained centered on composite-bow cavalry, lancers, fortress defense, and traditional siege equipment, with mature armor, horse gear, and stone fortification techniques; however, it lacked the large independent standing army and unified command needed to operate free of Mongol pressure.

threats.zh: 最大威胁来自蒙古伊儿汗国的直接干预、国内诸埃米尔割据与土库曼边疆势力坐大；外部还需警惕马穆鲁克影响、拜占庭边境冲突以及奇里乞亚亚美尼亚和十字军残余势力的复杂局面。
threats.en: The greatest threats came from direct intervention by the Mongol Ilkhanate, domestic fragmentation among amirs, and the growing power of Turkmen frontier groups; externally the state also had to watch Mamluk influence, Byzantine border conflict, and the complex situation involving Cilician Armenia and remaining Crusader enclaves.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口约三百万，主要分布于安纳托利亚中部高原、农业河谷、商路节点与若干沿山地区。民族和宗教构成复杂，包括突厥穆斯林、希腊语居民、亚美尼亚人、库尔德人及其他地方社群。蒙古征发、战争与迁徙使部分地区人口流动明显。
populationDescription.en: The population was about three million, concentrated in the central Anatolian plateau, agricultural valleys, trade-route nodes, and some mountain districts. It was ethnically and religiously mixed, including Turkic Muslims, Greek-speaking inhabitants, Armenians, Kurds, and other local communities. Mongol requisitioning, war, and migration caused notable population movement in some regions.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层大体包括苏丹与王族、宫廷和军事贵族、波斯文官与宗教学者、城市商人和行会工匠、游牧土库曼部族、农村佃农与自耕农，以及希腊、亚美尼亚等非穆斯林纳税社群与奴仆人口。
socialClasses.en: Social classes broadly included the sultan and royal house, court and military aristocracy, Persian bureaucrats and religious scholars, urban merchants and guild artisans, nomadic Turkmen tribes, rural tenants and peasant proprietors, and non-Muslim tax-paying Greek and Armenian communities as well as servile populations.

allies.zh: 鲁姆苏丹国在1280年几乎没有真正意义上的稳定强盟，更多是被迫依附伊儿汗国；部分宫廷派系会寻求与马穆鲁克或地方安纳托利亚势力秘密接触，以制衡蒙古影响。
allies.en: In 1280 the Sultanate of Rum had almost no stable great-power allies in the full sense, functioning instead in forced dependence on the Ilkhanate; some court factions sought clandestine contacts with the Mamluks or local Anatolian powers to balance Mongol influence.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对与压力来源并非单一国家，而是伊儿汗国的控制要求、国内离心贵族、边疆土库曼势力、与拜占庭及奇里乞亚亚美尼亚之间的边境摩擦，以及受马穆鲁克鼓动的反蒙古势力。
enemies.en: Its main enemies and pressures came not from a single state alone but from Ilkhanid control, centrifugal domestic nobles, frontier Turkmen groups, border friction with Byzantium and Cilician Armenia, and anti-Mongol elements encouraged by the Mamluks.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以生存为核心：表面上服从蒙古、按期贡纳并接受干预，实际上尽量在伊儿汗、马穆鲁克、拜占庭和地方安纳托利亚诸势力之间保持回旋，以保存王朝名号和都城科尼亚的控制。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was centered on survival: outwardly the sultanate submitted to Mongol authority, paid tribute, and accepted interference, while in practice trying to maneuver among the Ilkhanate, Mamluks, Byzantines, and local Anatolian powers in order to preserve the dynasty's title and hold on Konya.

era.zh: 中世纪后期欧亚交流时代
era.en: Late Medieval Age of Eurasian Exchange

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术和制度优势体现在成熟的石造建筑与拱顶工程、商队驿站网络、复合弓骑兵战术、城市手工业组织、灌溉与粮食储运体系，以及吸收波斯、阿拉伯和拜占庭传统的医学、天文学和法学知识。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and institutional strengths included mature stone architecture and vaulting, a caravanserai network, composite-bow cavalry warfare, urban craft organization, irrigation and grain-storage systems, and medical, astronomical, and legal knowledge drawing on Persian, Arabic, and Byzantine traditions.

strengths.zh: 鲁姆苏丹国仍控制安纳托利亚腹地的重要交通线和农业区，拥有深厚的塞尔柱行政传统、发达的城市建筑遗产、多民族商业网络，以及能在边疆快速集结骑兵的能力。
strengths.en: The Sultanate of Rum still controlled important inland Anatolian routes and agricultural zones, possessed a deep Seljuk administrative tradition, a developed urban architectural legacy, multiethnic commercial networks, and the ability to mobilize cavalry rapidly on the frontier.

weaknesses.zh: 其根本弱点是蒙古宗主压迫下的主权缺失、王位软弱、贵族和地方军阀离心、财政赤字、税基流失以及对地方土库曼和采邑武装的依赖，导致中央难以持续统治全国。
weaknesses.en: Its fundamental weaknesses were loss of sovereignty under Mongol overlordship, a weak throne, centrifugal nobles and provincial warlords, fiscal deficit, erosion of the tax base, and dependence on local Turkmen and iqta armed forces, all of which made sustained central rule difficult.

outlook.zh: 前景总体黯淡。若伊儿汗国继续强力干预、宫廷斗争不止、地方贝伊壮大，则鲁姆苏丹国很可能进一步瓦解为若干安纳托利亚侯国；王朝名义可能暂时保留，但实权将持续缩小。
outlook.en: The outlook was broadly bleak. If Ilkhanid intervention continued, court factionalism persisted, and local beys grew stronger, the Sultanate of Rum was likely to fragment further into multiple Anatolian principalities; the dynastic title might survive for a time, but real power would continue to shrink.

description.zh: 在蒙古宗主权下苟存的安纳托利亚塞尔柱国家，权威日趋衰弱。
description.en: An Anatolian Seljuk state surviving under Mongol suzerainty, with weakening authority.

rulerTitle.zh: 罗马人的皇帝与独裁者
rulerTitle.en: Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans

governmentForm.zh: 基督教罗马君主专制帝国，带有高度宫廷化的官僚体系与地方军政并行结构
governmentForm.en: A Christian Roman autocratic empire with a highly court-centered bureaucracy and parallel provincial civil-military administration

socialStructure.zh: 社会以皇帝与宫廷贵族为顶层，其下为高级教士、文官、军功贵族与地方大地主，中层包括城市商人、手工业者与下级官吏，底层为自由农民、佃农、海员与依附性劳作者。13世纪后贵族化趋势增强，地方显贵与大地产控制力上升。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the emperor and court aristocracy, followed by senior clergy, civil officials, military nobles, and provincial landholders. The middle strata included urban merchants, artisans, and minor officials, while the lower strata consisted of free peasants, tenant cultivators, sailors, and dependent laborers. By the 13th century, aristocratization had intensified, with magnates and large estates gaining increasing local power.

rulingClass.zh: 皇室、巴列奥略宗族、军政贵族家族、首都高级文官与东正教高阶教士
rulingClass.en: The imperial family, the Palaiologos clan, military-civil aristocratic houses, senior Constantinopolitan bureaucrats, and high Orthodox clergy

succession.zh: 原则上为世袭君主制，由在位皇帝指定或拥立共治皇帝以确保继承，但仍需宫廷、军队、教会与首都政治承认
succession.en: In principle a hereditary monarchy, with the reigning emperor often designating or crowning a co-emperor to secure succession, though effective accession still required acceptance by the court, army, church, and the politics of Constantinople

structure.zh: 1280年的帝国政府已不再具中期拜占庭的庞大整齐体系，而是在恢复首都后重建中央宫廷行政。皇帝通过宫廷、财政部门、司法机关、外交秘书机构与地方总督实施统治。旧有军区制已大幅衰退，地方更多依赖行省总督、边防军官与地方贵族合作。
structure.en: By 1280 the imperial government no longer possessed the vast and orderly machinery of middle Byzantine times, but had rebuilt a central court administration after recovering the capital. The emperor ruled through the palace, fiscal offices, judicial bodies, diplomatic secretariats, and provincial governors. The old theme system had greatly declined, and the provinces relied more on governors, frontier commanders, and cooperation with local aristocrats.

function.zh: 负责宫廷礼仪、诏令传达、皇室起居、授爵与接待外国使节，是政治权威公开展示的核心
function.en: Handled court ceremony, transmission of decrees, imperial household management, bestowal of dignities, and reception of foreign envoys, serving as the public core of political authority

function.zh: 征收地税、关税与商业税，管理国库、薪饷、铸币与皇室收入
function.en: Collected land taxes, customs, and commercial dues, and managed the treasury, salaries, coinage, and imperial revenues

function.zh: 负责法令解释、诉讼审理、公文起草、财产权确认与地方司法监督
function.en: Oversaw interpretation of law, adjudication of cases, drafting of official documents, confirmation of property rights, and supervision of provincial justice

function.zh: 处理与拉丁诸国、保加利亚、塞尔维亚、安纳托利亚突厥政权、蒙古诸汗国及教廷的通信、条约与情报事务
function.en: Managed correspondence, treaties, and intelligence regarding the Latin powers, Bulgaria, Serbia, Anatolian Turkish states, the Mongol khanates, and the papacy

name.zh: 地方行政与军务联络机构

function.zh: 协调行省总督、边境守军、舰队补给与地方赋役征发
function.en: Coordinated provincial governors, frontier garrisons, fleet provisioning, and the levying of local taxes and service obligations

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由行省总督、税务官、法官、城镇长官与边境军官共同运作。色雷斯、马其顿及爱琴海沿岸地区受首都控制较强，而小亚细亚残存领地与边区更依赖地方军政贵族和堡垒网络。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was run jointly by provincial governors, tax officers, judges, urban authorities, and frontier commanders. Thrace, Macedonia, and the Aegean littoral were more firmly controlled from the capital, while the remaining territories in Asia Minor and frontier zones depended more heavily on local military-administrative aristocrats and fortress networks.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以罗马法—拜占庭法传统为基础，辅以皇帝敕令、教会法与司法惯例。财产、婚姻、继承和商业契约均有成熟法理，但在地方执行上常受贵族权势、军情与财政需要影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on the Roman-Byzantine legal tradition, supplemented by imperial decrees, canon law, and judicial custom. Property, marriage, inheritance, and commercial contracts all had developed jurisprudence, though enforcement in the provinces was often shaped by aristocratic influence, military conditions, and fiscal necessity.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以土地税、人口与户役性征收、贸易关税、港口税、市场税和特许收入为主。恢复帝国后的财政基础较为脆弱，税负集中于仍受控制的农业区与海关贸易节点，部分土地则通过普罗诺亚形式授予军政人员以换取服务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on land tax, household and service-based levies, customs duties, port taxes, market dues, and concessionary income. The restored empire's fiscal base was fragile, so burdens fell heavily on the remaining agricultural districts and maritime customs points, while some lands were granted in pronoia form to military and administrative personnel in return for service.

religion.zh: 东正教基督教为国教与社会核心。尽管米海尔八世在里昂第二次大公会议后推动与罗马教会联合以换取外交利益，但在1280年帝国内部大多数教士、修士与民众仍强烈坚持东正教立场，反对拉丁化。
religion.en: Eastern Orthodox Christianity was the state religion and the core of society. Although Michael VIII promoted union with the Roman Church after the Second Council of Lyons in pursuit of diplomatic advantage, by 1280 most clergy, monks, and laypeople within the empire still strongly upheld Orthodox positions and opposed Latinization.

philosophy.zh: 思想传统结合希腊教父神学、亚里士多德逻辑、修辞教育与罗马政治理念。13世纪后期可见古典学术复兴趋势，学者重视文本校勘、哲学争辩与历史书写。
philosophy.en: The intellectual tradition combined Greek patristic theology, Aristotelian logic, rhetorical education, and Roman political concepts. In the late 13th century there were signs of renewed classicism, with scholars valuing textual study, philosophical disputation, and historical writing.

writingSystem.zh: 以希腊字母书写中世纪希腊语，官方文书、神学著作、法律文本和史书均使用高度成熟的书面传统
writingSystem.en: Used the Greek alphabet to write Medieval Greek, with a highly developed written tradition for official documents, theology, legal texts, and historiography

culturalAchievements.zh: 恢复君士坦丁堡后，帝国在宫廷礼仪、神学辩论、编年史写作、修道院文化、圣像与马赛克艺术方面重新活跃。巴列奥略时期开启了所谓“巴列奥略复兴”，体现在古典文献研究、宗教艺术更新与知识网络重建上。
culturalAchievements.en: After the recovery of Constantinople, the empire saw renewed activity in court ceremonial, theological debate, chronicle writing, monastic culture, and icon and mosaic art. The Palaiologan period initiated the so-called 'Palaiologan Renaissance,' visible in classical scholarship, renewed religious art, and the rebuilding of intellectual networks.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系希腊语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Hellenic branch

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年的拜占庭经济已较1204年前的帝国大幅缩小，但仍保有东地中海重要商路节点、富庶的色雷斯农业区、丝绸与手工业传统，以及首都市场的吸纳能力。经济规模在巴尔干和东地中海诸国中仍具分量，但对热那亚、威尼斯人及地方贵族网络依赖增强。
gdpDescription.en: By 1280 the Byzantine economy was greatly reduced from its pre-1204 imperial scale, yet it still retained important eastern Mediterranean trade nodes, the productive agriculture of Thrace, traditions of silk and urban crafts, and the pull of the Constantinopolitan market. Its economic weight remained significant in the Balkans and eastern Mediterranean, but dependence on Genoese, Venetian, and aristocratic networks had increased.

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食种植、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、畜牧业、丝绸织造、金属加工、造船修船、陶器、建筑和港口贸易服务
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, wine and olive oil production, pastoralism, silk weaving, metalworking, shipbuilding and repair, ceramics, construction, and port trade services

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、蜡、皮革、丝织品、染料、盐、木材、奴隶、金属制品，以及经转口贸易流通的香料、明矾和东方奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, wax, leather, silk textiles, dyestuffs, salt, timber, slaves, metal goods, and through re-export trade spices, alum, and eastern luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、工具、粮储与家庭劳力；城市居民则以作坊、店铺、库存与现金为主。贵族家庭集中拥有庄园、税权、役权和奢侈消费品，贫富差距明显。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary peasant household wealth lay mainly in land-use rights, livestock, tools, grain reserves, and family labor; urban residents held wealth in workshops, shops, inventories, and cash. Aristocratic households concentrated wealth in estates, fiscal rights, labor claims, and luxury goods, making inequality pronounced.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业租佃与地税为基础、由国家财政和贵族地产共同主导的混合经济，同时依赖海关税和地中海商业。政府对货币、关税与特许经营仍有影响力，但意大利海商在海上贸易中的地位不断上升。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy grounded in agrarian rents and land taxation, shaped jointly by the state fiscal system and aristocratic estates, while also relying heavily on customs and Mediterranean commerce. The government still influenced coinage, tariffs, and concessions, but Italian maritime merchants were increasingly dominant in overseas trade.

source.en: Customs, port dues, and commercial taxes

source.zh: 人头性杂税、司法罚金与临时征敛
source.en: Poll-type dues, judicial fines, and extraordinary levies

source.zh: 贡赋、附庸义务与其他收入
source.en: Tributes, vassal obligations, and other income

category.en: Court, ceremonial, and imperial household expenses

category.zh: 宗教机构、公共修缮与赈济
category.en: Religious institutions, public repairs, and relief

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库在恢复首都后已得到一定重建，但远不及科穆宁时期。其现金储备足以支撑短期战事、外交赠礼与宫廷支出，却难以长期承担多线战争或大规模舰队重建。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury had been partially rebuilt after the recovery of the capital, but remained far below Komnenian levels. Its cash reserves could sustain short-term campaigning, diplomatic gifts, and court expenditure, yet were insufficient for prolonged multi-front war or major naval reconstruction.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持首都、军队与外交平衡为优先，强调压缩开支、加紧征税、利用普罗诺亚授地减轻现金军费，并通过教会联合与外交通婚争取减少西方威胁。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized balancing the needs of the capital, the army, and diplomacy. It emphasized restrained spending, intensified taxation, use of pronoia grants to reduce direct cash military costs, and diplomatic union and marriages to lessen western threats.

description.zh: 以皇帝直属部队、近卫、骑兵与受薪步兵为核心，负责巴尔干和首都周边机动作战
description.en: Centered on imperial troops, guards, cavalry, and salaried infantry, responsible for mobile operations in the Balkans and around the capital

description.zh: 分驻色雷斯、马其顿、爱琴海岛屿及小亚细亚残余据点，承担守城、巡防与边境警戒
description.en: Stationed across Thrace, Macedonia, the Aegean islands, and remaining positions in Asia Minor, tasked with fortress defense, patrol, and frontier watch

description.zh: 规模较前代明显缩小，主要负责海峡、首都海运线与爱琴海沿岸防卫，部分依赖盟友或雇佣船队
description.en: Much reduced compared to earlier centuries, mainly responsible for the straits, the capital's sea lines, and Aegean coastal defense, with partial reliance on allied or hired fleets

description.zh: 包括突厥、拉丁、阿兰、库曼等来源不一的外来兵力，用于补充骑兵、弓骑兵和特殊作战需求
description.en: Included foreign troops of varied origins such as Turks, Latins, Alans, and Cumans, supplementing cavalry, horse archers, and specialized military needs

commanderInChief.zh: 皇帝米海尔八世·巴列奥略

technology.zh: 军备以链甲、札甲、长矛、剑、弓弩、攻城器械和坚固城防为主，海军仍知晓并有限使用希腊火传统，但整体海上技术与舰队规模已不及帝国盛期。
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail, lamellar armor, spears, swords, bows and crossbows, siege engines, and strong fortifications. The navy still retained knowledge of the Greek fire tradition in limited form, but overall naval technology and fleet scale were below the empire's high medieval peak.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方拉丁势力尤其安茹家族恢复拉丁帝国的企图、保加利亚与塞尔维亚在巴尔干的竞争、小亚细亚突厥边境压力，以及海上对热那亚和威尼斯势力的依赖与牵制。
threats.en: Major threats came from western Latin powers, especially Angevin ambitions to restore the Latin Empire, from Bulgarian and Serbian competition in the Balkans, from Turkish frontier pressure in Asia Minor, and from strategic dependence on and rivalry with Genoese and Venetian maritime power.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口在1204年后明显下降且地域分布破碎。1280年的人口主要集中于色雷斯、马其顿、沿海城市、爱琴海岛屿及若干仍受控制的小亚细亚地区。君士坦丁堡虽已恢复为最大城市，但尚未完全恢复到拉丁征服前水平。
populationDescription.en: The empire's population had declined significantly after 1204 and was unevenly distributed. In 1280 it was concentrated chiefly in Thrace, Macedonia, coastal cities, the Aegean islands, and a few remaining areas of Asia Minor. Constantinople had again become the largest city, but had not fully recovered its pre-Latin-conquest level.

description.zh: 拜占庭帝国首都，东西方交汇之城
description.en: Byzantine capital, city at the crossroads of East and West

socialClasses.zh: 皇室与高等贵族、高级教士、军政官僚、中小地主、城市商人与工匠、自由农民、佃农、海员、雇工与少量奴仆。少数拉丁人、亚美尼亚人、犹太人和其他外来社群活跃于商业与港口生活中。
socialClasses.en: The social order included the imperial family and high aristocracy, senior clergy, military and civil officials, lesser landholders, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenant farmers, sailors, wage laborers, and a smaller number of slaves or servants. Minority Latins, Armenians, Jews, and other outside communities were active in commerce and port life.

allies.zh: 主要依靠与热那亚的合作关系、对部分蒙古势力的务实接触，以及通过婚姻和条约分化巴尔干与拉丁诸势力；不存在绝对稳固的长期盟友体系。
allies.en: The empire relied chiefly on cooperation with Genoa, pragmatic contacts with some Mongol powers, and the use of marriages and treaties to divide Balkan and Latin rivals; it lacked a fully stable long-term alliance system.

enemies.zh: 安茹王朝的西西里与其拉丁盟友、部分威尼斯利益集团、敌对的保加利亚与塞尔维亚势力，以及安纳托利亚边境上的突厥政权
enemies.en: The Angevin kingdom of Sicily and its Latin partners, parts of the Venetian interest network, hostile Bulgarian and Serbian forces, and Turkish polities on the Anatolian frontier

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交战略以保住君士坦丁堡和阻止西方十字军式复辟为第一目标。米海尔八世通过教会联合、婚姻外交、贿赂、分化敌人及有限军事干预来争取生存空间，同时尽力维持对巴尔干和爱琴海的影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was primarily aimed at holding Constantinople and preventing a western crusading-style restoration of Latin rule. Michael VIII sought strategic survival through church union, marriage diplomacy, bribery, dividing enemies, and limited military intervention, while trying to preserve influence in the Balkans and Aegean.

era.zh: 中世纪后期东地中海帝国技术阶段
era.en: Late medieval eastern Mediterranean imperial technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 其关键优势不在全新发明，而在长期积累的城市防御、穹顶建筑、文书行政、铸币、攻城术、海峡航运管理、丝织工艺与古典知识保存。巴列奥略时期在学术抄写、图像艺术与建筑修复方面尤见复兴。
keyInnovations.en: Its key strengths lay less in wholly new inventions than in long-developed capacities in urban fortification, domed construction, documentary administration, coinage, siegecraft, strait navigation management, silk manufacture, and preservation of classical knowledge. The Palaiologan period was especially marked by revival in manuscript culture, visual art, and architectural restoration.

strengths.zh: 拥有君士坦丁堡这一无可替代的战略首都、成熟的罗马官僚与法律传统、深厚的正教文化凝聚力、仍具价值的海关与贸易税源，以及灵活而老练的外交手腕。
strengths.en: It possessed the irreplaceable strategic capital of Constantinople, a mature Roman bureaucratic and legal tradition, the cohesion of Orthodox culture, valuable customs and trade revenues, and a flexible and sophisticated diplomatic tradition.

weaknesses.zh: 领土缩小、税基不足、海军衰落、对雇佣兵和意大利海商依赖过重、安纳托利亚防线脆弱、地方贵族势力坐大，并因教会联合政策引发严重内部宗教反弹。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included reduced territory, a narrow tax base, naval decline, heavy reliance on mercenaries and Italian maritime merchants, a fragile Anatolian frontier, the growing power of provincial aristocrats, and severe internal religious backlash caused by the church-union policy.

outlook.zh: 1280年的帝国处于相对稳定但高压的平衡状态。若西方安茹威胁被成功遏制，帝国仍可能在巴尔干维持数十年生存并实现有限文化复兴；但若财政进一步恶化、海军无法恢复且小亚细亚继续失守，则长期衰退几乎不可避免。
outlook.en: In 1280 the empire stood in a relatively stable but highly pressured equilibrium. If Angevin western threats were successfully checked, it could still survive for decades in the Balkans and achieve a limited cultural revival; but if finances worsened further, naval recovery failed, and Asia Minor continued to be lost, long-term decline would become nearly unavoidable.

description.zh: 1261年复辟后的拜占庭重新控制君士坦丁堡，但在巴尔干与安纳托利亚面临多方压力。
description.en: Restored in 1261, Byzantium again held Constantinople but faced pressure in both the Balkans and Anatolia.

leaderTitle.zh: 罗马人的皇帝与独裁者
leaderTitle.en: Emperor and Autocrat of the Romans

ideology.zh: 帝国复兴、外交均衡与西方联合
ideology.en: Imperial restoration, diplomatic balancing, and union with the West

relationship.zh: 巴列奥略王朝于1261年收复君士坦丁堡，结束了拉丁帝国的统治，标志着拜占庭帝国的复辟。米海尔八世通过精妙的外交手段——包括与热那亚结盟、推动里昂教会联合——在西方和东方势力间艰难维持帝国的生存。
relationship.en: The Palaiologan dynasty recaptured Constantinople in 1261, ending the Latin Empire and restoring Byzantine rule. Michael VIII maintained the empire's survival through sophisticated diplomacy—including the Genoese alliance and the Union of Lyon—while navigating between Western and Eastern powers.

description.zh: 米海尔八世·巴列奥略是拜占庭帝国最后一个王朝的创始人，1261年他利用拉丁帝国的虚弱，联合热那亚人成功收复君士坦丁堡。他面临的最大威胁是西西里的安茹王朝查理一世企图重建拉丁帝国的野心。为化解此威胁，米海尔在1274年接受了里昂教会联合，承认教皇权威，但此举遭到国内东正教会和民众的强烈反对。他还通过资助西西里晚祷事件(1282)成功瓦解了安茹的入侵计划。然而帝国领土已大为缩小，财政窘迫，军事力量有限，不得不依赖外交和雇佣军维持生存。巴列奥略时代虽在文化上开创了'巴列奥略文艺复兴'，但帝国的实力已今非昔比。
description.en: Michael VIII Palaiologos founded the last Byzantine dynasty, exploiting Latin Empire weakness and allying with Genoa to recapture Constantinople in 1261. His greatest threat was Charles of Anjou's ambition to restore the Latin Empire. To counter this, Michael accepted the Union of Lyon in 1274, recognizing papal authority—a deeply unpopular move domestically among the Orthodox Church and populace. He also funded the Sicilian Vespers (1282), which successfully dismantled Charles's invasion plans. Yet the empire's territory was greatly reduced, finances strained, and military capacity limited, forcing reliance on diplomacy and mercenaries. Though the Palaiologan era produced a cultural 'Palaiologan Renaissance,' the empire's power was a shadow of its former self.

role.zh: 帝国复辟者，精通外交权术
role.en: Restorer of the empire, master diplomat

role.zh: 米海尔之子，继承人，后来废除里昂联合
role.en: Michael's son and heir, later repudiated the Union of Lyon

ideology.zh: 地方自治与大土地所有制
ideology.en: Provincial autonomy and large-scale land ownership

relationship.zh: 地方贵族大地主是帝国统治的双刃剑。他们提供了军事人才和地方治理能力，但同时通过积累土地侵蚀了自由农民阶层和国家税基。这些家族常与皇室联姻，在宫廷中拥有巨大影响力，但也时常挑战中央权威。
relationship.en: Provincial aristocratic magnates were a double-edged sword for imperial rule. They provided military talent and local governance capacity but simultaneously eroded the free peasantry and the state's tax base through land accumulation. These families frequently intermarried with the imperial house and wielded enormous influence at court while periodically challenging central authority.

description.zh: 拜占庭帝国晚期的贵族大地主集团是一个由军事世家和地方豪门构成的松散利益群体。复辟后的帝国中，坎塔库泽诺斯、杜卡斯、菲兰特罗佩诺斯等家族控制着色雷斯、马其顿和爱琴海岛屿的大片土地。他们通过普罗尼亚（军事采邑）制度获得土地使用权，逐渐将这些临时性授予转化为世袭财产。自由小农的持续减少削弱了帝国的征兵和税收基础，而这些大家族的私人武装力量却在增长。在政治上，他们与巴列奥略皇室既合作又竞争，构成了帝国内部最重要的政治力量之一，他们的忠诚度往往取决于皇帝能否满足其利益诉求。
description.en: The aristocratic land magnates of late Byzantium were a loose interest group of military families and provincial grandees. In the restored empire, families like the Kantakouzenoi, Doukai, and Philanthropenoi controlled vast estates across Thrace, Macedonia, and the Aegean islands. Through the pronoia (military fief) system they obtained land grants, gradually converting these temporary concessions into hereditary property. The steady decline of free smallholders weakened the empire's recruitment and tax base, while these great families' private armed forces grew. Politically, they both cooperated and competed with the Palaiologan court, forming one of the empire's most significant internal forces, with their loyalty contingent on the emperor's ability to satisfy their interests.

name.zh: 阿莱克修斯·菲兰特罗佩诺斯

role.zh: 小亚细亚地区杰出将领，后因叛乱被捕
role.en: Outstanding general in Asia Minor, later arrested for rebellion

name.zh: 约翰·坎塔库泽诺斯家族

role.zh: 色雷斯地区最富有的贵族家族之一，后出一位皇帝
role.en: One of the wealthiest noble families in Thrace, later produced an emperor

ideology.zh: 神秘主义东正教神学，反对拉丁联合
ideology.en: Mystical Orthodox theology, opposition to Latin Union

relationship.zh: 静修派代表了拜占庭东正教中最虔诚和保守的力量。他们坚决反对米海尔八世的里昂教会联合政策，认为与罗马的妥协是对正统信仰的背叛。静修派虽非政治组织，但通过其深厚的民间影响力和修道院网络，对帝国宗教政策产生了重要制衡作用。
relationship.en: The Hesychasts represented the most devout and conservative force within Byzantine Orthodoxy. They firmly opposed Michael VIII's Union of Lyon policy, viewing any compromise with Rome as a betrayal of the true faith. Though not a political organization, the Hesychasts exerted significant counterbalancing influence on imperial religious policy through their deep popular appeal and monastic networks.

description.zh: 静修主义（Hesychasm）是东正教神秘主义传统中的核心修行方式，强调通过内在祈祷、静默冥想和心灵净化来直接体验神圣之光。虽然格里高利·帕拉马斯——静修主义最伟大的神学家——要到14世纪中叶才系统阐述其教义，但1280年代的阿索斯山修道院群已经是这一传统的重要中心。静修派修士反对米海尔八世为对抗安茹威胁而推行的里昂联合（1274），视其为对东正教信仰完整性的严重威胁。他们在民众中享有崇高的精神权威，修道院也是抵制拉丁化的文化堡垒。当安德洛尼卡二世继位后废除里昂联合时，很大程度上是回应了以静修派为代表的东正教民意。这一运动后来深刻塑造了拜占庭晚期的精神文化面貌。
description.en: Hesychasm was a central spiritual practice within the Orthodox mystical tradition, emphasizing direct experience of divine light through inner prayer, silent contemplation, and purification of the heart. Although Gregory Palamas—Hesychasm's greatest theologian—would not systematically articulate its doctrines until the mid-14th century, the monastic communities on Mount Athos were already a vital center of this tradition in the 1280s. Hesychast monks opposed Michael VIII's Union of Lyon (1274), adopted to counter the Angevin threat, viewing it as a grave threat to Orthodox doctrinal integrity. They commanded deep spiritual authority among the populace, and the monasteries served as cultural bastions against Latinization. When Andronikos II later repudiated the Union upon succeeding his father, he was largely responding to Orthodox popular sentiment led by the Hesychasts. This movement would profoundly shape the spiritual and cultural landscape of late Byzantium.

role.zh: 东正教修道传统的守护者，反拉丁联合运动的精神领袖
role.en: Guardians of the Orthodox monastic tradition, spiritual leaders of the anti-Latin Union movement

name.zh: 君士坦丁堡牧首约翰十一世

role.zh: 支持里昂联合的牧首，被静修派视为对手
role.en: Patriarch who supported the Union of Lyon, viewed as an adversary by the Hesychasts

governmentForm.zh: 选举君主制下的封建帝国，皇权有限，诸侯、公国、主教辖区与自由城市拥有广泛自治权
governmentForm.en: A feudal elective monarchy in which imperial authority was limited and princes, duchies, bishoprics, and free cities possessed extensive autonomy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由高级贵族、教会阶层、骑士、小贵族、城市市民、行会工匠、自由农与农奴组成，各地区差异显著
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of high nobility, clergy, knights, lesser nobles, urban burghers, guild artisans, free peasants, and serfs, with strong regional variation

rulingClass.zh: 世俗诸侯、采邑主教与大修道院长构成帝国统治核心，国王须依赖选侯与地方领主合作施政
rulingClass.en: Secular princes, prince-bishops, and great abbots formed the core ruling elite, while the king depended on cooperation from electors and territorial lords

succession.zh: 并非严格世袭，罗马人的国王由主要诸侯选举产生，哈布斯堡家族在1280年虽掌权但地位尚未绝对稳固
succession.en: Succession was not strictly hereditary; the King of the Romans was chosen by leading princes, and although the Habsburgs held power in 1280 their position was not yet unassailable

structure.zh: 帝国政府缺乏统一常设中央官僚机构，核心由国王宫廷、王室书记局、宫廷法院、封臣会议与地区性领主行政体系构成；意大利与德意志地区在实际治理上高度分散
structure.en: The empire lacked a unified permanent central bureaucracy; its core consisted of the royal court, chancery, court tribunals, feudal assemblies, and the administrative systems of territorial lords. Germany and imperial Italy were highly decentralized in practice

function.zh: 起草特许状、敕令、封地确认文书与外交函件，维系国王对诸侯和教会的法理权威
function.en: Drafted charters, decrees, confirmations of fiefs, and diplomatic correspondence, sustaining the king's legal authority over princes and the Church

function.zh: 处理王权直辖事务、封建纠纷、领地争议与部分高级司法案件，但执行依赖地方领主
function.en: Handled royal demesne matters, feudal disputes, territorial conflicts, and some high judicial cases, though enforcement depended on local lords

function.zh: 管理哈布斯堡家族领地收入、王室关税、铸币收益、犹太人保护税与临时征收
function.en: Managed revenues from Habsburg lands, royal tolls, mint profits, Jewish taxes, and extraordinary levies

function.zh: 负责王室随行宫廷、加冕礼仪、饮食仓储、差旅与使节接待
function.en: Oversaw the itinerant royal household, coronation ceremony, provisioning, travel, and reception of envoys

name.zh: 帝国封臣与诸侯联络署
name.en: Office for Imperial Princes and Vassal Relations

function.zh: 协调诸侯效忠、召开宫廷会议、处理封地授予与政治调停
function.en: Coordinated princely loyalty, convened court assemblies, and managed grants of fiefs and political mediation

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理主要由公国、侯国、伯国、采邑主教区、帝国修道院、自由帝国城市与骑士领地自行实施。国王在奥地利等王室领地影响较强，在广大帝国范围内则多依赖封建契约、司法确认与临时巡行施政
localAdmin.en: Local administration was mainly conducted by duchies, margraviates, counties, prince-bishoprics, imperial abbeys, free imperial cities, and knightly lordships. The king exercised stronger authority in his dynastic lands such as Austria, but across the wider empire he relied on feudal bonds, judicial confirmation, and itinerant rule

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合罗马法复兴、教会法、封建习惯法与地方城市法。1280年的帝国尚无后世统一的帝国法院体系，司法高度分散，王权裁决更多体现仲裁与象征性最高审判权
legalSystem.en: The legal system blended revived Roman law, canon law, feudal custom, and local urban law. In 1280 the empire did not yet possess the later unified imperial court system; justice was highly fragmented, and royal judgments often functioned as arbitration and symbolic supreme jurisdiction

taxationSystem.zh: 帝国层面的常设直接税很弱，财政主要来自王室直辖地产、通行税、市场税、铸币权收益、封建贡赋与非常征收。各诸侯国和城市则自行征收地租、消费税、关卡税与手工业税
taxationSystem.en: Permanent direct taxation at the imperial level was weak. Finances relied chiefly on royal demesne income, tolls, market dues, mint revenues, feudal aids, and extraordinary levies. Individual principalities and cities collected their own rents, excises, customs, and craft taxes

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教为主，罗马天主教会在政治、教育、司法与社会救济中占核心地位，同时边缘地区存在犹太社群与少量异端思潮
religion.en: Latin Christianity predominated, with the Roman Catholic Church central to politics, education, justice, and charity; Jewish communities and small heterodox currents existed in some regions

philosophy.zh: 经院哲学在修道院学校与新兴大学中占主导，受亚里士多德拉丁译本、奥古斯丁传统与教会法学影响，政治观念强调神授秩序、封建义务与基督教君权
philosophy.en: Scholastic philosophy dominated monastic schools and emerging universities, shaped by Latin Aristotelianism, Augustinian traditions, and canon law; political thought emphasized divinely ordered hierarchy, feudal obligation, and Christian kingship

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；拉丁文用于教会、法律与王室文书，中高地德语、中低地德语、意大利诸方言与捷克语等俗语在地方文书和文学中日益重要
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used throughout; Latin served church, legal, and royal documents, while Middle High German, Middle Low German, Italian vernaculars, Czech, and other local tongues gained importance in local records and literature

culturalAchievements.zh: 哥特式建筑在科隆、斯特拉斯堡等地发展显著；骑士文学、宫廷抒情诗和编年史写作繁荣；城市行会艺术、金工、彩绘玻璃与宗教雕塑高度成熟
culturalAchievements.en: Gothic architecture advanced notably in places such as Cologne and Strasbourg; chivalric literature, courtly lyric poetry, and chronicle writing flourished; urban guild arts, metalwork, stained glass, and religious sculpture were highly developed

languageFamily.zh: 以印欧语系为主，核心包括西日耳曼语支诸德语方言、罗曼语支北意大利与勃艮第地区方言，以及斯拉夫语支的捷克等语言
languageFamily.en: The empire was predominantly Indo-European in language, chiefly West Germanic dialects, Romance dialects in northern Italy and Burgundy, and Slavic languages such as Czech

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济总量庞大，但因政治分裂与统计不可统一而高度分散。莱茵河、多瑙河、北海与波罗的海贸易网络连接城市、修道院地产和诸侯领地，农业仍占绝对主导，城市手工业与区域市场增长明显
gdpDescription.en: The empire possessed a large aggregate economy, though one highly fragmented by political division and the absence of unified accounting. Rhine, Danube, North Sea, and Baltic trade networks linked towns, monastic estates, and princely lands; agriculture remained overwhelmingly dominant, while urban craft production and regional markets expanded markedly

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与畜牧农业、葡萄酒生产、盐业、银铜铅采矿、铁器锻造、纺织、皮革、木材加工、河运与区域贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain and livestock agriculture, wine production, salt extraction, silver-copper-lead mining, ironworking, textiles, leather, timber processing, river transport, and regional commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、盐、羊毛布、亚麻布、金属矿石、铁器、毛皮、木材、蜂蜡、琥珀、香料转口品与奢侈织物
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, salt, woolens, linen, metal ores, iron goods, furs, timber, beeswax, amber, re-exported spices, and luxury textiles

name.en: Pfennig and regional silver coinage system

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地使用权、牲畜、农具、储粮与小额银币。城市商人与高阶教士、贵族积累了远高于平均水平的财富，而农民家庭普遍接近维生线
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth consisted of land-use rights, livestock, tools, stored grain, and small amounts of silver coin. Urban merchants and higher clergy and nobles accumulated wealth far above the average, while peasant households generally lived near subsistence

economicSystem.zh: 以封建地产经济为基础，叠加不断扩张的城市市场、行会制度和跨区域贸易网络。皇权难以统一市场规则，但诸侯与城市通过特许、关卡和铸币权塑造地方经济秩序
economicSystem.en: The economy was grounded in feudal landed production, overlaid by expanding urban markets, guild structures, and long-distance trade networks. Royal power could not standardize market rules, but princes and cities shaped local economic order through charters, tolls, and minting rights

source.zh: 犹太人保护税与特别贡赋

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限且流动性脆弱，部分财富以器物、抵押收入、未兑现债权和领地收益体现，而非集中现金储备。鲁道夫一世正通过收回王室失地与整合奥地利资源改善财政基础
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited and financially fragile; part of royal wealth existed as objects, pledged revenues, uncollected claims, and landed income rather than concentrated cash reserves. Rudolf I was improving the fiscal base by recovering alienated crown lands and consolidating Austrian resources

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向保守与机会主义并存：一方面依赖传统王室权利与巡行征收，另一方面通过没收、赎回、封地重授与婚姻联盟扩大哈布斯堡实际财源
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy combined conservatism with opportunism: it relied on traditional royal rights and itinerant collection, while also expanding Habsburg practical revenues through confiscation, redemption, regranting of fiefs, and marriage alliances

name.zh: 封建骑士与诸侯重骑兵
name.en: Feudal knights and princely heavy cavalry

description.zh: 帝国最具威望的作战力量，由诸侯、伯爵、骑士与其扈从组成，擅长冲击与野战决战，但维持成本高且服役期限有限
description.en: The empire's most prestigious fighting force, composed of princes, counts, knights, and their retinues; effective in shock action and pitched battle, but expensive and limited in term of service

description.zh: 由领主征召农民、城镇民兵和雇佣步兵构成，装备长矛、盾牌、弩和短兵器，是攻城、守城与补充会战兵力的主体
description.en: Raised from peasant levies, town militias, and hired foot soldiers, equipped with spears, shields, crossbows, and sidearms; they formed the main body for siege work, defense, and support in major battles

description.zh: 城市和雇佣兵体系提供大量弩手，尤其适用于城防与攻城；传统弓箭兵在部分地区仍有使用，但地位次于弩兵
description.en: Towns and mercenary systems supplied many crossbowmen, especially valuable in siege and urban defense; traditional bowmen were still used in some regions but ranked below crossbowmen

description.zh: 由国王直辖的骑士随从、城堡守军和受薪士兵组成，是最接近常备军的核心力量，主要用于护卫、镇压叛乱与保障王权存在
description.en: Composed of the king's direct knightly retainers, castle garrisons, and paid soldiers, this was the closest thing to a standing force, used for protection, suppression of revolt, and asserting royal presence

description.zh: 驻守要塞、河关与边区据点，分散于奥地利、波希米亚边缘、阿尔卑斯通道与莱茵沿线，强调防御与交通控制
description.en: Stationed in fortresses, river toll points, and frontier strongholds, especially in Austria, along the Bohemian frontier, Alpine passes, and the Rhine; focused on defense and route control

commanderInChief.zh: 鲁道夫一世，罗马人的国王
commanderInChief.en: Rudolf I, King of the Romans

technology.zh: 军事技术以锁子甲与逐渐增多的板片加固甲、骑枪、长剑、长矛、弩、投石机和攻城器械为主，火药武器尚未形成实战体系。帝国擅长城堡战、围攻与封建骑兵冲击，但统一指挥能力有限
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail with increasing plate reinforcement, lances, swords, spears, crossbows, trebuchets, and siege engines; gunpowder weapons had not yet formed a practical battlefield system. The empire excelled in castle warfare, sieges, and feudal cavalry shock, but suffered from limited unified command

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自内部诸侯竞争、波希米亚王权残余势力、意大利城邦与教皇政治牵制，以及与法国、匈牙利和周边边境领主的潜在冲突。蒙古诸汗国对帝国本体构成的是遥远战略压力而非直接入侵威胁
threats.en: Major threats came from internal princely rivalries, residual Bohemian royal ambitions, the politics of Italian communes and the papacy, and possible conflict with France, Hungary, and neighboring frontier lords. The Mongol khanates posed a distant strategic pressure rather than a direct invasion threat to the imperial core

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 帝国人口约一千二百万，主要分布于莱茵河流域、士瓦本、巴伐利亚、萨克森、奥地利与北意大利帝国领地。人口密度在肥沃河谷、商业走廊与城市周边较高，山区和边缘林地较低
populationDescription.en: The empire's population was about twelve million, concentrated in the Rhineland, Swabia, Bavaria, Saxony, Austria, and the imperial territories of northern Italy. Density was higher in fertile river valleys, commercial corridors, and around cities, and lower in mountains and frontier forests

description.zh: 哈布斯堡帝国首都，欧洲音乐之都
description.en: Habsburg capital, European city of music

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、诸侯、伯爵和高级教士；中层包括骑士、小贵族、富裕商人、行会师傅和城市官员；下层由普通市民、工匠、佃农、自由农和农奴组成。犹太社群多从事金融与贸易中介，但常受特别税和社会风险影响
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, princes, counts, and high clergy; the middle ranks included knights, lesser nobles, wealthy merchants, guild masters, and urban officials; the lower classes were made up of common townspeople, artisans, tenants, free peasants, and serfs. Jewish communities often served as intermediaries in finance and trade but faced special taxes and social danger

allies.zh: 帝国并无稳定统一的盟友体系。鲁道夫一世主要依靠与部分德意志诸侯、教会领主及家族联姻网络合作，并努力争取教廷承认与中立
allies.en: The empire lacked a stable unified alliance system. Rudolf I relied chiefly on cooperation with selected German princes, ecclesiastical lords, and dynastic marriage networks, while seeking papal recognition and neutrality

enemies.zh: 最直接的敌对力量是波希米亚的普热米斯尔遗留势力及反对哈布斯堡扩张的诸侯集团；在西部则需警惕法兰西王权影响，在意大利则面临教皇党与皇帝党城市政治的反复对抗
enemies.en: The most immediate hostile forces were the remnants of Přemyslid power in Bohemia and princely coalitions opposing Habsburg expansion; in the west French royal influence required attention, while in Italy recurrent struggles between Guelf and Ghibelline urban factions complicated imperial claims

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以稳固德意志核心、扩大哈布斯堡在奥地利的实际统治、避免与教廷全面决裂、并在意大利维持象征性宗主权为主。帝国整体并不具备像高度中央集权国家那样持续统一的外交执行力
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on stabilizing the German core, expanding Habsburg effective rule in Austria, avoiding a full rupture with the papacy, and maintaining symbolic overlordship in Italy. The empire as a whole did not possess the continuous unified diplomatic capacity of a highly centralized state

era.zh: 中世纪盛期晚段
era.en: Late High Middle Ages

keyInnovations.zh: 重型犁、三圃制、水磨与风磨的广泛应用提升农业产出；哥特式建筑、城市石造防御工事、弩与配重投石机体现工程与军技进步；会计、契约书写和行会组织能力在商业城市中持续增强
keyInnovations.en: The heavy plough, three-field rotation, and widespread use of watermills and windmills improved agricultural output; Gothic architecture, stone urban fortifications, crossbows, and counterweight trebuchets reflected advances in engineering and warfare; accounting, contractual literacy, and guild organization continued to strengthen in commercial towns

strengths.zh: 人口与土地广阔，农业和矿业基础坚实，莱茵—多瑙—波罗的海贸易网络活跃，城市经济不断成长，教会与贵族秩序提供政治合法性，鲁道夫一世在1270年代后已显著恢复王权威望并奠定哈布斯堡扩张基础
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in broad population and territory, solid agricultural and mining bases, active Rhine-Danube-Baltic trade networks, growing urban economies, and the legitimizing framework of Church and nobility. Since the 1270s Rudolf I had significantly restored royal prestige and laid the foundations for Habsburg expansion

weaknesses.zh: 帝国最大弱点是结构性分裂：中央财政薄弱、常备官僚不足、司法和税制碎片化、军事动员依赖诸侯合作，意大利宗主权多流于名义。任何强势国王去世后，选举政治都可能再次引发不稳定
weaknesses.en: The empire's greatest weakness was structural fragmentation: weak central finance, a limited standing bureaucracy, fragmented law and taxation, military mobilization dependent on princely cooperation, and imperial authority in Italy often more nominal than real. After any strong king's death, electoral politics could easily reopen instability

outlook.zh: 1280年的神圣罗马帝国总体稳定并处于恢复期。若鲁道夫一世继续整合奥地利与王室地产，帝国王权可在德意志地区进一步加强；但从长远看，诸侯自治与选举君主制将持续限制中央集权，使帝国更像多中心政治共同体而非统一国家
outlook.en: In 1280 the Holy Roman Empire was broadly stable and in recovery. If Rudolf I continued consolidating Austria and crown lands, royal power could strengthen further within Germany; yet in the long run princely autonomy and the elective monarchy would continue to limit centralization, leaving the empire more a polycentric political community than a unified state

description.zh: 由众多德意志与意大利诸侯构成的松散帝国，皇权有限但仍具广泛名义权威。
description.en: A loose empire of many German and Italian principalities, with limited imperial power but broad nominal authority.

ideology.zh: 王朝扩张与王室领地整合
ideology.en: Dynastic expansion and consolidation of crown lands

relationship.zh: 哈布斯堡家族通过1273年选举获得王位，鲁道夫一世致力于将奥地利、施蒂里亚等公国纳入家族世袭领地，奠定哈布斯堡数百年霸业的基础。作为帝国名义最高统治者，鲁道夫需在诸侯间维持微妙平衡。
relationship.en: The Habsburgs gained the throne through the 1273 election. Rudolf I worked to consolidate Austria, Styria, and other duchies as hereditary family domains, laying the foundation for centuries of Habsburg dominance. As nominal head of the empire, Rudolf had to maintain a delicate balance among the princes.

description.zh: 鲁道夫一世是哈布斯堡家族第一位成为德意志国王的成员，1273年由选侯推举上位，结束了大空位时代的混乱。他于1278年在马希菲尔德战役中击败波希米亚国王奥托卡二世，夺取奥地利和施蒂里亚公国，将其转化为哈布斯堡家族的世袭领地。鲁道夫虽未能获得教皇加冕为皇帝，但通过务实的领土扩张政策，成功将哈布斯堡从瑞士小贵族提升为帝国最重要的王朝之一。他的统治标志着帝国从大空位时期的无序向有限秩序恢复的转折。
description.en: Rudolf I was the first Habsburg to become German King, elected in 1273 to end the chaos of the Great Interregnum. His decisive victory over Ottokar II of Bohemia at the Battle on the Marchfeld in 1278 secured Austria and Styria as hereditary Habsburg lands. Though he never received papal coronation as Emperor, Rudolf's pragmatic territorial policies transformed the Habsburgs from minor Swiss nobles into one of the empire's most important dynasties. His reign marked the transition from the disorder of the Interregnum toward a limited restoration of imperial order.

role.zh: 哈布斯堡家族首位德意志国王，奠定家族霸业基础
role.en: First Habsburg German King, laid the foundation of the dynasty's power

role.zh: 鲁道夫之子，继承奥地利领地并日后继位为国王
role.en: Rudolf's son, inherited Austrian lands and later became King

ideology.zh: 诸侯自治与选举君主制

relationship.zh: 选侯集团是帝国政治的核心权力掣肘，他们通过选举国王的特权确保王权不会过度集中。选侯们对鲁道夫一世扩大哈布斯堡领地的野心心存警惕，经常利用选举权和帝国议会来限制皇权。
relationship.en: The Prince-Electors were the central check on imperial power, using their exclusive right to elect the king to prevent excessive centralization. They watched Rudolf I's Habsburg territorial ambitions warily and frequently leveraged electoral politics and the Imperial Diet to constrain royal authority.

description.zh: 选帝侯制度是神圣罗马帝国最独特的政治机制之一。七大选侯——三位教会选侯（美因茨、科隆、特里尔大主教）和四位世俗选侯（波希米亚国王、莱茵-普法尔茨伯爵、萨克森公爵、勃兰登堡边疆伯爵）——掌握着选举德意志国王的专属权力。大空位时代（1254-1273）的经验使诸侯们更加坚定地维护自治权，他们有意选择相对弱小的鲁道夫正是为了防止出现另一个强势的施陶芬式皇帝。选侯们各自拥有独立的领地、军队、铸币权和司法权，构成了帝国去中心化治理的基石。
description.en: The electoral college was one of the Holy Roman Empire's most distinctive political institutions. The seven Electors—three ecclesiastical (the Archbishops of Mainz, Cologne, and Trier) and four secular (the King of Bohemia, Count Palatine of the Rhine, Duke of Saxony, and Margrave of Brandenburg)—held the exclusive power to choose the German King. The experience of the Great Interregnum (1254–1273) reinforced the princes' determination to defend their autonomy; they deliberately chose the relatively weak Rudolf precisely to prevent another powerful Hohenstaufen-style emperor. Each Elector maintained independent territories, armies, minting rights, and judicial authority, forming the cornerstone of the empire's decentralized governance.

role.zh: 帝国首席选侯，主持国王选举
role.en: Leading Elector, presided over royal elections

name.zh: 齐格弗里德·冯·韦斯特堡

role.zh: 教会选侯之一，维护莱茵兰教会势力
role.en: One of the ecclesiastical Electors, defended Rhenish Church interests

ideology.zh: 教皇至上权与教会对世俗权力的监督
ideology.en: Papal supremacy and ecclesiastical oversight of secular power

relationship.zh: 教皇派延续了叙任权之争以来教会对帝国皇权的制衡传统。教皇拒绝为鲁道夫一世加冕为皇帝，同时在意大利事务上积极扩张教廷世俗权力，与帝国在意大利北部的名义管辖权形成持续张力。
relationship.en: The Papal party continued the tradition of ecclesiastical checks on imperial power dating back to the Investiture Controversy. The Pope refused to crown Rudolf I as Emperor while actively expanding papal temporal power in Italian affairs, creating ongoing tension with the empire's nominal jurisdiction over northern Italy.

description.zh: 教皇派代表了中世纪欧洲最根本的政治张力之一——教权与皇权的对立。自格里高利七世与亨利四世的叙任权之争以来，教皇始终坚持对皇帝的道德和政治监督权。1280年在位的尼古拉三世出身罗马奥尔西尼家族，是一位精明的政治家，他利用帝国皇权的虚弱来扩大教廷在意大利的世俗领地和政治影响力。教皇派在帝国内部的支持者主要集中在教会诸侯和意大利归尔甫派城市中，他们主张教皇对皇帝拥有最终的精神权威，并反对任何试图恢复霍亨施陶芬时代强势皇权的企图。
description.en: The Papal party represented one of medieval Europe's most fundamental political tensions—the rivalry between papal and imperial authority. Since the Investiture Controversy between Gregory VII and Henry IV, popes consistently asserted moral and political oversight of emperors. Nicholas III, pope in 1280 and a member of the Roman Orsini family, was a shrewd politician who exploited imperial weakness to expand papal temporal domains and political influence in Italy. Papal supporters within the empire were concentrated among ecclesiastical princes and Italian Guelph cities, asserting the pope's ultimate spiritual authority over the emperor and opposing any attempt to restore the strong imperial power of the Hohenstaufen era.

role.zh: 积极扩张教廷世俗权力，抵制皇权干预意大利事务
role.en: Actively expanded papal temporal power, resisted imperial interference in Italian affairs

role.zh: 法国出身的高级教士，代表亲安茹教廷势力
role.en: French-born senior cleric, representing the pro-Angevin papal faction

governmentForm.zh: 封建世袭君主制，在王室主导下结合伯爵领、王家直辖地与教会法权
governmentForm.en: Feudal hereditary monarchy, combining royal authority with earldoms, royal demesne, and ecclesiastical jurisdictions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由国王、盖尔与诺曼化贵族、高级教士、骑士与地主、自由佃农、农民及城镇商人构成；高地与低地在语言、习俗和土地关系上存在明显差异
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the king, Gaelic and Normanized nobles, senior clergy, knights and landholders, free tenants, peasants, and burgh merchants; Highlands and Lowlands showed marked differences in language, custom, and landholding

rulingClass.zh: 王室、主要伯爵家族、诺曼裔与本地贵族、主教与大修道院长
rulingClass.en: The royal house, major earldom families, Norman-descended and native nobles, bishops, and leading abbots

succession.zh: 父系世袭的封建继承制，通常由合法直系后裔继承；在1280年王位预定由国王之外孙女挪威的玛格丽特承继，因其子女已亡
succession.en: Patrilineal hereditary feudal succession, normally passing to the legitimate direct heir; by 1280 the throne was expected to pass to the king’s granddaughter Margaret of Norway, as his children had predeceased him

structure.zh: 王国由国王与御前会议统治，中央机构仍较精简。国王通过王室官员、司法巡视、郡长与王室城堡管理地方，同时依赖伯爵与教会合作维持秩序。议政形式以贵族与教士出席的王室会议为主，尚未形成后世成熟的议会体制
structure.en: The kingdom was governed by the king and royal council, with a still relatively compact central apparatus. The crown ruled through royal officers, judicial circuits, sheriffs, and royal castles, while relying on earls and the church to maintain order. Deliberation centered on royal councils attended by nobles and clergy rather than a fully developed later parliamentary system

function.zh: 起草、誊写并加玺王室敕令、特许状、土地授予与外交文书
function.en: Drafted, engrossed, and sealed royal writs, charters, land grants, and diplomatic documents

function.zh: 核算王室收入、管理地租、郡长缴纳、司法罚金、关税与封建应付款项
function.en: Audited royal income and managed rents, sheriff payments, judicial fines, customs, and feudal dues

function.zh: 监督王国高级司法、巡回审判与重大刑民案件；传统上分北境与洛锡安等司法区域
function.en: Oversaw high justice, judicial circuits, and major criminal and civil cases; traditionally divided into regions such as Scotia and Lothian

function.zh: 负责宫廷供给、王室出行、侍从、礼仪、仓储与日常行政
function.en: Handled court supply, royal travel, attendants, ceremony, stores, and daily administration

function.zh: 通过郡长、城堡总管和地方官执行征收、治安、兵役召集与王令
function.en: Executed collection, policing, military summons, and royal orders through sheriffs, constables, and local officers

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以郡为核心，由郡长代表王权；王家城堡与王室直辖地构成王权支点。边疆与高地地区仍大量依赖地方领主、伯爵与盖尔习惯法权威，城镇则由获特许的王家自治市管理贸易与税收
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on sheriffdoms headed by sheriffs acting for the crown; royal castles and demesnes formed the backbone of royal authority. In frontier and Highland areas the king still relied heavily on local lords, earls, and Gaelic customary authority, while chartered royal burghs managed trade and local taxation

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合封建法、王室敕令、教会法、地方习惯法与部分盖尔布里昂传统。重大案件由王室司法官与郡法院处理，教会事项归教会法院，土地与继承纠纷常牵涉书面特许状和领主法庭
legalSystem.en: The legal system mixed feudal law, royal writs, canon law, local custom, and some surviving Gaelic brehon traditions. Major cases were handled by royal justiciars and sheriff courts, ecclesiastical matters by church courts, and land or succession disputes often involved written charters and seigneurial courts

taxationSystem.zh: 王室收入主要来自王家地产地租、郡长上缴、司法罚金、封建继承税与监护权、港口关税、铸币与偶发性特别征收；常规全国性直接税较有限
taxationSystem.en: Royal revenue came mainly from rents on crown lands, sheriff ferms, judicial fines, feudal reliefs and wardships, port customs, coinage profits, and occasional extraordinary levies; routine kingdom-wide direct taxation remained limited

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教性信仰，主教区、修道院与堂区网络深入社会生活；圣人崇拜、朝圣和教会节期极具影响力
religion.en: Roman Catholic Christianity was the dominant faith, with dioceses, monasteries, and parish networks deeply embedded in social life; saintly cults, pilgrimage, and the liturgical calendar were highly influential

philosophy.zh: 思想以拉丁基督教神学与教会法观念为主，贵族与教士受经院哲学初步影响；政治上强调君权、封建忠诚与基督教秩序
philosophy.en: Thought was shaped chiefly by Latin Christian theology and canon law, with nobles and clergy showing some influence from scholasticism; politically it emphasized kingship, feudal loyalty, and Christian order

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写语言为拉丁文，行政与教会文书亦见法语影响；书写使用拉丁字母。民间口语包括中古盖尔语、早期苏格兰语与诺斯语残余
writingSystem.en: Latin was the principal written language, with administrative and aristocratic usage also showing French influence; writing used the Latin alphabet. Spoken languages included Middle Gaelic, early Scots, and residual Norse in some areas

culturalAchievements.zh: 十二至十三世纪的修道院改革、石砌教堂与修道院建筑、王家自治市发展、编纂特许状与法律文书、吟游诗人和盖尔口述传统的延续，构成当时文化成就的核心
culturalAchievements.en: The core cultural achievements of the twelfth and thirteenth centuries included monastic reform, stone church and abbey building, the growth of royal burghs, the issuing of charters and legal records, and the continued vitality of bardic and Gaelic oral traditions

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系；主要包括凯尔特语族中的盖尔语，以及日益扩展的日耳曼语支早期苏格兰语；拉丁语为教会与行政书面语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European; chiefly Celtic Gaelic alongside an expanding Germanic early Scots, while Latin served as the ecclesiastical and administrative written language

gdpDescription.zh: 苏格兰在1280年的经济规模属西北欧洲中等偏小王国水平，以农业、畜牧、羊毛与地方贸易为基础。低地农业和东岸港口较活跃，高地与群岛则更多依赖牧畜、渔业和地方交换。整体经济稳定，但货币化程度和城市密度低于英格兰北部以南地区
gdpDescription.en: In 1280 Scotland’s economy ranked as that of a small-to-medium northwestern European kingdom, grounded in agriculture, pastoralism, wool, and regional trade. Lowland farming and east-coast ports were relatively active, while the Highlands and Isles relied more on livestock, fishing, and local exchange. The economy was stable overall, though less monetized and less urbanized than areas south of northern England

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧养羊、牛马饲养、羊毛生产、渔业、盐业、小规模采矿、林业、皮革加工、酿造与城镇手工业
mainIndustries.en: Arable farming, sheep raising, cattle and horse husbandry, wool production, fishing, salt making, small-scale mining, forestry, leatherworking, brewing, and urban crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以羊毛、兽皮、皮革、咸鱼、木材、盐和少量牲畜为主；进口葡萄酒、精制布匹、金属制品、奢侈品、香料和教会用品
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on wool, hides, leather, salted fish, timber, salt, and some livestock; imports included wine, fine cloth, metal goods, luxury wares, spices, and ecclesiastical supplies
