householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备与简陋器具上；低地地主和城镇商人拥有较多货币与可转让财产，高地家庭则更重视牛群与亲族网络
householdWealth.en: For ordinary households, wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and simple tools; Lowland landholders and burgh merchants possessed more coin and transferable property, while Highland households valued cattle and kinship networks more heavily

economicSystem.zh: 以封建地产经济为主，辅以王家自治市推动的市场交换和北海贸易；货币经济正在扩展，但实物地租、劳役与地方互惠关系仍占重要位置
economicSystem.en: A feudal land-based economy supplemented by market exchange fostered through royal burghs and North Sea trade; monetization was expanding, but rents in kind, labor obligations, and local reciprocal ties still remained important

source.zh: 王家地产与直辖地收入
封建继承税、监护权与附带权利
source.en: Feudal reliefs, wardships, and incidental rights

category.zh: 司法、道路渡口与杂项公共责任
category.en: Justice, roads, ferries, and miscellaneous public obligations

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库并非全为现银，而是由现款、库存器物、可征收债权与未来收入预期组成。1280年前后财政状况总体稳健，足以维持宫廷、边防与常规外交，但无力长期承担大规模海外战争
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was not held entirely in coin, but consisted of cash, stored valuables, collectible claims, and anticipated income. Around 1280 the fiscal position was generally sound enough for the court, frontier defense, and routine diplomacy, though insufficient for prolonged large-scale overseas war

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守稳健，重点在维护王家地产收益、控制宫廷支出、扩大港口关税与确保郡长按额上缴。王权倾向避免过度非常征税，以维持贵族与教会合作
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was conservative and stability-oriented, emphasizing crown-land revenue, restraint in court expenditure, increased customs at ports, and regular sheriff payments. The monarchy generally avoided excessive extraordinary taxation in order to preserve cooperation from nobles and clergy

description.zh: 由国王直属骑士、贵族扈从和受封骑士组成，用于快速机动、突袭和决战中的冲击
description.en: Composed of royal household knights, noble retinues, and enfeoffed knights, used for mobility, raiding, and shock action in battle

description.zh: 王国主要兵力，由郡县与领主辖地征集的长矛兵、持盾步兵和杂役构成，战斗力随地区与领主组织能力而异
description.en: The kingdom’s principal force, made up of spearmen, shield-bearing infantry, and auxiliaries levied from sheriffdoms and lordships, with quality varying by region and lordly organization

name.en: Castle garrisons and royal household guard

description.zh: 驻守王家要塞、看守边地与保护王室行宫的小规模常备兵力
description.en: Small standing forces holding royal fortresses, watching frontier zones, and protecting the itinerant royal court

name.zh: 西海岸与群岛海上兵力
name.en: Western seaborne forces and island contingents

description.zh: 主要来自西海岸领主与群岛地区的桨帆船与海上战士，用于沿岸巡逻、突袭和运输
description.en: Drawn largely from west-coast lords and island regions, consisting of galleys and maritime fighters for coastal patrol, raiding, and transport

technology.zh: 军事技术以锁子甲、长矛、剑、弓弩、木石城堡与投石攻城器为主。海上使用西部桨帆船，高地与群岛作战强调机动和地域熟悉。尚未形成后世苏格兰长枪阵的成熟体系
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail armor, spears, swords, bows and crossbows, timber-and-stone castles, and traction siege engines. Western galleys served at sea, while Highland and island warfare emphasized mobility and local knowledge. The later mature Scottish schiltron system had not yet fully developed

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与英格兰的边境紧张、挪威在西部海域与群岛的影响余波，以及高地和偏远领地时有不服王命的地方势力。1266年《珀斯条约》后挪威威胁已显著下降，但王国仍需整合西部地区
threats.en: The main threats came from border tensions with England, the lingering effects of Norwegian influence in the western seas and islands, and occasional resistance from local powers in the Highlands and remote lordships. After the Treaty of Perth in 1266, the Norwegian threat had sharply declined, but the kingdom still had to integrate the western regions

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 1280年前后的苏格兰人口大致在五十万左右，分布极不均衡。低地东部和南部农业区人口较密，王家自治市与修道院周边形成小型聚落网络；高地、群岛和北部地广人稀
populationDescription.en: Around 1280 Scotland’s population was roughly half a million, distributed very unevenly. The eastern and southern Lowland farming zones were relatively denser, and small settlement networks clustered around royal burghs and monasteries; the Highlands, Isles, and far north were much more sparsely peopled

socialClasses.zh: 上层为国王、伯爵、男爵和高级教士；中间层包括骑士、地主、修士、教区神职和自治市商人；下层为自由佃农、农民、牧民、渔民与家内仆役。高地氏族纽带较强，低地封建地租与城镇商业更发达
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the king, earls, barons, and senior clergy; the middle strata included knights, landholders, monks, parish clergy, and burgh merchants; the lower strata were free tenants, peasants, herders, fishers, and household servants. Clan ties were stronger in the Highlands, while feudal rents and town commerce were more developed in the Lowlands

allies.zh: 与挪威在1266年后维持和解关系，并通过王室联姻和使节往来与英格兰、法国及教廷保持正式交往；无固定大陆强盟，但总体外交环境尚可
allies.en: After 1266 Scotland maintained a reconciled relationship with Norway, and through dynastic ties and embassies kept formal relations with England, France, and the papacy; it had no fixed great continental alliance, but its general diplomatic environment was acceptable

enemies.zh: 1280年并无公开全面战争中的宿敌，但英格兰是最主要的潜在对手；部分西部与高地地方势力也可能构成内部或边缘性敌对
enemies.en: In 1280 there was no enemy in open full-scale war, but England was the principal potential rival; some western and Highland local powers could also present internal or peripheral hostility

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护独立、避免受英格兰支配、巩固对赫布里底与西部海疆的新控制、并通过婚姻外交与教廷认可提升王室合法性为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving independence, avoiding subordination to England, consolidating newly secured control over the Hebrides and western seaboard, and strengthening royal legitimacy through dynastic diplomacy and papal recognition

era.zh: 中世纪盛期晚段
era.en: Late High Middle Ages

keyInnovations.zh: 石砌城堡与修道院建筑技术成熟，重犁农业、风车与水磨在低地逐步扩展，王室文书与印玺行政更加规范，港口贸易与铸币制度趋于稳定
keyInnovations.en: Stone castle and monastic building techniques were mature, heavy-plough agriculture and mills spread gradually in the Lowlands, royal charter and seal administration became more regularized, and port trade and coinage grew more stable

strengths.zh: 王权在亚历山大三世时期较稳固，国内总体和平，王室财政尚称健康；1266年后西部海疆地位改善，自治市贸易增长，贵族与教会大体合作
strengths.en: Royal authority was relatively solid under Alexander III, the realm was broadly peaceful, and royal finances were reasonably healthy; after 1266 the western seaboard position improved, burgh trade expanded, and nobles and clergy broadly cooperated

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政规模有限，中央机构较薄弱，对高地和偏远地区控制不均；王位继承前景脆弱，缺乏成年男性直系继承人，面对英格兰压力时制度韧性不足
weaknesses.en: Population and fiscal scale were limited, central institutions remained thin, and control over the Highlands and remote regions was uneven; the succession outlook was fragile, lacking an adult male direct heir, and the kingdom’s institutions had limited resilience under strong English pressure

outlook.zh: 若亚历山大三世继续在位，苏格兰短期前景稳定且有望进一步整合西部与发展贸易；但一旦王位继承出现危机，英格兰极可能干预，王国稳定将迅速恶化
outlook.en: If Alexander III remained on the throne, Scotland’s short-term outlook was stable, with prospects for further western integration and commercial growth; yet any succession crisis would invite likely English intervention and could rapidly destabilize the kingdom

description.zh: 独立的苏格兰王国，在13世纪后期维持相对稳固统治。
description.en: An independent Scottish kingdom maintaining comparatively stable rule in the late thirteenth century.

governmentForm.zh: 封建君主制，国王通过直属领地、王室法庭与附庸体系逐步强化中央权力
governmentForm.en: A feudal monarchy in which the king increasingly strengthened central authority through the royal domain, royal courts, and the vassalic system

socialStructure.zh: 社会由祈祷者、战斗者与劳动者三大等级构成，即教士、贵族与平民；其中城市市民与王室官吏的重要性不断上升
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the three orders—those who pray, those who fight, and those who work—namely clergy, nobility, and commoners; within this framework, urban burghers and royal officials were becoming increasingly important

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王族、世俗大贵族、高级教士以及服务于王室的法学官僚共同构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, the royal family, major secular nobles, senior clergy, and legally trained royal administrators

succession.zh: 王位依卡佩王朝父系长子继承传统传承，强调男性直系继承与王朝连续性
succession.en: The throne passed according to the Capetian tradition of patrilineal primogeniture, emphasizing direct male succession and dynastic continuity

structure.zh: 1280年的法兰西王国并无近代意义上的统一部制内阁，而是以王室宫廷、御前会议、巴黎高等法院、财政收支机构与地方总管体系共同运作。国王依靠近臣、法学家、书记员、教士与骑士官员处理司法、财政、外交和军役事务。
structure.en: In 1280 the Kingdom of France did not possess a modern cabinet of ministries, but operated through the royal household, the king's council, the Parlement of Paris, fiscal offices, and a network of local baillis and seneschals. The king relied on close councillors, jurists, clerks, clerics, and knightly officers to manage justice, finance, diplomacy, and military obligations.

function.zh: 为国王提供政策、外交、封臣关系与重大司法事务建议，是王权决策核心
function.en: Advised the king on policy, diplomacy, feudal relations, and major judicial affairs, serving as the core of royal decision-making

function.zh: 负责王室司法、受理上诉、确认法令，并扩展王室法的适用范围
function.en: Handled royal justice, heard appeals, confirmed ordinances, and extended the reach of royal law

name.en: Chamber of Accounts and Royal Treasury

function.zh: 管理王室领地收入、核算赋税、审计官员账目并保管金银与文书
function.en: Managed revenues from the royal domain, calculated taxes, audited officials' accounts, and safeguarded bullion and records

function.zh: 起草、认证并颁发王室敕令、特许状、封地文书及外交书信
function.en: Drafted, authenticated, and issued royal ordinances, charters, feudal instruments, and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 在北方法区由总管、南方法区由司法总管代表国王监督司法、财政、征召与治安
function.en: Represented the king through baillis in the north and seneschals in the south, supervising justice, finance, levies, and order

localAdmin.zh: 王室直属领地由总管与司法总管治理，下辖副手、书记员、法警与收税人员；在封建大领地内，国王则通过宗主权、法庭上诉与盟约干预地方。城市多享有特许权，由市政团体、行会与王室代表共同管理。
localAdmin.en: The royal domain was administered by baillis and seneschals with assistants, clerks, sergeants, and tax collectors beneath them; in great feudal principalities the king intervened through overlordship, appellate jurisdiction, and negotiated obligations. Many towns possessed charters and were governed jointly by urban bodies, guilds, and royal representatives.

legalSystem.zh: 法兰西法律呈多元结构：北方以习惯法为主，南方较多受罗马法影响；教会法适用于婚姻与宗教事务；王室司法正在扩大，并借助巴黎高等法院将“国王之和平”与上诉权推广到更广地区。
legalSystem.en: French law was plural in character: the north was dominated by customary law, while the south was more influenced by Roman law; canon law governed marriage and ecclesiastical matters; royal justice was expanding, and through the Parlement of Paris the king extended the 'king's peace' and appellate jurisdiction over wider regions.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自王室领地地租、关卡与市场税、封建权利、司法罚金、铸币收益以及在特殊情况下征收的非常赋税。1280年时，法国尚未形成后世固定全国性直接税体系，王权财政基础仍以王室领地和间接征收为主。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue derived chiefly from rents of the royal domain, tolls and market dues, feudal rights, judicial fines, seigniorial incidents, and coinage profits, along with extraordinary levies in special circumstances. In 1280 France had not yet developed the later permanent kingdom-wide direct tax system, so royal finance still rested primarily on domainal income and indirect exactions.

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教中的罗马天主教为绝对主导，教会深刻影响政治合法性、教育、慈善、历法、婚姻与日常生活；圣徒崇拜、朝圣与修道院文化极为兴盛。
religion.en: Latin Christianity in the form of Roman Catholicism was overwhelmingly dominant, and the Church deeply shaped political legitimacy, education, charity, the calendar, marriage, and daily life; the cult of saints, pilgrimage, and monastic culture were highly developed.

philosophy.zh: 思想界以经院哲学为核心，巴黎大学是拉丁西方最重要的学术中心之一；亚里士多德哲学、基督教神学与法学研究相互交织，同时骑士伦理与王权神授观念也塑造社会价值。
philosophy.en: Scholasticism stood at the center of intellectual life, and the University of Paris was one of the foremost academic centers of the Latin West; Aristotelian philosophy, Christian theology, and legal studies intersected, while chivalric ethics and ideas of divinely sanctioned kingship also shaped social values.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；拉丁语用于教会、学术与正式文书，古法语广泛用于宫廷文学、司法实务与地方行政交流。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used throughout; Latin served the Church, scholarship, and formal documentation, while Old French was widely employed in courtly literature, practical justice, and local administration

culturalAchievements.zh: 13世纪的法国在哥特式建筑、大学教育、神学论辩、编年史写作、骑士文学与彩饰手抄本方面成就卓著。巴黎圣母院、沙特尔主教座堂、兰斯主教座堂等宏伟建筑体现了王国的宗教与工艺实力，王室圣礼堂传统与圣路易时期留下的文化威望仍深刻影响社会。
culturalAchievements.en: Thirteenth-century France excelled in Gothic architecture, university learning, theological disputation, chronicle writing, chivalric literature, and illuminated manuscripts. Great monuments such as Notre-Dame de Paris, Chartres Cathedral, and Reims Cathedral displayed the kingdom's religious and artisanal power, while the cultural prestige of the Sainte-Chapelle tradition and the era of Saint Louis continued to shape society.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属印欧语系罗曼语族，北部通行各类奥伊语，南部则有奥克语；拉丁语是跨地区书面与教会语言。
languageFamily.en: The principal spoken languages belonged to the Indo-European Romance family, with varieties of the langues d'oïl in the north and Occitan in the south; Latin remained the transregional written and ecclesiastical language.

gdpDescription.zh: 法兰西是西欧人口最多、农业产出最强的王国之一，经济以粮食、葡萄酒、畜牧、手工业与区域贸易为基础。整体财富高度分散于乡村、修道院、贵族庄园与繁荣城市之中，王室可直接动员的资源虽有限于其制度条件，但国家总体经济体量在拉丁西方居前列。
gdpDescription.en: France was one of the most populous and agriculturally productive kingdoms in Western Europe, with an economy based on grain, wine, livestock, handicrafts, and regional trade. Wealth was widely dispersed across villages, monasteries, noble estates, and prosperous towns; although the crown could mobilize only part of this wealth directly, the kingdom's overall economic scale ranked among the largest in the Latin West.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、葡萄种植与酿酒、羊毛与呢绒加工、皮革、盐业、铁器锻造、建筑石材开采、河运与市场贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, viticulture and wine production, wool and cloth processing, leatherworking, salt production, ironworking, quarrying for major construction, river transport, and market trade

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、盐、羊毛、毛织品、皮革、木材、铁器、蜡、染料、奢侈布料、香料转口品与朝圣相关商品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, salt, wool, woolen cloth, leather, timber, iron goods, wax, dyestuffs, luxury textiles, re-exported spices, and pilgrimage-related goods

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户通常拥有有限农具、牲畜与租佃土地使用权，容易受歉收与赋役波动影响；城市工匠与商人财富高于乡村平均水平；大贵族、主教区与修道院掌握大量土地和实物收入。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary peasant households typically possessed modest tools, livestock, and access to leased or customary land, and remained vulnerable to poor harvests and seigniorial burdens; urban artisans and merchants enjoyed higher-than-rural average wealth; great nobles, bishoprics, and monasteries controlled extensive lands and substantial income in kind.

economicSystem.zh: 以封建土地关系为基础的农业经济，辅以增长中的城市商业与区域市场网络。经济活动深受领主权、行会规制、教会节期和地方特许权影响，但巴黎、香槟地区与大西洋—地中海通道显示出日益整合的商业活力。
economicSystem.en: A predominantly agrarian economy rooted in feudal land relations, complemented by expanding urban commerce and regional market networks. Economic life was shaped by lordship, guild regulation, church calendars, and local privileges, yet Paris, the Champagne region, and Atlantic-Mediterranean routes revealed increasingly integrated commercial dynamism.

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库并非完全由现银构成，还包括应收账款、储备贵金属、珠宝器物与可快速变现的资源。法兰西财政较许多邻国稳健，但仍受战争、歉收、铸币变动与封臣忠诚度影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury did not consist solely of coined cash, but also included receivables, bullion reserves, jewels, plate, and resources that could be monetized quickly. French finances were more stable than those of many neighbors, yet remained vulnerable to war, harvest failure, coinage fluctuations, and the loyalty of vassals.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调扩大王室领地收益、加强账目审计、提高司法和行政对地方收入的控制，并在必要时诉诸非常征收。整体目标是在不激化大贵族与教会反弹的前提下稳步增强王权财力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized increasing returns from the royal domain, strengthening audit procedures, and tightening judicial and administrative control over local revenues, while resorting to extraordinary levies when necessary. The broader aim was to enhance royal financial power without provoking excessive resistance from magnates and the Church.

description.zh: 由国王直属骑士、伯爵与诸侯所率封臣骑士构成，是战场突击与贵族威望核心力量
description.en: Comprised the king's own knights and the vassal knights led by counts and princes, forming the core striking arm and symbol of noble prestige

description.zh: 包括长矛兵、盾兵、弩手、地方民兵与领主征召步卒，数量庞大但训练水平不一
description.en: Included spearmen, shield bearers, crossbowmen, local militias, and lordly levied infantry, numerous but uneven in training

description.zh: 负责王室城堡、边地要塞、桥梁和重要城市的守备，常由职业军士与地方驻军组成
description.en: Guarded royal castles, frontier strongholds, bridges, and major towns, often consisting of professional sergeants and local garrisons

description.zh: 在战时增补，尤其适用于围城、守城与远程火力支援，部分来自法国南部、意大利或伊比利亚地区
description.en: Hired in wartime, especially useful in siege warfare, fortress defense, and ranged support, with some drawn from southern France, Italy, or Iberia

description.zh: 围绕国王与王室核心机构服务的精锐骑士、军士与武装侍从，是较具常备性质的部队
description.en: Elite knights, sergeants, and armed retainers serving around the king and the core royal institutions, forming the most standing-like forces

technology.zh: 军事技术以锁子甲与逐步增多的板甲部件、长枪冲锋骑兵、弩、投石机、攻城塔与石砲为主。法军擅长封建集结和围城作战，但其指挥体系仍受贵族分权与服役期限限制。
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail armor with increasing plate reinforcement, lance-armed shock cavalry, crossbows, trebuchets, siege towers, and stone-throwing engines. French forces were strong in feudal mobilization and siege warfare, though their command structure remained constrained by noble particularism and limited service terms.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与英格兰王室在阿基坦等封地问题上的长期竞争、与佛兰德及南部边区相关的政治摩擦，以及低地与帝国边界事务。来自蒙古帝国的威胁对法国属间接性质，更多体现为外交与宗教想象，而非法国本土即时军事压力。
threats.en: The principal threats came from long-term rivalry with the English crown over fiefs such as Aquitaine, political friction involving Flanders and southern frontier regions, and issues along the borderlands with the Empire and Low Countries. Any threat from the Mongol world was indirect for France, appearing more in diplomacy and religious imagination than as an immediate military danger to the kingdom itself.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

description.zh: 蒙古骑兵标志武器，射程远精度高
description.en: Signature Mongol cavalry weapon with great range and accuracy

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 约1500万人口使法兰西成为13世纪末欧洲人口最庞大的政治体之一。人口主要分布于北法谷物区、塞纳与卢瓦尔流域、诺曼底、香槟、朗格多克和阿基坦等农业较发达地区。
populationDescription.en: With about 15 million inhabitants, France was one of the most populous polities in late thirteenth-century Europe. Population was concentrated in the grain-growing regions of northern France, the Seine and Loire basins, Normandy, Champagne, Languedoc, and Aquitaine.

description.zh: 法国首都，欧洲启蒙运动中心
description.en: French capital, center of the European Enlightenment

socialClasses.zh: 人口主体为农民，包括自由农、佃农、农奴残余与村社成员；上层包括世俗贵族与教士；城市中则有商人、行会工匠、学者、学生、书记员与贫民。犹太社群虽规模不大，但在部分城镇商业与放贷活动中具有可见存在，且易受王权政策变化影响。
socialClasses.en: The majority of the population consisted of peasants, including free tenants, customary cultivators, surviving serfs, and village community members; the upper strata included secular nobles and clergy; in towns lived merchants, guild artisans, scholars, students, clerks, and the urban poor. Jewish communities, though not large, were visibly present in commerce and moneylending in some towns and remained vulnerable to shifts in royal policy.

allies.zh: 法兰西没有固定意义上的现代同盟体系，但与教廷维持重要关系，并通过王室联姻、封建协定和临时条约争取低地诸侯、苏格兰及若干伊比利亚和意大利势力的合作。
allies.en: France did not maintain a fixed alliance system in the modern sense, but it cultivated important relations with the papacy and sought cooperation from Low Countries princes, Scotland, and various Iberian and Italian powers through dynastic marriages, feudal agreements, and temporary treaties.

enemies.zh: 最重要的对手是英格兰王国，双方因法国境内英王封地、海峡贸易与王权威望长期竞争；同时，部分强大封臣与边境诸侯也会在特定时期成为内部或半外部挑战者。
enemies.en: Its most important rival was the Kingdom of England, with which it competed over English-held fiefs in France, Channel trade, and royal prestige; at the same time, some powerful vassals and border princes could become internal or semi-external challengers in particular periods.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以巩固王权、削弱过强封臣、限制英王在法领地影响、维护与教廷的可操作关系，以及通过婚姻与法律手段扩张王室权利为核心。法国并积极参与十字军世界的象征政治，尽管在1280年其重心更偏向欧洲本土事务。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on consolidating royal authority, curbing overly powerful vassals, limiting the influence of the English king within French lands, preserving a workable relationship with the papacy, and expanding royal rights through marriage and legal mechanisms. France also remained deeply engaged in the symbolic politics of the crusading world, though by 1280 its practical focus lay more on European affairs.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期晚段
era.en: Late High Middle Ages

keyInnovations.zh: 重犁、三圃制、水磨与风磨的广泛使用支撑农业扩张；哥特式建筑中的尖拱、飞扶壁与肋拱顶代表工程高峰；大学制度、经院方法、系统化法学训练与不断成熟的书吏文书文化提升了国家治理能力。
keyInnovations.en: The widespread use of the heavy plough, three-field rotation, watermills, and windmills underpinned agricultural expansion; pointed arches, flying buttresses, and rib vaulting in Gothic architecture represented an engineering high point; the university system, scholastic method, systematic legal training, and increasingly sophisticated documentary culture enhanced the kingdom's governing capacity.

strengths.zh: 法兰西王国拥有庞大人口、肥沃耕地、发达教会网络、日益成熟的王室司法和行政机构，以及欧洲首屈一指的文化中心巴黎。卡佩王朝连续统治带来较强合法性，王权较前代显著扩张。
strengths.en: The Kingdom of France possessed a large population, fertile farmland, an extensive ecclesiastical network, increasingly mature royal judicial and administrative institutions, and Paris as one of Europe's foremost cultural centers. Continuous Capetian rule provided strong legitimacy, and royal authority had expanded markedly compared with earlier generations.

weaknesses.zh: 国家仍深受封建分权制约，国王对全境资源的直接控制有限；南北法律与语言差异明显；财政体系尚不完全统一；军队依赖封臣服役与临时征召，持续作战能力受限。若遭遇长期对英战争或内部叛乱，财政与政治凝聚力可能承压。
weaknesses.en: The realm remained constrained by feudal decentralization, and the king's direct control over the resources of the whole kingdom was limited; northern and southern legal and linguistic differences were pronounced; the fiscal system was not yet fully unified; and the army depended heavily on vassal service and temporary levies, limiting sustained campaigning capacity. In the event of prolonged war with England or internal rebellion, fiscal and political cohesion could come under strain.

outlook.zh: 1280年的法兰西总体前景强劲。若王室能够继续通过司法、婚姻、购买、继承与行政整合扩大直属权力，法国有望在未来数代内成为拉丁西方最强大的君主国之一。然而，王权与封臣、英法关系、教廷政治及财政承载能力仍将决定其扩张速度与稳定程度。
outlook.en: In 1280 France's overall outlook was strong. If the crown could continue enlarging its direct power through justice, marriage, purchase, inheritance, and administrative integration, France was well positioned to become one of the most powerful monarchies in the Latin West over the coming generations. Yet relations between crown and vassals, Anglo-French rivalry, papal politics, and fiscal capacity would still determine the pace and durability of that rise.

description.zh: 中世纪西欧最强大王国之一，王权持续扩张并主导法兰西腹地。
description.en: One of the strongest kingdoms in medieval Western Europe, with royal power expanding across the French heartland.

governmentForm.zh: 封建君主制下不断强化的王权行政国家，国王通过御前会议、财政署、司法巡回与郡县官体系统治全国。
governmentForm.en: A feudal monarchy with an increasingly centralized royal administrative state, governed through the king's council, the Exchequer, itinerant justice, and the county sheriff system.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以封建等级为骨架，由国王、伯爵与男爵、骑士、教士、自由佃农、农奴和城镇市民组成；教会与世俗贵族共同构成上层权力结构。
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around feudal hierarchy, consisting of the king, earls and barons, knights, clergy, free tenants, serfs, and urban burgesses; ecclesiastical and secular elites jointly formed the upper power structure.

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王室近臣、大贵族、高级教士、郡长官与王室法官构成统治阶层。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, royal household magnates, major barons, senior clergy, sheriffs, and royal justices.

succession.zh: 王位通常依长子继承制传承，但仍需贵族与教会承认，王室婚姻与领地安排对继承稳定极为重要。
succession.en: The crown generally passed by primogeniture, though recognition by the nobility and Church remained important, and royal marriages and land settlements were crucial to succession stability.

structure.zh: 1280年的英格兰王国由国王直接统治，中央机构包括御前会议、财政署、王座法庭、民事诉讼法院和普通诉讼法院。地方上依郡、百户区和自治城市管理，王室通过郡长官、巡回法官、验尸官与各类委任官员伸张权力。
structure.en: In 1280 the Kingdom of England was ruled directly by the king, with central institutions including the King's Council, Exchequer, King's Bench, Court of Common Pleas, and Chancery. Locally it was administered through shires, hundreds, and chartered towns, with royal authority exercised by sheriffs, itinerant justices, coroners, and various commissioned officers.

function.zh: 负责最高层政策、军政决策、封臣事务、外交与王室行政协调。
function.en: Handled high policy, military and political decisions, feudal affairs, diplomacy, and coordination of royal administration.

function.zh: 征收和审核王室收入，管理郡长官账目、地租、罚金、关税与封建义务。
function.en: Collected and audited royal revenue, managing sheriff accounts, rents, fines, customs, and feudal dues.

function.zh: 起草和封缄王室敕令、特许状、司法文书与行政命令，维持全国书面治理。
function.en: Drafted and sealed royal writs, charters, judicial instruments, and administrative orders, sustaining written governance across the realm.

function.zh: 包括王座法庭、普通诉讼法院和巡回审判体系，处理刑事、民事、土地与王权相关案件。
function.en: Included King's Bench, Common Pleas, and the eyre system, dealing with criminal, civil, land, and crown-related cases.

function.zh: 管理宫廷开支、军需采购、随驾行政和部分机动财政职能。
function.en: Managed court expenditure, military procurement, itinerant administration, and some mobile fiscal functions.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以郡为核心，由郡长官代表国王执行财政、司法和治安职能；百户区负责基层法庭与征发；自治城市和教会领地享有不同程度特许权。爱德华一世时期，王权对地方司法与特许权的审查更趋严格。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on the shire, where sheriffs represented the king in fiscal, judicial, and policing duties; hundreds handled lower courts and levies; chartered towns and ecclesiastical liberties enjoyed varying degrees of privilege. Under Edward I, royal scrutiny of local justice and franchises became more rigorous.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以普通法为核心，建立在王室令状、判例实践、巡回审判和陪审制度基础上。1280年前后，王权通过成文法令和司法程序改革不断限制私人司法，强化对土地、继承、契约和治安案件的控制。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on common law, built on royal writs, precedent-based practice, itinerant justice, and jury procedures. Around 1280, the crown increasingly curbed private jurisdictions through statutes and procedural reform, strengthening control over land, inheritance, contract, and public order cases.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自王室领地收益、封建税与继承金、司法罚金、城市与犹太社群课税、羊毛和皮革关税，以及在特殊情况下由全国同意征收的动产税。税制兼具封建性与货币化特征。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from crown lands, feudal incidents and reliefs, judicial fines, taxation of towns and Jewish communities, customs on wool and hides, and, in special cases, nationally granted taxes on movables. The tax system combined feudal obligations with increasing monetization.

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教为主，隶属罗马天主教体系。教会深刻影响教育、法律、慈善、节庆与政治合法性；修道院、主教座堂和堂区网络遍布全国。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Latin Christianity within the Roman Catholic system. The Church deeply shaped education, law, charity, festivals, and political legitimacy; monasteries, cathedrals, and parish networks covered the realm.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以经院哲学、基督教神学和王权法理为主，同时受罗马法、教会法与亚里士多德思想经由大学传播的影响。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life was dominated by scholastic philosophy, Christian theology, and theories of royal authority, while also being influenced by Roman law, canon law, and Aristotelian thought transmitted through universities.

writingSystem.zh: 书写主要采用拉丁字母。行政、教会和学术文书多用拉丁文，法律与贵族社会大量使用盎格鲁-诺曼法语，民间口语则以中世纪英语为主。
writingSystem.en: Writing used the Latin alphabet. Latin predominated in administration, Church, and scholarship; Anglo-Norman French was heavily used in law and aristocratic society; vernacular speech was chiefly Middle English.

culturalAchievements.zh: 英格兰在哥特式建筑、法律文书化、编年史写作、大学教育和教会艺术方面成就显著。威斯敏斯特、林肯、索尔兹伯里等大教堂工程展现高超建筑技术，王室法庭与文书实践也构成中世纪欧洲制度文化的重要样本。
culturalAchievements.en: England showed notable achievements in Gothic architecture, documentary legal culture, chronicle writing, university education, and ecclesiastical art. Cathedral building at Westminster, Lincoln, and Salisbury displayed advanced construction skill, while royal courts and documentary practice formed an important model of medieval European institutional culture.

languageFamily.zh: 主要口语属于日耳曼语族西日耳曼支的中世纪英语；上层社会与法律领域广泛使用罗曼语族的盎格鲁-诺曼法语；拉丁语为跨地区书面与宗教语言。
languageFamily.en: The main vernacular belonged to the West Germanic branch as Middle English; the elite and legal sphere widely used Anglo-Norman French of the Romance family; Latin served as the supraregional written and religious language.

gdpDescription.zh: 1280年前后的英格兰经济以农业为基础，同时拥有欧洲较高程度的市场整合、货币使用和羊毛出口能力。王国财富主要来自粮食生产、畜牧、羊毛贸易、地租、城市手工业与区域集市网络。若以近似货币化价值估算，英格兰在西欧属于中等偏强经济体，但仍远不能与高度都市化的意大利地区相比。
gdpDescription.en: England's economy around 1280 was fundamentally agrarian, yet it possessed comparatively strong market integration, monetization, and wool export capacity by European standards. Wealth derived mainly from grain production, pastoralism, wool trade, rents, urban crafts, and regional market networks. In approximate monetized value, England was a moderately strong West European economy, though still far less urbanized than the Italian regions.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、牧羊业、羊毛生产与出口、皮革加工、酿酒、磨坊业、金属加工、建筑、船运和地方手工业。
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, sheep raising, wool production and export, leather processing, brewing, milling, metalworking, construction, shipping, and local crafts.

tradeGoods.zh: 主要出口原羊毛、羊皮、铅、锡、皮革和部分谷物；主要进口葡萄酒、盐、香料、染料、奢侈纺织品、金属制品和地中海商品。
tradeGoods.en: Major exports included raw wool, woolfells, lead, tin, leather, and some grain; major imports included wine, salt, spices, dyestuffs, luxury textiles, metalware, and Mediterranean goods.

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富体现在土地占有、牲畜、农具、谷物储备和村社权利上。贵族与修道院掌握大片地产与磨坊、渔场、林地等附属收益；城镇商人和手工业者则逐步积累货币资产与库存商品。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was embodied in landholding, livestock, tools, grain stocks, and customary village rights. Nobles and monasteries controlled extensive estates with mills, fisheries, and woodland revenues, while townsmen and craftsmen increasingly accumulated cash assets and inventories.

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园经济和封建地产关系为基础，同时深度嵌入货币租佃、区域市场、城市特许与跨海贸易网络的混合经济体。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy grounded in manorial production and feudal landed relations, yet deeply integrated into money rents, regional markets, urban privileges, and overseas trade networks.

source.zh: 封建税、继承金与监护婚姻收益
source.en: Feudal dues, reliefs, wardships, and marriage revenues

source.zh: 关税与羊毛皮革出口征收
source.en: Customs and duties on wool and leather exports

source.zh: 城市、犹太社群及特别摊派
铸币、林地、港口与杂项收入
source.en: Mint, forest, port, and miscellaneous revenues

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室库藏并非全部以现银静置，而是分散体现为财政署账面余额、银币储备、待征收入、贵重器物与可调用信用。英格兰财政在和平年份通常能够维持运转，但大规模战争会迅速压缩储备。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was not entirely a static hoard of coin, but was distributed across Exchequer balances, silver reserves, receivables, valuables, and callable credit. In peaceful years England's finances were generally functional, but major wars could quickly strain reserves.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 爱德华一世延续并强化以账目审核、书面令状和法定征收为基础的财政治理，强调提高常规收入并在必要时通过议税与非常税筹措战争资金。
fiscalPolicy.en: Edward I continued and strengthened fiscal governance based on audit, written writs, and legally grounded levies, seeking to improve ordinary revenues while resorting to granted taxation and extraordinary impositions when war financing was needed.

description.zh: 由伯爵、男爵和骑士按封建义务或受薪召集组成，是野战中的冲击核心，但人数有限且服役期通常较短。
description.en: Raised by earls, barons, and knights through feudal obligation or pay, they formed the shock core of field armies, though their numbers were limited and service terms often short.

description.zh: 主要由自由民、乡村征召者和城镇民兵组成，装备长矛、盾牌、刀剑、短弓或长弓，在守城、行军护卫和大规模战役中数量最为重要。
description.en: Composed mainly of freemen, rural levies, and urban militia, armed with spears, shields, swords, short bows, or longbows; they were numerically most important in garrison duty, marching escorts, and major campaigns.

description.zh: 包括英格兰与威尔士地区征募的弓手。1280年时长弓尚未完全制度化为后来那种决定性兵种，但熟练射手的重要性已日益上升。
description.en: Included archers recruited from England and Wales. In 1280 the longbow was not yet institutionalized as the later decisive arm, but the importance of skilled missile troops was clearly rising.

description.zh: 由守堡士兵、雇佣兵、水手和港口征发船员构成，用于守卫边境要塞、海峡航道和运输任务；英格兰尚无常设海军，战时依赖王室船只与商船征用。
description.en: Consisted of garrison troops, hired men, sailors, and requisitioned port crews, used for frontier fortresses, Channel routes, and transport duties; England had no permanent navy and relied in war on royal vessels and impressed merchant shipping.

technology.zh: 军事技术以锁子甲、头盔、盾牌、长枪、弩、弓、攻城塔、投石机和石砌城堡体系为核心。英格兰在筑城、后勤征发和司法化征兵方面较成熟，但尚处火药兵器传入欧洲之前。
technology.en: Military technology centered on mail armor, helmets, shields, lances, crossbows, bows, siege towers, trebuchets, and a stone-castle system. England was relatively mature in fortification, logistics, and legally organized levies, but predated the spread of gunpowder weaponry in Europe.

threats.zh: 主要安全压力来自不列颠群岛内部与周边：威尔士诸侯势力、与苏格兰关系恶化的潜在风险、海峡对岸法国王权竞争，以及爱尔兰边区治理困难。1280年尚无来自蒙古诸汗国的直接军事威胁。
threats.en: The principal security pressures came from within and around the British Isles: Welsh princely resistance, the latent risk of worsening relations with Scotland, competition with Capetian France across the Channel, and difficulties in governing the Irish marches. In 1280 there was no direct military threat from the Mongol khanates.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 早期火铳与火箭（中国/伊斯兰世界传入）
description.en: Early firearms and rockets (introduced from China/Islamic world)

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 1280年的英格兰人口处于中世纪高位，尚未经历14世纪黑死病造成的剧烈崩塌。人口增长推动垦殖、村庄扩展与市场繁荣，但也使边际土地开发加深、社会脆弱性上升。
populationDescription.en: England's population in 1280 stood near a medieval high point, before the demographic collapse of the fourteenth-century Black Death. Population growth stimulated assarting, village expansion, and market activity, but also pushed cultivation onto marginal lands and increased social vulnerability.

description.zh: 大英帝国首都，全球贸易与金融中心
description.en: Capital of the British Empire, global trade and financial center

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与高等贵族、地方领主与骑士、高级与下级教士、城镇商人和行会手工业者、自由佃农、农奴及无地劳作者。不同地区的自由程度差异明显，东部和东南部货币租佃倾向更强。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the crown and high nobility, local lords and knights, higher and lower clergy, urban merchants and guild craftsmen, free tenants, serfs, and landless laborers. Degrees of freedom varied regionally, with stronger tendencies toward money rents in the east and southeast.

allies.zh: 英格兰在1280年没有绝对稳固的长期同盟体系，但与部分低地国家商人网络、教廷以及若干大陆诸侯保持合作关系；在不列颠内部则寻求通过宗主权、婚姻和封臣关系扩大影响。
allies.en: England in 1280 lacked a rigid long-term alliance system, but maintained cooperative relations with merchant networks in the Low Countries, the papacy, and various continental princes; within the British Isles it sought influence through overlordship, marriage, and vassal ties.

enemies.zh: 主要竞争与敌对对象是威尔士本土诸侯，尤其是格温内斯政权；法国王国是长期战略竞争者；与苏格兰虽未全面开战，但潜在矛盾持续存在。
enemies.en: The main rivals and enemies were the native Welsh princes, especially the regime of Gwynedd; the Kingdom of France was a long-term strategic competitor; and relations with Scotland, though not yet openly at war, carried persistent tensions.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护海峡两岸王室权益、强化对威尔士和爱尔兰的支配、限制苏格兰独立行动空间，并在欧洲事务中通过王室婚姻、教廷交涉与商贸联系维护地位为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on defending royal interests on both sides of the Channel, strengthening dominance in Wales and Ireland, limiting Scotland's room for independent action, and maintaining standing in European affairs through dynastic marriage, papal negotiation, and commerce.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期欧洲封建—商业扩张时代
era.en: High Medieval European feudal-commercial expansion era

keyInnovations.zh: 重型犁、三圃制、水磨与风磨、石拱与哥特式建筑技术、普及的书面行政、王室令状司法、硬币货币化和改良海运组织是这一时期英格兰的重要技术与制度创新。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional innovations in England included the heavy plough, three-field rotation, watermills and windmills, stone vaulting and Gothic building techniques, widespread written administration, writ-based royal justice, coined monetization, and improved maritime organization.

strengths.zh: 英格兰的优势在于相对强大的王权财政与司法体系、成熟的郡县行政、富有竞争力的羊毛出口、人口规模可观以及伦敦主导的商业网络。爱德华一世个人统治能力强，能够将封建义务与书面行政结合起来。
strengths.en: England's strengths lay in its comparatively strong royal fiscal and judicial system, mature shire administration, competitive wool exports, substantial population, and a London-centered commercial network. Edward I was a capable ruler able to combine feudal obligation with documentary administration.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于财政常因战争而紧张，常备军规模有限，对贵族合作和临时征召依赖很大；地区差异明显，威尔士、爱尔兰边区和未来的苏格兰问题都可能拖累国家资源。经济仍高度依赖农业收成，易受歉收冲击。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included finances that could become strained by warfare, a limited standing army, and heavy reliance on noble cooperation and temporary levies; regional disparities were significant, and Wales, the Irish marches, and the future Scottish question could all drain resources. The economy still depended heavily on agricultural harvests and was vulnerable to bad years.

outlook.zh: 1280年的英格兰总体稳定且上升，预计将继续推进王权集中化并更积极干预不列颠诸地。但若对威尔士和苏格兰发动长期战争，财政、地方忠诚和社会承受力都将受到严峻考验。
outlook.en: In 1280 England was broadly stable and ascending, likely to continue centralizing royal authority and intervening more forcefully across the British Isles. Yet prolonged wars in Wales and Scotland would place severe strain on finances, local loyalty, and social endurance.

description.zh: 在爱德华一世统治下强化行政并积极干预不列颠诸地。
description.en: Under Edward I, England strengthened royal administration and intervened actively across the British Isles.

ruler.zh: 马格努斯三世·拉杜洛斯

governmentForm.zh: 世袭封建君主制，王权正在加强，并通过法令与王室官员削弱地方豪强和贵族私权
governmentForm.en: Hereditary feudal monarchy with strengthening royal authority, using legislation and royal officials to curb local magnates and private noble power

socialStructure.zh: 社会以自由农民、地方贵族、教士和少量城镇居民为主；与欧洲许多地区相比，自由持地农民比例较高，芬兰地区则处于更松散的整合和殖民扩张过程中
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on free peasants, local nobility, clergy, and a small urban population; compared with much of Europe, Sweden had a relatively large class of freeholding peasants, while Finland was under looser integration and ongoing expansion

rulingClass.zh: 国王、比耶博家族宗支、高级贵族、主教与大教堂教士团共同构成统治精英
rulingClass.en: The king, branches of the House of Bjelbo, high nobility, bishops, and cathedral chapters formed the ruling elite

succession.zh: 父系世袭为主，但仍受贵族与教会承认及地方选举传统影响；王位巩固依赖王族联盟与贵族支持
succession.en: Primarily patrilineal hereditary succession, though still shaped by noble and ecclesiastical recognition and older elective traditions; consolidation depended on dynastic alliances and aristocratic support

structure.zh: 1280年的瑞典正从分散的地方王权向较成体系的王国治理过渡。国王依靠王室法庭、巡回官员、领地管理者与教会合作进行统治，全国尚无后世意义上的完备中央官僚机构，但已形成围绕王室、御前会议和教省网络的治理骨架
structure.en: In 1280 Sweden was transitioning from fragmented regional kingship toward more coherent royal government. The king ruled through the royal court, itinerant officials, estate administrators, and cooperation with the Church. A fully developed central bureaucracy did not yet exist, but a governing framework had emerged around the crown, royal council, and ecclesiastical network

function.zh: 处理王国重大决策、贵族协商、封赏、对外事务与高等司法
function.en: Handled major royal decisions, consultation with nobles, grants, foreign affairs, and high justice

function.zh: 管理王室领地、实物赋税、租税、王室仓储与征收收入
function.en: Managed royal lands, in-kind taxation, rents, royal storage, and collection of revenues

function.zh: 通过法律宣示、地方官和集会法庭维持治安，执行王命并处理诉讼与罚金
function.en: Maintained order through legal proclamations, local officers, and assembly courts, enforcing royal commands and handling suits and fines

function.zh: 起草诏令、保管文书、与主教区和修道机构协调，并借助教士识字能力支撑行政
function.en: Drafted charters, preserved records, coordinated with dioceses and monasteries, and relied on clerical literacy to support administration

function.zh: 协调沿海地区船只征发、军事召集、渡海后勤与边境防务
function.en: Coordinated coastal ship levies, military musters, cross-water logistics, and frontier defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以传统的乡区、百户区和省域法律共同体为基础，由地方执法官、王室代理人、贵族领主与教会共享治理。国王通过拉格曼、治安官及王室城堡与庄园逐步扩大直接控制，尤其在梅拉伦湖地区和通往芬兰的东部方向
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on traditional districts, hundreds, and provincial law communities, with governance shared among lawmen, royal agents, noble lords, and the Church. The king expanded direct control through lawmen, bailiff-like officers, and royal castles and estates, especially around Lake Mälaren and eastward toward Finland

legalSystem.zh: 法律以各省习惯法为基础，由地方集会和拉格曼主持审理；王权正推动更统一的王国法秩序。1280年前后，王室通过《阿尔斯诺法规》确认骑士与侍从的特权，也体现王权与贵族之间的制度化协商
legalSystem.en: Law was based on provincial customary laws, administered through local assemblies under lawmen; the crown was gradually promoting a more unified legal order. Around 1280, the crown’s Ordinance of Alsnö confirmed privileges of knights and mounted retainers, reflecting the institutionalization of bargaining between monarchy and nobility

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以王室领地收入、实物贡赋、罚金、通行与市场收入、教会协助征收的捐输及战争时期临时征发为主。货币税仍有限，许多义务以粮食、牲畜、劳役、船只和兵役形式履行
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on revenues from royal estates, in-kind dues, judicial fines, toll and market income, levies collected with ecclesiastical assistance, and extraordinary wartime exactions. Monetized taxation remained limited, and many obligations were rendered in grain, livestock, labor, ships, and military service

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，乌普萨拉总主教区是宗教与政治核心。基督教化已基本完成，但乡村民间习俗和较早的北欧传统仍在节庆、习惯和地方观念中保留痕迹
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the established religion, with the archbishopric of Uppsala serving as a religious and political center. Christianization was largely complete, though rural customs and older Norse traditions still survived in festivals, habits, and local beliefs

philosophy.zh: 思想上以拉丁基督教世界观为主，强调王权的神授正当性、教会法秩序、伦理教化与封建忠诚。神学、教会法和经院思维通过主教与修道院传入，但尚未形成高度城市化的学术中心
philosophy.en: Thought was dominated by the Latin Christian worldview, emphasizing divinely sanctioned kingship, canon-law order, moral instruction, and feudal loyalty. Theology, canon law, and scholastic currents entered through bishops and monasteries, though Sweden had not yet developed highly urbanized academic centers

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写语言为拉丁文，文书、教会记录与王室特许状多用拉丁字母书写；古瑞典语开始在法律和地方实践中逐渐显现书面用途
writingSystem.en: The principal written language was Latin, used in charters, church records, and royal documents in the Latin alphabet; Old Swedish was gradually beginning to appear in legal and local practical contexts

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就主要体现在教堂建筑、石砌教区组织、法典编纂传统、王室特许状制度和地区性圣徒崇拜。哥特兰和梅拉伦流域的教堂壁画、石制洗礼盆和宗教金工艺展现了北欧与德意志、波罗的海世界的文化交流
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements were most visible in church building, the parish stone-church network, traditions of law codification, royal charter practice, and regional saint cults. Church murals, stone baptismal fonts, and religious metalwork in Gotland and the Mälaren basin reflected Nordic interaction with German and Baltic cultural worlds

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支；口语为古瑞典语及芬兰地区的芬兰语、萨米语等地方语言，官方文书以拉丁文为主
languageFamily.en: North Germanic; spoken languages included Old Swedish and, in Finland, Finnish and Sámi and other local tongues, while official documentation was primarily in Latin

gdpDescription.zh: 瑞典经济在1280年属于中等偏低规模的北欧农业经济，但因自由农民结构、铁产出、毛皮贸易、木材与波罗的海海运而具有韧性。与法兰西或英格兰等西欧大国相比总量有限，但在北方区域已属较稳健的王国经济
gdpDescription.en: Sweden’s economy in 1280 was a modest-sized Nordic agrarian economy, but one with resilience due to its free peasant structure, iron output, fur trade, timber, and Baltic shipping. Its total scale was limited compared with major western kingdoms such as France or England, yet within the north it was a relatively sound kingdom economy

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧、渔业、铁冶炼、焦油与木材生产、毛皮采集与转运、船只制造、地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, fishing, iron smelting, tar and timber production, fur collection and transit, shipbuilding, and local crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口铁、毛皮、黄油、兽皮、木材、焦油、鱼类和猎隼等北方特产；进口盐、葡萄酒、精制布匹、金属制成品、磨石、香料与部分谷物
tradeGoods.en: Exports included iron, furs, butter, hides, timber, tar, fish, and northern luxury items such as gyrfalcons; imports included salt, wine, fine cloth, metal manufactures, millstones, spices, and some grain

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富主要以土地使用权、牲畜、谷仓储备、渔具、船只和银饰体现。自由农民家庭通常拥有比欧洲农奴制地区更高的自主性，但总体生活水平仍受气候、收成和地方交通条件限制
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, fishing gear, boats, and silver ornaments. Free peasant households often had greater autonomy than those in more serf-dominated parts of Europe, though overall living standards remained constrained by climate, harvests, and transport conditions

economicSystem.zh: 以农村自给和地方交换为基础的封建—农民经济，并与波罗的海商业网络相连接。货币化程度有限，但港口、集市和教会网络逐步推动商业扩展
economicSystem.en: A feudal-peasant economy based on rural self-provisioning and local exchange, connected to wider Baltic commercial networks. Monetization remained limited, but ports, markets, and church networks were gradually expanding trade

source.zh: 罚金、司法收入与法庭款项
source.en: Fines, judicial income, and court payments
Provincial dues and commuted in-kind renders

source.zh: 特别征发与封臣义务折现
source.en: Extraordinary levies and commuted feudal obligations

category.zh: 宫廷、王室家产与巡行统治
category.en: Court, royal household, and itinerant kingship

category.zh: 军事动员、海防与城堡维护
category.en: Military mobilization, naval defense, and castle upkeep

category.zh: 教会关系、捐赠与宗教建筑支持
category.en: Church relations, donations, and support for religious building

category.zh: 储备、赈济与不可预见支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库规模不大但足以应对常规巡行、短期动员和政治赏赐。财富多以银、器皿、库存货物、王室地产收益权与可征调实物形式保存，而非纯粹铸币现金
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was modest but sufficient for regular itineration, short-term mobilization, and political patronage. Wealth was held in silver, plate, stored goods, rights to estate income, and callable in-kind resources rather than in coined cash alone

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向谨慎保守，依赖稳定的领地收入、司法罚金和有限扩大的贸易收费；同时通过向骑士阶层授予免税和特权来换取军事服务与政治支持，体现王权巩固下的交换性财政安排
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively cautious, relying on stable estate revenues, judicial fines, and a limited expansion of trade dues; at the same time, tax exemptions and privileges were granted to the knightly class in return for military service and political support, reflecting a bargaining fiscal order under a strengthening monarchy

description.zh: 由国王家臣、受封骑士与贵族侍从组成，是机动性和政治可靠性较高的核心兵力；1280年前后王权正通过骑士特权制度加强这一层的军事作用
description.en: Composed of royal retainers, enfeoffed knights, and noble mounted followers, this was the core force with relatively high mobility and political reliability; around 1280 the crown was strengthening this layer through knightly privilege

description.zh: 来自各地自由农民和地方社群，装备以长矛、斧、盾、弓为主，是王国防御和区域动员的主体
description.en: Raised from free peasants and local communities, equipped mainly with spears, axes, shields, and bows; they formed the bulk of the kingdom’s defensive and regional mobilization forces

name.zh: 海上征发船队与沿海兵员
name.en: Naval Levy Fleet and Coastal Troops

description.zh: 依托沿海区与湖区的船只义务编成，用于运兵、巡逻、护航和对芬兰方向的行动，战时尤为重要
description.en: Organized through ship obligations from coastal and lake districts, used for transport, patrol, convoying, and operations toward Finland; especially important in wartime

description.zh: 驻守王室要地、仓储和交通节点，承担守备、押运和维持王权象征性存在的职责
description.en: Stationed at royal strongpoints, stores, and communications nodes, responsible for defense, escort, and maintaining the symbolic presence of royal authority

commanderInChief.zh: 国王马格努斯三世·拉杜洛斯

technology.zh: 军事技术属13世纪北欧水平。主要使用锁子甲、头盔、木盾、长矛、战斧、剑和弓；攻城技术较为基础，依赖木制工事、简易投射器和封锁。海军以桨帆并用的北方船只和地方运输船为主
technology.en: Military technology was typical of the 13th-century north. Forces used mail armor, helmets, wooden shields, spears, battle-axes, swords, and bows; siegecraft was basic, relying on timber works, simple engines, and blockade. Naval forces centered on northern oared-and-sailed vessels and local transport craft

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与丹麦、挪威的王朝和边界竞争，波罗的海海上安全，以及对芬兰和东部边疆控制力不足所引发的冲突；内部则需防范贵族结盟与地方离心
threats.en: Principal threats came from dynastic and border rivalry with Denmark and Norway, insecurity in the Baltic, and conflict arising from incomplete control over Finland and the eastern frontier; internally, the crown also had to guard against noble coalitions and local centrifugal power

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口约50万，分布稀疏，主要集中于梅拉伦湖区、东约特兰、西约特兰、哥特兰及若干沿海谷地。北部和内陆地区人口稀薄，芬兰的王国控制区域仍属低密度边疆
populationDescription.en: The population was about 500,000 and sparsely distributed, concentrated mainly in the Mälaren region, Östergötland, Västergötland, Gotland, and certain coastal valleys. Northern and inland areas were thinly settled, and the kingdom’s Finnish zones remained low-density frontier regions

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括国王与王族、世俗贵族、骑士与侍从、主教和教士、自由农民、佃农与仆役、城镇商人和手工业者，以及芬兰和北方边疆的部落与地方社群。自由农民是王国的重要税役和军事基础
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the king and royal kin, secular nobility, knights and retainers, bishops and clergy, free peasants, tenants and servants, town merchants and artisans, and frontier tribal and local communities in Finland and the north. Free peasants were a major basis of taxation and military obligation

allies.zh: 无固定近代意义上的正式盟友体系，但与教廷、北德商人与若干波罗的海基督教势力维持合作；王朝婚姻与贵族网络是外交的重要工具
allies.en: There was no fixed alliance system in the modern sense, but Sweden maintained cooperation with the papacy, north German merchants, and various Christian powers of the Baltic; dynastic marriage and noble networks were important diplomatic tools

enemies.zh: 不存在长期全面战争中的单一死敌，但与丹麦、挪威在王位、边界、贸易和地区影响力上持续竞争；东部边疆还面临与诺夫哥罗德势力圈的潜在摩擦
enemies.en: There was no single perpetual enemy in all-out war, but Sweden competed continuously with Denmark and Norway over thrones, borders, trade, and regional influence; on the eastern frontier there was also latent friction with the Novgorodian sphere

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以巩固北欧地位、维持与教廷和德意志商业世界的联系、限制丹麦优势，并逐步向芬兰和波罗的海东岸扩展影响为核心。王权的对外行动通常与内部整合密切相连
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on consolidating Sweden’s position in the Nordic world, maintaining ties with the papacy and the German commercial sphere, checking Danish advantage, and gradually extending influence into Finland and the eastern Baltic. External action was closely tied to internal consolidation of royal rule

era.zh: 中世纪盛期晚段的北欧技术阶段
era.en: Late High Medieval Nordic technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 重型木结构建筑和石砌教堂扩展，铁冶炼持续发展，海船建造和沿海航行经验成熟，水磨与简单机械在部分地区应用增加，书面特许状和教会记录制度更加常态化
keyInnovations.en: Expansion of heavy timber construction and stone churches, continued development of iron smelting, mature shipbuilding and coastal navigation, wider use of watermills and simple mechanical devices in some areas, and more routine use of written charters and ecclesiastical record-keeping

strengths.zh: 王国在1280年最大的优势是政局相对稳定、王权正稳步加强、自由农民基础较广、教会组织完善、波罗的海贸易联系增长，并且对芬兰的拓展为长期战略提供空间
strengths.en: The kingdom’s chief strengths in 1280 were relative political stability, steadily strengthening royal authority, a broad base of free peasants, a well-developed church organization, growing Baltic trade ties, and room for long-term strategic expansion into Finland

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于人口少且分散、财政和行政能力有限、城市化水平低、常备军规模小、交通受季节与地理限制明显，并且仍需与地方贵族和区域法律传统反复协商
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in sparse population, limited fiscal and administrative capacity, low urbanization, a small standing army, communications heavily constrained by season and geography, and the continued need to bargain with local nobles and provincial legal traditions

outlook.zh: 短期前景总体稳定并偏向上升。若马格努斯三世能够继续通过法令、封赏和教会合作巩固中央权威，瑞典将在13世纪末至14世纪初进一步形成更强的王国结构，并在芬兰和波罗的海事务中扩大影响；但一旦王位继承或贵族关系失衡，整合进程仍可能放缓
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was broadly stable and mildly favorable. If Magnus III could continue consolidating central authority through legislation, patronage, and cooperation with the Church, Sweden would develop a stronger kingdom structure by the late 13th and early 14th centuries and expand its influence in Finland and Baltic affairs; however, any disruption in succession or noble relations could still slow the process of integration

description.zh: 正在巩固王权并向芬兰扩展影响的北欧王国。
description.en: A Nordic kingdom consolidating royal authority and extending influence into Finland.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭君主制，辅以王室议事会、地方法律集会与教会合作治理
governmentForm.en: Hereditary monarchy supported by a royal council, regional law assemblies, and cooperative governance with the Church

socialStructure.zh: 社会以自由农民为主体，其上为国王、贵族与主教等教俗精英，其下有佃农、仆役与少量奴役残余；海外属地由地方首领、法官与教士共同维系
socialStructure.en: Society was dominated by freeholding peasants, above whom stood the king, nobles, and bishops as secular and ecclesiastical elites, while tenants, servants, and residual unfree dependents occupied lower ranks; overseas dependencies were held together by local chieftains, lawmen, and clergy

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王室家臣、主教团、高级神职人员以及地方贵族与大地主
rulingClass.en: The king, royal retainers, bishops, senior clergy, and regional nobles and major landholders

succession.zh: 父系世袭君主制，王位通常由合法王子继承，但仍需贵族、主教与地方承认以确保稳定
succession.en: Patrilineal hereditary monarchy, with the throne normally passing to a legitimate royal son, though recognition by nobles, bishops, and regional assemblies remained important for stability

structure.zh: 1280年的挪威王国正处于王权整合与制度化时期。国王在卑尔根统治本土挪威，并通过总督、法官、主教和地方领袖管理冰岛、法罗群岛、设得兰、奥克尼和格陵兰等北大西洋属地。中央治理依赖王室议事会、宫廷官员、王室文书系统与教会合作，而地方层面则继续保留各地的辛格集会和旧法传统。
structure.en: In 1280 the Kingdom of Norway was in a phase of royal consolidation and institutionalization. The king ruled mainland Norway from Bergen and governed Iceland, the Faroe Islands, Shetland, Orkney, and Greenland through governors, lawmen, bishops, and local leaders. Central rule relied on the royal council, court officers, the royal chancery, and cooperation with the Church, while local administration still preserved regional things and older legal traditions.

function.zh: 协助国王处理立法、司法、外交、继承与重大财政事务
function.en: Assisted the king in legislation, justice, diplomacy, succession, and major fiscal matters

function.zh: 起草敕令、保管诏书、处理王室通信并记录土地、税务与特许权
function.en: Drafted royal decrees, preserved charters, managed royal correspondence, and recorded lands, taxes, and privileges

function.zh: 管理王室地产、地租、罚金、税收与港口收入
function.en: Managed royal estates, rents, fines, taxes, and port revenues

function.zh: 监督法律集会、任命或认可法律宣讲人和地方法官，执行王法
function.en: Oversaw law assemblies, appointed or recognized lawmen and local judges, and enforced royal law

function.zh: 协调冰岛、法罗群岛、奥克尼、设得兰和格陵兰的贡赋、司法和王室代表
function.en: Coordinated tribute, justice, and royal representation in Iceland, the Faroes, Orkney, Shetland, and Greenland

name.en: Royal Military and Naval Defense Office

function.zh: 组织徭役舰队、沿海防务、征召与王室侍从武装
function.en: Organized the levy fleet, coastal defense, mobilization, and the king's armed retainers

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以法律区、郡与港口为基础，由王室任命的总督、领主代理人、法官和税收征收人执行。传统的地方辛格集会仍在司法和调解中发挥作用，教区神职人员也参与基层秩序维持。冰岛则在归附挪威后保持较强的地方习惯法和酋长—法律人体系，但王权影响已日益加强。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was based on law provinces, districts, and ports, carried out by royal governors, estate agents, lawmen, and tax collectors. Traditional local things still played important roles in justice and arbitration, while parish clergy also supported local order. Iceland, after submitting to Norway, retained strong customary law and a chieftain-lawman framework, though royal influence was steadily increasing.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系建立在成文王法、地区习惯法和教会法之上。马格努斯六世此前推行的全国法典改革深刻影响1280年的政治秩序，使挪威逐步从分散的地方习惯法走向统一王国法。重大案件可上诉至王室或高级法庭，教会婚姻、遗嘱和部分道德案件则受教会法约束。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on written royal law, regional custom, and canon law. The kingdom-wide legal reforms introduced under Magnus VI strongly shaped political order in 1280, gradually moving Norway from fragmented local custom toward a more unified law of the realm. Major cases could be appealed to royal or higher courts, while marriage, wills, and some moral matters fell under canon law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自王室地产租赋、罚金、港口关税、贸易征收费、属地贡赋以及特定时期的非常征收。常规直接税水平较低，国家收入更多依赖王室土地、司法罚没和海上贸易节点控制。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on rents from royal estates, fines, harbor customs, trade dues, tribute from dependencies, and extraordinary levies in special circumstances. Routine direct taxation was comparatively limited, and royal income depended more heavily on crown lands, judicial forfeitures, and control of maritime trade points.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，主教区、修道院与堂区网络已深度融入王国治理；同时，古诺斯传统记忆仍存于传说、节俗和文学之中
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the state religion, with bishoprics, monasteries, and parish networks deeply integrated into royal governance; at the same time, memories of Old Norse tradition survived in legend, seasonal custom, and literature

philosophy.zh: 思想上以拉丁基督教神学、王权合法性观念与北欧法律伦理相结合；宫廷与教会精英也吸收欧洲经院思想，但地方社会仍以实用法理、荣誉观念和习惯法传统为主
philosophy.en: Intellectually, the kingdom combined Latin Christian theology, ideas of royal legitimacy, and Norse legal ethics; court and church elites also absorbed elements of European scholastic thought, though local society remained grounded in practical law, honor codes, and customary tradition

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母书写古诺斯语与拉丁语；符文字母在正式行政中已边缘化，但仍具有纪念与地方用途
writingSystem.en: The kingdom chiefly used the Latin alphabet to write Old Norse and Latin; runic writing had become marginal in formal administration but still retained commemorative and local uses

culturalAchievements.zh: 挪威在13世纪后期仍是古诺斯书面文化的重要中心之一，王室与教会支持编年史、王家传记、圣徒传和法律文本的整理。海上航行传统、木结构建筑、教堂艺术、金属工艺以及关于北大西洋和王权历史的叙事文学，构成其主要文化成就。
culturalAchievements.en: In the late thirteenth century Norway remained one of the important centers of written Old Norse culture, with crown and church supporting the compilation of chronicles, royal sagas, saints' lives, and legal texts. Maritime tradition, timber architecture, church art, metalwork, and narrative literature about the North Atlantic and royal history formed its main cultural achievements.

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支，主要语言为古西诺斯语的挪威变体，行政和教会广泛使用拉丁语
languageFamily.en: North Germanic; the main spoken language was a Norwegian form of Old West Norse, while Latin was widely used in administration and the Church

gdpDescription.zh: 挪威经济规模在欧洲属中小王国水平，但凭借渔业、海运、北大西洋航线与王室对战略港口的控制，具备高于其人口规模的外向型贸易能力。山地和寒冷气候限制了粮食产出，迫使其在某些年份依赖谷物进口，尤其来自英格兰、丹麦和德意志港口。
gdpDescription.en: Norway's economy ranked as that of a small-to-medium European kingdom, but through fisheries, shipping, North Atlantic routes, and royal control of strategic ports, it possessed an outward-facing commercial capacity larger than its population might suggest. Mountainous terrain and a cold climate limited grain production, forcing dependence in some years on imported grain, especially from England, Denmark, and German ports.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业与鱼干生产、畜牧业、沿海航运、造船、木材、毛皮、少量铁器与皂石开采、王室地产经营以及北大西洋贸易中转
mainIndustries.en: Fishing and stockfish production, pastoral farming, coastal shipping, shipbuilding, timber, furs, limited ironworking and soapstone quarrying, management of royal estates, and North Atlantic trade transit

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以鱼干、毛皮、牛皮、鲸产物、木材、绳索材料和少量金属制品为主；进口则包括谷物、麦芽酒、葡萄酒、盐、织物、奢侈金属器、教会用品与部分武器
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on stockfish, furs, hides, whale products, timber, rope materials, and limited metal goods; imports included grain, ale, wine, salt, cloth, luxury metalware, church goods, and some weapons

name.zh: 挪威银币与计值马克体系
name.en: Norwegian silver coin and mark accounting system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富差异很大。沿海自由农户通常拥有船只份额、牲畜、渔具和少量银器，属于王国较稳健的生产阶层；内陆贫农和佃户则更易受歉收影响。海外属地的财富更多体现在船、羊群、牛群和贸易关系中，而非大量铸币积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Coastal freeholding farmers often possessed shares in boats, livestock, fishing gear, and small amounts of silverware, forming one of the kingdom's more resilient productive strata; inland poor peasants and tenants were more vulnerable to bad harvests. In the overseas dependencies, wealth was more often embodied in ships, flocks, cattle, and trading ties than in large coin holdings.

economicSystem.zh: 以农村自给经济为基础、辅以王室地产收入、港口贸易和海上交换网络的混合经济。市场化程度在卑尔根等港口较高，而大部分乡村仍以实物生产、租赋、劳役和季节性交换为主。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on rural subsistence, supplemented by crown-estate income, port trade, and maritime exchange networks. Commercialization was relatively high in ports such as Bergen, while most rural areas still relied on in-kind production, rents, labor obligations, and seasonal exchange.

source.zh: 关税、港口税与贸易征费
source.en: Customs, harbor dues, and trade levies

source.zh: 海外属地贡赋与特许收入
source.en: Tribute and privileges from overseas dependencies

source.zh: 铸币、杂项税费与临时征收
source.en: Minting, miscellaneous dues, and extraordinary levies

category.en: Fleet, coastal defense, and military obligations

category.zh: 教会合作、捐赠与宗教建筑支持
category.en: Church cooperation, grants, and support for religious buildings

category.zh: 储备、赈济与不可预见支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财库并非完全以现银保存，而是由卑尔根等地的银币、贵金属器、租赋应收、王室地产收益权和可调动的海上征发能力共同构成。对一个人口有限的北方王国而言，其财政状况在1280年前后相对稳健。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was not held entirely as coin, but consisted of silver in Bergen and other centers, precious objects, receivables from rents, income rights from crown estates, and mobilizable maritime obligations. For a sparsely populated northern kingdom, its fiscal position around 1280 was relatively sound.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，强调维护王室地产收益、控制港口税源、避免过度压榨自由农民，并在需要时通过临时征收和属地贡赋补足支出。稳定优先于激进扩张。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, emphasizing preservation of crown-estate income, control of customs revenues, and avoidance of excessive burdens on free peasants, while using extraordinary levies and tribute from dependencies when necessary. Stability took priority over aggressive expansion.

description.zh: 由国王的侍从、职业武装随员和部分骑乘战士组成，是王权最可靠的核心兵力
description.en: Composed of the king's retainers, professional armed attendants, and some mounted warriors, forming the most reliable core of royal force

description.zh: 依托传统海上征役体系，从沿海各区征集船只与水手，适于巡航、运输、海上袭击和防御
description.en: Raised through the traditional maritime levy system, drawing ships and crews from coastal districts for patrol, transport, raiding, and defense

description.zh: 由自由农民和地方武装组成，装备水平不一，主要承担守备、集结和短期战役任务
description.en: Made up of free peasants and local armed men with uneven equipment, mainly suited for garrison duty, mustering, and short campaigns

description.zh: 部署于奥克尼、设得兰、冰岛等地的小规模守备力量，维持治安、港口控制和王室权威
description.en: Small garrison forces in Orkney, Shetland, Iceland, and related dependencies, maintaining order, port control, and royal authority

technology.zh: 军备以长船、运输船、锁子甲、长矛、斧、剑、弓与木盾为主，攻城能力有限。海军组织和北海航行经验是其优势，重骑兵和大型攻城器械则逊于欧洲大陆强国。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on longships, transports, mail armor, spears, axes, swords, bows, and wooden shields, with limited siege capability. Naval organization and North Sea sailing experience were key strengths, while heavy cavalry and large siege engines lagged behind major continental powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与苏格兰关于赫布里底群岛及西方海域影响力的长期后果、与丹麦及瑞典的北欧竞争、海盗与海上治安问题，以及远距离统治北大西洋属地的后勤困难。1280年王国总体稳定，但外海控制始终需要舰队维持。
threats.en: The main threats came from the long aftermath of conflict with Scotland over the Hebrides and western sea influence, Nordic rivalry with Denmark and Sweden, piracy and maritime insecurity, and the logistical difficulty of ruling North Atlantic dependencies at great distance. In 1280 the kingdom was broadly stable, but control of outer waters always required an active fleet.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口约35万，集中分布于西部和东部峡湾、沿海农渔结合地区以及特隆赫姆和维肯等较适宜耕作的区域。人口密度低，聚落分散，北大西洋属地人口规模小但具有重要的战略和贸易价值。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 350,000, concentrated in the western and eastern fjord districts, mixed farming-fishing coastal zones, and more arable areas such as Trøndelag and Viken. Population density was low and settlements dispersed, while the North Atlantic dependencies were sparsely populated but strategically and commercially important.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与大贵族、主教和教士、地方地主与武装自由民、普通自由农民、佃户、渔民、手工业者、商人以及少量仆役和边缘依附人口。与欧洲许多地区相比，自由农民比例较高，是挪威社会的重要特征。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included the royal house and great nobles, bishops and clergy, local landholders and armed free men, ordinary free peasants, tenants, fishermen, artisans, merchants, and a small number of servants and marginal dependents. Compared with many parts of Europe, the relatively high proportion of free peasants was a major characteristic of Norwegian society.

allies.zh: 无固定意义上的强力军事同盟，但与教廷、若干英格兰商人网络以及北海诸港维持实用合作；与奥克尼、法罗和冰岛等属地保持宗主—附属关系
allies.en: There were no strong permanent military allies in the modern sense, but Norway maintained practical cooperation with the papacy, various English merchant networks, and North Sea ports; it also held suzerain-dependent relations with Orkney, the Faroes, and Iceland

enemies.zh: 无全面大战中的宿敌，但与苏格兰存在近年战争后的紧张余波，并持续警惕丹麦和瑞典在斯堪的纳维亚的竞争影响
enemies.en: There was no enemy in full-scale war at the time, but tensions lingered with Scotland after recent warfare, and Norway remained wary of Danish and Swedish competition in Scandinavia

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交重点在于维持北大西洋海上网络、保障属地忠诚、守护与不列颠群岛和北海港口的贸易联系，并通过教会与王室婚姻政治巩固合法性。挪威并不追求欧陆霸权，而是致力于海域控制、王权稳定和商路安全。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving the North Atlantic maritime network, ensuring the loyalty of dependencies, protecting trade links with the British Isles and North Sea ports, and reinforcing legitimacy through church diplomacy and dynastic marriage politics. Norway did not seek continental hegemony, but rather maritime control, royal stability, and secure trade routes.

era.zh: 中世纪盛期晚段的北欧海洋王国
era.en: A Scandinavian maritime kingdom of the late High Middle Ages

keyInnovations.zh: 成熟的北海与北大西洋航海技术、长船与货船建造传统、王国法典编纂、木教堂与石质主教座堂建筑、寒冷海域渔业加工和长距离海运组织能力
keyInnovations.en: Mature North Sea and North Atlantic navigation, traditions of longship and cargo-vessel construction, kingdom-wide legal codification, wooden stave churches and stone cathedral building, and strong capabilities in cold-water fish processing and long-distance maritime organization

strengths.zh: 挪威的优势在于成熟的海上传统、对北大西洋属地的历史联系、较高比例的自由农民、稳健而不断制度化的王权，以及卑尔根作为贸易节点的战略价值。其法律统一和行政整合在13世纪后期达到较高水平。
strengths.en: Norway's strengths lay in its mature maritime tradition, historic links to the North Atlantic dependencies, relatively numerous free peasants, a stable and increasingly institutionalized monarchy, and the strategic value of Bergen as a trade hub. Its legal unification and administrative consolidation had reached a comparatively high level in the late thirteenth century.

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括人口稀少、农业基础脆弱、财政规模有限、长距离属地统治成本高，以及在重骑兵、攻城战和欧陆兵制方面不及南方大国。恶劣天气、歉收和海运中断都可能迅速削弱国家承受力。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included a sparse population, a fragile agricultural base, limited fiscal scale, the high cost of ruling distant dependencies, and inferiority to southern powers in heavy cavalry, siege warfare, and continental military systems. Harsh weather, poor harvests, and disruptions to seaborne transport could quickly reduce state resilience.

outlook.zh: 1280年的挪威总体稳定，仍是北大西洋世界的重要王国。若能维持王权与教会平衡、保障对卑尔根和海外属地的控制，并避免卷入消耗性的斯堪的纳维亚大战，其国势可继续稳固；但从长期看，人口与资源约束将限制其在欧洲权力格局中的上升空间。
outlook.en: In 1280 Norway was broadly stable and remained an important kingdom of the North Atlantic world. If it could preserve the balance between crown and church, maintain control over Bergen and the overseas dependencies, and avoid being drawn into exhausting Scandinavian wars, its position could remain secure; in the longer run, however, demographic and resource constraints would limit its rise within the wider European balance of power.

description.zh: 北大西洋取向的斯堪的纳维亚王国，仍控制冰岛等海外属地。
description.en: A North Atlantic-oriented Scandinavian kingdom still controlling Iceland and other overseas dependencies.

governmentForm.zh: 世袭封建君主制，国王与高级贵族、主教团及地方廷会共享部分实际权力
governmentForm.en: Hereditary feudal monarchy in which the king shared practical power with leading nobles, bishops, and provincial assemblies

socialStructure.zh: 社会以王室、世俗贵族、教士、自由农民、佃农与城镇居民为主要层级；丹麦农村自由民传统仍较强，但大地主与教会庄园权力正在扩张
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the royal house, secular nobility, clergy, free peasants, tenants, and townspeople; Denmark still retained a comparatively strong tradition of free rural farmers, though large landlords and church estates were expanding their power

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王国大贵族、主教与重要王室官员构成统治阶层
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of the king, major nobles of the realm, bishops, and principal royal officials

succession.zh: 名义上为王朝世袭，但需经丹麦各地廷会与权贵承认，带有选举确认色彩
succession.en: Nominally dynastic and hereditary, but requiring recognition by regional assemblies and magnates, giving succession an elective-confirmatory character

structure.zh: 1280年的丹麦尚无高度官僚化中央机构，政务围绕国王宫廷展开。王室依靠总管、财政官、书记官、地方总督、城堡长官以及主教网络治理国家；全国重要决策常需与王国贵族和教会领袖协商，尤其在《手书》颁布后王权受到更明确限制
structure.en: In 1280 Denmark did not yet possess a highly bureaucratized central administration; government revolved around the royal court. The crown ruled through stewards, financial officers, clerks, local governors, castle commanders, and the episcopal network; major decisions often required consultation with the realm's nobles and church leaders, especially after the issuance of the charter that more clearly constrained royal authority

function.zh: 负责国王日常统治、诏令发布、接见、巡行安排及王室财产管理
function.en: Handled the king’s daily governance, issuance of orders, audiences, itineraries, and management of royal property

function.zh: 征收王领地租税、王室赋役、关税、罚金与市场收入，保管钱币和贵金属
function.en: Collected revenues from crown lands, dues, tolls, fines, and market income, and kept coin and precious metal reserves

function.zh: 起草宪章、封地文书、司法命令和外交函件，以拉丁文为主要书写语言
function.en: Drafted charters, enfeoffment documents, judicial orders, and diplomatic correspondence, chiefly in Latin

function.zh: 由各地区王室官员、城堡长官与郡级执法者执行治安、征税、征召与司法
function.en: Through regional royal officers, castle commanders, and district enforcers it administered order, taxation, levy obligations, and justice

function.zh: 协调主教区、什一税争议、教会法事务及与教廷的关系
function.en: Coordinated dioceses, tithe disputes, canon-law matters, and relations with the papacy

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以西兰、菲英、日德兰、斯科讷等地区为基础，由王室任命的地方官、城堡守将和传统地方廷会共同运作。乡村事务仍深受地方习惯法与自由农民社群影响，城市则由享有特许权的市镇议事群体管理部分税务和商业秩序
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on regions such as Zealand, Funen, Jutland, and Scania, operating through royal appointees, castle commanders, and traditional provincial assemblies. Rural affairs remained strongly shaped by customary law and communities of free peasants, while chartered towns managed parts of taxation and commercial order through urban governing groups

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以地方习惯法为核心，如《日德兰法》与其他区域法传统，并辅以王室敕令与教会法。司法在廷会、地方法庭、王室法庭与教会法庭之间分层进行。1282年前后，贵族迫使国王接受对任意逮捕与课税的限制，显示法权正从纯粹王命走向契约化
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on provincial customary law, including the Jutland Law and other regional traditions, supplemented by royal ordinances and canon law. Justice was dispensed through assemblies, local courts, royal courts, and ecclesiastical courts. Around 1282, the nobles compelled the king to accept limits on arbitrary arrest and taxation, showing authority shifting from pure royal command toward a more contractual order

taxationSystem.zh: 税制以王领地收入、地租、桥渡与海峡关税、市场税、铸币收益、司法罚金及非常时期征敛为主。常规直接税能力有限，王权高度依赖王室地产和通行费；在战争或危机中则向贵族、教会和农民要求额外援助，但常引发抵制
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on revenues from crown lands, rents, bridge and strait tolls, market dues, seigniorage, judicial fines, and extraordinary levies. Regular direct-tax capacity was limited, and the monarchy depended heavily on royal estates and transit tolls; in war or crisis it sought additional aid from nobles, clergy, and peasants, often provoking resistance

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教与社会核心制度，主教区、修道院、堂区网络和圣人崇拜深刻影响政治、教育与日常生活
religion.en: Roman Catholic Christianity was the state religion and central social institution, with dioceses, monasteries, parish networks, and saintly devotion deeply shaping politics, education, and daily life

philosophy.zh: 思想上以中世纪拉丁基督教世界观为主，强调王权受神意约束、教会法秩序、道德统治与封建忠诚；受经院哲学影响的神学论辩主要见于主教座堂学校与修道院
philosophy.en: Thought was dominated by the medieval Latin Christian worldview, emphasizing kingship under divine constraint, canon-law order, moral rule, and feudal loyalty; theological debate influenced by scholasticism appeared mainly in cathedral schools and monasteries

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系为拉丁字母；拉丁文用于官方、教会与法律文书，古丹麦语在口头和部分地方文本中使用，旧式卢恩文字已趋边缘化但仍具象征意义
writingSystem.en: The primary writing system was the Latin alphabet; Latin was used for official, ecclesiastical, and legal documents, while Old Danish was spoken and appeared in some local texts, and older runic writing had become marginal though still symbolically important

culturalAchievements.zh: 丹麦在13世纪已形成较成熟的教会建筑传统，罗曼式与早期哥特式石造教堂广布全国；王国法律编纂、修道院抄写活动、圣史与王室谱系书写、宗教艺术与海洋文化共同构成其文化成就。海峡与波罗的海贸易也促进了城镇文化与外来工艺传播
culturalAchievements.en: By the 13th century Denmark had developed a mature ecclesiastical building tradition, with Romanesque and early Gothic stone churches spread widely across the realm; legal codification, monastic manuscript culture, sacred history and royal genealogical writing, religious art, and maritime culture formed key cultural achievements. Trade across the straits and the Baltic also fostered urban culture and the spread of foreign crafts

languageFamily.zh: 北日耳曼语支，使用古丹麦语；学术与教会领域广泛使用拉丁文
languageFamily.en: North Germanic language family, with Old Danish in common use; Latin was widely used in scholarly and ecclesiastical spheres

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、渔业和区域海贸为基础，整体在人口规模不大的欧洲王国中较为稳健。丹麦的优势在于控制连接北海与波罗的海的海峡、拥有较强的海运能力以及斯科讷渔业，但受限于耕地条件、政治冲突和有限的城市化水平，整体财富规模仍属中等
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on agriculture, livestock, fishing, and regional maritime trade, and was relatively solid for a European kingdom of modest population. Denmark’s strengths lay in controlling the straits connecting the North Sea and Baltic, possessing capable seafaring networks, and benefiting from Scania’s fisheries; yet arable limits, political conflict, and low urbanization kept overall wealth at a middling level

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、畜牧业、鲱鱼渔业、盐渍与干制鱼品、航运、造船、木材加工、毛皮转运、地方手工业和市场贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, livestock raising, herring fisheries, salted and dried fish production, shipping, shipbuilding, timber working, fur transit trade, local handicrafts, and market exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、牛马、黄油、奶酪、皮革、皮毛、鲱鱼、木材、焦油、蜂蜡，以及输入的盐、葡萄酒、精制布匹、金属器和奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Grain, cattle and horses, butter, cheese, leather, furs, herring, timber, tar, beeswax, and imported salt, wine, fine cloth, metal goods, and luxuries

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户的财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、船只份额和家用品上；贵族与教会则拥有大片庄园、磨坊、渔场与司法收益。城乡财富差距明显，但丹麦自由农传统使部分农民家庭较欧洲其他地区更具独立性
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was held mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, boat shares, and domestic goods; nobles and the church possessed large estates, mills, fisheries, and judicial incomes. Wealth gaps between town and countryside and between elites and commoners were marked, but Denmark’s tradition of free peasants gave some rural families greater independence than in parts of Europe

economicSystem.zh: 封建—市场混合经济。农村生产由自由农、佃农、庄园与教会地产主导，城镇和港口通过特许权与关税体系运作，长途贸易逐渐纳入北海—波罗的海商业网络
economicSystem.en: A feudal-market mixed economy. Rural production was dominated by free peasants, tenants, manorial estates, and church lands, while towns and ports operated through chartered privileges and toll systems, with long-distance trade increasingly tied to the North Sea-Baltic commercial network

source.zh: 司法罚金与王权特许收益
source.en: Judicial fines and royal prerogative income

category.zh: 外交、教会关系与赎买支出
category.en: Diplomacy, church relations, and redemption payments

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库规模有限，且相当部分财富并非现钱，而是分散于王室地产、应收赋税、铸币权和可征调劳役之中。在贵族逼迫王权让步的背景下，国库承受持续压力
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was limited in size, and a substantial part of royal wealth existed not as cash but in crown lands, receivable dues, minting rights, and mobilizable labor obligations. Under pressure from nobles forcing concessions from the crown, the treasury was under continuing strain

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持王权、宫廷和海上军事能力为优先，倾向动用非常征敛与特许收益填补缺口；但1282年前后贵族争取到对任意征税的限制，使国王财政灵活性下降
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized sustaining royal authority, the court, and maritime military capacity, often relying on extraordinary levies and prerogative incomes to fill gaps; but around 1282 the nobles won limits on arbitrary taxation, reducing the king’s fiscal flexibility

name.zh: 王室骑士与贵族重骑兵
name.en: Royal Knights and Noble Heavy Cavalry

description.zh: 由国王直属骑士、封臣与随从组成，是战场上的突击核心，但人数有限且依赖贵族忠诚
description.en: Composed of the king’s own knights, feudal vassals, and retainers, forming the battlefield shock core, though limited in number and dependent on noble loyalty

description.zh: 主要来自自由农民、庄园附属人口和城镇民兵，装备以长矛、盾牌、斧和弓为主，适合守城、集体防御和短期动员
description.en: Drawn mainly from free peasants, estate dependents, and town militias, armed chiefly with spears, shields, axes, and bows, suitable for garrisoning, collective defense, and short-term mobilization

description.zh: 以传统海上征役体系动员船只与水手，承担沿海巡逻、海峡控制、运输和对波罗的海的军事行动
description.en: Mobilized through the traditional naval levy system, providing ships and crews for coastal patrol, control of the straits, transport, and operations in the Baltic

technology.zh: 军备属于13世纪北欧水平：锁子甲、头盔、长盾、长矛、剑、战斧、弩与攻城器械广泛使用；海军以桨帆并用的北方船只和改进的军商两用船为主。丹麦擅长海峡机动和近海作战，但在长期围城和大陆大战中资源有限
technology.en: Military technology matched 13th-century Scandinavian norms: mail armor, helmets, long shields, spears, swords, battle-axes, crossbows, and siege engines were widely used; the navy relied on northern oared-and-sailed vessels and improved dual-use military-commercial ships. Denmark excelled at strait mobility and near-coastal warfare, but had limited resources for prolonged sieges and major continental campaigns

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自国内而非远方：王权与贵族冲突、地方叛乱风险、与挪威和瑞典在北欧权力平衡中的竞争，以及对波罗的海沿岸影响力的争夺。远东蒙古诸汗国并非直接军事威胁，但其欧亚贸易影响间接改变了北方商业环境
