taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要以黄金、象牙、牲畜、谷物储备和进口奢侈品形式保存，而非单一铸币储藏。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was held mainly in gold, ivory, livestock, grain reserves, and imported prestige goods rather than in a single coined medium.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持王廷威望、保障贸易通道与通过再分配稳定附庸关系。积累适度盈余以应对歉收、继承争议或贸易波动。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to maintain court prestige, secure trade routes, and stabilize dependent relationships through redistribution. Moderate surpluses were accumulated to meet poor harvests, succession disputes, or trade fluctuations.

unit.en: head of cattle, weights of gold

description.zh: 常备精锐，负责王都防卫、宫廷安全与威慑地方酋长。
description.en: A permanent elite force responsible for defense of the capital, palace security, and intimidation of local chiefs.

description.zh: 战时由各酋长征集，装备长矛、盾牌、弓箭和短兵器。
description.en: Raised by chiefs in wartime, armed with spears, shields, bows, and short weapons.

description.zh: 负责护卫商路、牧场与矿区，兼具侦察和治安功能。
description.en: Protected trade routes, grazing lands, and mining zones, serving both reconnaissance and policing roles.

technology.zh: 军备以铁制长矛、刀斧、木盾和弓箭为主，缺乏骑兵与攻城机械。优势在于动员地方附庸、熟悉地形以及控制石砌核心据点。
technology.en: Military equipment centered on iron-tipped spears, knives and axes, wooden shields, and bows, with little cavalry or siege machinery. Its strengths lay in mobilizing dependent chiefs, knowledge of terrain, and control of stone-built core strongholds.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自地区性附庸叛离、贸易路线竞争以及旱灾引发的内部紧张，而非大型外来帝国入侵。
threats.en: The main threats came from regional vassal defection, competition over trade routes, and internal stress caused by drought rather than from invasion by a large external empire.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口以绍纳语族农牧居民为主，集中在高原农业区、牧场地带与贸易节点周边。王都及其周边聚落人口密度较高，是政治、宗教与交换中心。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted mainly of Shona-speaking agro-pastoral communities concentrated in plateau farming zones, grazing areas, and trade nodes. The capital and its surrounding settlements had relatively high population density and served as political, ritual, and exchange centers.

name.zh: 马普本古布后继贸易聚落
name.en: Successor trade settlement in the Mapungubwe sphere

socialClasses.zh: 王族与贵族、地方酋长、祭司与顾问、自由农牧民、工匠、侍从与依附者。
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and nobles, local chiefs, ritual specialists and advisers, free farmers and herders, artisans, retainers, and dependents.

allies.zh: 与周边绍纳语酋邦及通往斯瓦希里海岸的中介商人保持合作关系。
allies.en: Maintained cooperative ties with neighboring Shona chiefdoms and intermediary traders connected to the Swahili coast.

enemies.zh: 无固定大型宿敌，但与竞争性内陆酋邦和争夺贸易控制权的势力存在摩擦。
enemies.en: It had no fixed great enemy, but friction existed with rival inland chiefdoms and powers competing for control of trade.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在控制金与象牙输出通道、吸纳附庸并通过礼物与婚姻维持区域影响力。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling gold and ivory export routes, incorporating dependents, and maintaining regional influence through gifts and marriage ties.

era.zh: 南部非洲晚期铁器时代
era.en: Southern African Late Iron Age

keyInnovations.zh: 干砌石建筑、铁器锻造、金矿开采与区域性贸易组织是其关键技术与制度优势。
keyInnovations.en: Dry-stone architecture, ironworking, gold extraction, and regional trade organization were its key technological and institutional strengths.

strengths.zh: 控制黄金与象牙贸易、拥有强大象征性王权，并具备高水平石建筑与区域整合能力。
strengths.en: It controlled gold and ivory trade, possessed powerful symbolic kingship, and showed advanced stone-building and regional integration capacity.

weaknesses.zh: 行政依赖个人效忠与贡赋网络，文字与制度化官僚较弱，易受生态压力和贸易转移影响。
weaknesses.en: Its administration depended on personal loyalty and tribute networks, while writing and formal bureaucracy were weak, making it vulnerable to ecological stress and shifts in trade.

outlook.zh: 至1280年前后仍处于兴盛期，但未来能否维持中心地位将取决于资源、继承稳定和贸易路线控制。
outlook.en: Around 1280 it remained in a flourishing phase, but its future centrality would depend on resources, stable succession, and control of trade routes.

description.zh: 大津巴布韦是1280年前后南部非洲以石砌都城、黄金贸易和神圣王权著称的强盛内陆王国。
description.en: Around 1280, Great Zimbabwe was a prosperous inland southern African kingdom renowned for its stone-built capital, gold trade, and sacred kingship.

governmentForm.zh: 以世袭酋长为核心、由多个地方酋邦构成的部落酋邦体系。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary chiefly system composed of multiple local chiefdoms under paramount caciques.

socialStructure.zh: 泰诺社会大体分为酋长、贵族与战士、宗教与仪式专家、普通村社成员等层次。多数人口居住在村落中，从事木薯种植、渔猎与手工业，社会关系以亲族与贡赋义务为纽带。
socialStructure.en: Taíno society was broadly stratified into caciques, nobles and warriors, ritual specialists, and common village members. Most people lived in settlements engaged in cassava cultivation, fishing, hunting, and handicrafts, with kinship and tribute obligations binding society together.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由各地酋长家族及其亲属网络构成，并依靠威望、祭仪权与贡赋分配维持权力。大酋长在区域层面协调盟约、婚姻与战争。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of chiefly lineages and their kin networks, maintaining power through prestige, ritual authority, and tribute redistribution. Paramount caciques coordinated alliances, marriages, and warfare at the regional level.

succession.zh: 继承通常遵循酋长家族的世袭原则，并常受母系亲属关系影响。若主支断绝，权位可在近亲支系中转移。
succession.en: Succession generally followed hereditary chiefly principles and was often influenced by matrilineal kinship ties. If the main line failed, authority could pass to a closely related branch.

structure.zh: 政治结构较为分散，由若干村社与地区酋邦组成，平时以贡赋、婚盟和仪式联系。大酋长并无严格官僚体系，而是通过亲族首领、传令者与祭司协助治理。
structure.en: The political structure was decentralized, made up of villages and regional chiefdoms linked by tribute, marriage alliances, and ritual ties. The paramount cacique did not command a strict bureaucracy, instead governing through kin leaders, messengers, and ritual specialists.

function.zh: 管理粮食、棉织品、劳役与礼物征集，并负责再分配。
function.en: Managed collection of food, cotton goods, labor obligations, and gifts, and oversaw redistribution.

function.zh: 主持公共仪式、祖灵与神灵崇拜，并协调村落间的纠纷与盟约。
function.en: Conducted public rites, ancestral and spirit worship, and coordinated disputes and alliances among settlements.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落首领和家族长者为核心，实际权力依附于个人威望与亲族支持。较大的聚落可能受次级酋长统辖，并向上级酋长纳贡。
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on village headmen and lineage elders, with authority resting on personal prestige and kin support. Larger settlements could be ruled by subordinate caciques who rendered tribute to higher chiefs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要依靠习惯法、长者裁断与酋长裁决，没有成文法典。对盗窃、违抗贡赋义务或破坏盟约的行为，通常以赔偿、羞辱或强制劳役处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based mainly on customary practice, elder mediation, and chiefly judgment, without a written legal code. Theft, refusal of tribute obligations, or breach of alliance was commonly handled through compensation, public shaming, or forced labor.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础不是货币税，而是以木薯、鱼获、棉布、工艺品和劳役构成的贡赋制度。大酋长通过再分配贡物巩固政治关系。
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base was not monetary taxation but a tribute system of cassava, fish, cotton cloth, crafts, and labor services. Paramount caciques reinforced political ties by redistributing these goods.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰

culturalAchievements.zh: 泰诺人擅长木薯农业、独木舟航海、棉纺织与仪式球戏，形成了富有加勒比特色的村社文化。其陶器、雕刻与神像制作体现出成熟的宗教象征体系。
culturalAchievements.en: The Taíno excelled in cassava agriculture, canoe navigation, cotton weaving, and ritual ball games, creating a distinctive Caribbean village culture. Their pottery, carving, and zemí iconography reflected a developed religious symbolic system.

languageFamily.zh: 阿拉瓦克语系
languageFamily.en: Arawakan language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵信仰、祖灵崇拜和对自然力量的敬畏为核心，政治与宗教密不可分。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic belief, ancestral reverence, and respect for natural forces, with politics and religion tightly intertwined.

writingSystem.zh: 无成熟文字系统，以口传传统、仪式歌谣和图像符号传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No fully developed writing system; knowledge was preserved through oral tradition, ritual chants, and symbolic imagery.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给农业和酋长再分配为主，市场交换规模较小。总产出主要体现在粮食、渔获、棉制品、木器和劳役，而非可计量的货币流通。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was dominated by subsistence agriculture and chiefly redistribution, with only limited market exchange. Output was expressed mainly in food, fish, cotton goods, wooden items, and labor rather than monetized circulation.

mainIndustries.zh: 木薯种植、渔业、棉纺织、陶器、木器与独木舟制作
mainIndustries.en: Cassava cultivation, fishing, cotton weaving, pottery, woodworking, and canoe building

tradeGoods.zh: 棉布、盐、鱼干、贝饰、陶器、木薯饼、羽饰
tradeGoods.en: Cotton cloth, salt, dried fish, shell ornaments, pottery, cassava bread, and feather goods

unitName.zh: 以贡物与物物交换计值
unitName.en: Valued in tribute goods and barter

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为耕地使用权、木薯储备、渔具、棉织品和住房，而非贵金属。酋长家族则控制更多劳力、礼器与储粮。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly expressed through access to fields, cassava stores, fishing gear, cotton goods, and housing rather than precious metals. Chiefly families controlled larger pools of labor, prestige objects, and stored food.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 村社自给与酋长贡赋再分配并存的部落经济体系。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy combining village subsistence with chiefly tribute redistribution.

unit.zh: 两白银（多为实物储备折算）
unit.en: taels of silver (mostly converted from in-kind reserves)

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库主要不是金银，而是储存的粮食、棉布、工具和礼器，其价值以白银折算。酋长财富高度依赖实物仓储与追随者支持。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly not of bullion but of stored food, cotton cloth, tools, and prestige goods, here converted into silver value. Chiefly wealth depended heavily on in-kind stores and follower support.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在维持贡赋平衡、保障酋长再分配能力，并在灾荒或冲突时动员劳役和储备。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining tribute balance, preserving chiefly redistributive capacity, and mobilizing labor and reserves during famine or conflict.

unit.zh: 以贡物与物物交换计值
unit.en: Valued in tribute goods and barter

description.zh: 由酋长直属战士组成，负责护卫首领与执行突袭行动。
description.en: Composed of warriors directly attached to caciques, protecting leaders and carrying out raids.

description.zh: 以村落成年男性为主，使用木矛、棍棒、弓箭和盾牌作战。
description.en: Primarily adult males from settlements, fighting with wooden spears, clubs, bows, and shields.

description.zh: 依靠大型独木舟沿海与岛际机动，适用于袭扰、侦察与运输。
description.en: Used large dugout canoes for coastal and inter-island movement, suitable for raiding, reconnaissance, and transport.

technology.zh: 军事技术以木、石、骨质武器和独木舟机动为主，不具备金属甲胄与攻城能力。其优势在熟悉海岸、湿热环境与短程突袭。
technology.en: Military technology relied on wood, stone, and bone weapons and canoe mobility, lacking metal armor and siege capability. Its advantages lay in knowledge of coasts, humid terrain, and short-range raids.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自岛际酋邦竞争、资源争夺、飓风后社会压力及与加勒比诸群体的冲突。
threats.en: Main threats came from inter-island chiefdom rivalry, resource competition, post-hurricane social stress, and conflict with Carib groups.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口以分散村落居民为主，集中在耕地、水源和海岸资源丰富地区。婴幼儿与青年人口占比较高，社会延续依赖稳定农业与渔业环境。
populationDescription.en: The population was mainly rural and dispersed across settlements near arable land, water sources, and coastal resources. Children and youth formed a large share, and social continuity depended on stable agricultural and fishing conditions.

socialClasses.zh: 大酋长与地方酋长、贵族与战士、祭司与仪式专家、普通村民、附属家庭与俘获者。
socialClasses.en: Paramount and local caciques, nobles and warriors, priests and ritual specialists, common villagers, dependent households, and captives.

allies.zh: 主要依靠邻近酋邦间的婚盟、互市与共同祭仪形成松散同盟。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly loose arrangements among neighboring chiefdoms through marriage, exchange, and shared ritual.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括争夺海域与资源的其他岛屿群体，尤其与加勒比方向的武装群体存在紧张关系。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included other island groups competing over waters and resources, especially armed groups associated with the Carib sphere.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持区域平衡、通过礼物交换稳定边界、必要时进行报复性袭击为主。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed at maintaining regional balance, stabilizing boundaries through gift exchange, and launching retaliatory raids when necessary.

era.zh: 加勒比晚期新石器—园艺社会阶段
era.en: Late Caribbean Neolithic-horticultural stage

keyInnovations.zh: 高垄木薯种植、木薯毒素处理技术、远程独木舟航行、棉纺织与仪式球场营建。
keyInnovations.en: Raised-field cassava cultivation, cassava detoxification techniques, long-range dugout canoe travel, cotton weaving, and ceremonial ball-court construction.

strengths.zh: 适应加勒比海岛环境，农业与渔业结合稳健，航海与村社组织灵活。酋长再分配体系有助于维持社会整合与灾后恢复。
strengths.en: Well adapted to the Caribbean island environment, combining resilient agriculture and fishing with flexible navigation and village organization. The chiefly redistributive system helped preserve cohesion and post-disaster recovery.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏金属工具与重武器，政治整合度有限，面对长期战争或大范围外来入侵时脆弱。经济剩余较低，也限制了专业化发展。
weaknesses.en: It lacked metal tools and heavy weapons, and its political integration was limited, making it vulnerable in prolonged warfare or against large-scale external invasion. Low economic surplus also constrained specialization.

outlook.zh: 至1280年前后，泰诺诸酋邦总体繁荣稳定，但其未来仍高度受岛际竞争、生态波动与外部接触影响。
outlook.en: Around 1280, the Taíno chiefdoms were generally prosperous and stable, but their future remained highly dependent on inter-island rivalry, ecological fluctuation, and outside contact.

description.zh: 1280年前后的泰诺人是以农业、渔业和酋长贡赋网络维系的加勒比海岛酋邦社会。
description.en: Around 1280, the Taíno were a Caribbean island chiefdom society sustained by agriculture, fishing, and chiefly tribute networks.

governmentForm.zh: 以安德里安纳王族为核心、依托高地聚落网络的早期王国体制。
governmentForm.en: An early kingdom centered on the Andriana royal nobility and supported by a network of highland settlements.

socialStructure.zh: 梅里纳社会以王族与贵族、自由平民、依附群体和奴役人口的雏形层次构成。高地稻作、牲畜所有权与亲族关系决定了社会地位与义务分配。
socialStructure.en: Merina society was structured around an emerging hierarchy of royals and nobles, free commoners, dependent groups, and early forms of servile population. Highland rice cultivation, cattle ownership, and kinship ties determined status and obligations.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是安德里安纳王族及其附属贵族家族，控制要塞山丘、祭祀权与军事动员。其权力基础建立在土地、牲畜和人身依附关系之上。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of the Andriana royal nobility and allied noble houses, controlling fortified hills, ritual authority, and military mobilization. Their power rested on land, cattle, and relations of personal dependence.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族内部世袭，但实际继承常受长幼次序、母系联姻和贵族支持影响。继位争议可能通过协商、结盟或武力解决。
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the royal line, but actual succession was shaped by seniority, maternal alliances, and noble backing. Disputes could be settled by negotiation, alliance, or force.

structure.zh: 1280年前后的梅里纳政权仍处于早期整合阶段，中央权力围绕王室山城与近支贵族展开。国家机构不复杂，但已具备征役、征贡、治安与祭祀协调的基本职能。
structure.en: Around 1280, the Merina polity was still in an early phase of consolidation, with central authority centered on the royal hill settlement and close noble kin. State institutions were not elaborate, but they already handled labor levies, tribute, order, and ritual coordination.

function.zh: 征收稻米、牲畜、手工业品与劳役，并管理王室储备。
function.en: Collected rice, cattle, craft goods, and labor obligations, and managed royal reserves.

function.zh: 组织山地防御、村社征召与武装集结。
function.en: Organized hill defense, village levies, and armed musters.

name.zh: 王族礼仪与祖灵祭祀事务
name.en: Royal Ritual and Ancestor Cult Affairs

function.zh: 负责祖灵祭典、加冕礼制与王室合法性象征。
function.en: Handled ancestral rites, coronation observances, and symbols of royal legitimacy.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依托山丘聚落首领、家族长者和王室委任的亲贵。边缘地区对王权的服从程度不一，通常通过贡赋、婚姻和军事支持维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on hill-settlement leaders, lineage elders, and royal kinsmen appointed by the court. Peripheral areas varied in obedience to royal power and were held through tribute, marriage, and military support.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、王室裁断与祖先传统为基础，尚无系统成文法。土地争议、牲畜纠纷和婚姻义务多由长者与贵族共同裁决。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, royal judgment, and ancestral tradition, without a systematic written code. Land disputes, cattle quarrels, and marriage obligations were commonly judged by elders and nobles together.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政来源主要是实物贡赋与劳役征发，包括稻米、牛只、手工业品与公共工程劳力。王室通过控制高地要塞与储粮体系实现征收。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income came chiefly from in-kind tribute and labor levies, including rice, cattle, craft goods, and manpower for public works. The crown collected these through control of highland forts and storage systems.

religion.zh: 祖先崇拜

culturalAchievements.zh: 梅里纳高地社会发展出成熟的山丘聚落、防御土木工事和稻作管理传统，并以口传谱系巩固王权记忆。祖灵祭祀、葬俗与王族礼制构成文化核心。
culturalAchievements.en: Highland Merina society developed fortified hill settlements, earthwork defenses, and rice-management traditions, while oral genealogy reinforced royal memory. Ancestor worship, funerary customs, and royal ritual formed the cultural core.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系马达加斯加语支
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Malagasy branch

philosophy.zh: 重视祖灵、王权神圣性、土地与亲族连续性，政治秩序被视为人与祖先之间契约的延续。
philosophy.en: It emphasized ancestors, the sacred character of kingship, and the continuity of land and kin, viewing political order as an extension of the bond between the living and the ancestors.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字系统，以口传谱系、仪式语言与记忆传统保存历史。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; history was preserved through oral genealogy, ritual speech, and memory traditions.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以高地稻作为核心，辅以牛牧、铁器制作、木工与地方交换。虽然货币化程度有限，但粮食剩余与牲畜财富使其比一般部落社会更具积累能力。
gdpDescription.en: The economy centered on highland rice cultivation, supported by cattle herding, ironworking, woodworking, and local exchange. Monetization remained limited, but food surplus and livestock wealth gave it greater capacity for accumulation than many tribal societies.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、牛牧、铁器锻造、木工、织造、土木营建
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, cattle herding, iron forging, woodworking, weaving, and earthwork construction

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、牛只、铁器、木器、编织品、陶器、蜂蜡
tradeGoods.en: Rice, cattle, iron tools, wooden goods, woven products, pottery, and beeswax

unitName.zh: 以稻米、牛只和铁器计值
unitName.en: Valued in rice, cattle, and iron goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常由稻田、牛群、铁制工具、房屋与亲族支持体现。贵族家庭则额外拥有更多劳役人口、山丘防御地和祭礼资源。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was usually expressed in rice fields, cattle, iron tools, houses, and kin support. Noble households additionally held more labor dependents, defended hill sites, and ritual resources.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以王室征贡、家族土地经营和地方交换相结合的早期王国经济。
economicSystem.en: An early kingdom economy combining royal tribute, lineage-based landholding, and local exchange.

source.en: Iron goods and local exchange gains

unit.zh: 两白银（以粮食、牛只和铁器折算）
unit.en: taels of silver (converted from grain, cattle, and iron goods)

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室财富主要储存在稻米、牛群、铁器和实物贡纳中，少有成规模金银储备。财政稳定性取决于收成、牛疫情况与地方忠诚。
treasuryDescription.en: Royal wealth was stored mainly in rice, cattle, iron goods, and in-kind tribute, with little large-scale bullion reserve. Fiscal stability depended on harvests, cattle disease conditions, and local loyalty.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向积累实物储备、巩固王室山城和通过礼仪再分配换取政治忠诚。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored building in-kind reserves, strengthening the royal hill center, and using ceremonial redistribution to secure political loyalty.

unit.zh: 以稻米、牛只和铁器计值
unit.en: Valued in rice, cattle, and iron goods

description.zh: 由王室直接供养的核心武装，负责王城守卫与突击作战。
description.en: A core force directly maintained by the crown, responsible for guarding the royal center and shock action.

description.zh: 由村社与贵族动员，装备长矛、盾牌、弓箭和铁制刀具。
description.en: Mobilized by villages and nobles, equipped with spears, shields, bows, and iron blades.

description.zh: 驻守山丘防御点与土木工事，擅长本地地形防御。
description.en: Stationed at hill defenses and earthwork positions, skilled in terrain-based defense.

technology.zh: 军备以铁制刃器、长矛、弓箭、木盾和山地工事为主，缺乏骑兵与火器。其优势在高地防御、堡垒山丘和短程动员效率。
technology.en: Armament relied on iron blades, spears, bows, wooden shields, and hill fortifications, with no cavalry or firearms. Its strengths lay in highland defense, fortified hills, and efficient short-range mobilization.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自马达加斯加高地内部竞争势力、周边族群冲突、收成不稳及地方贵族离心。
threats.en: Main threats came from rival powers in the Malagasy highlands, conflict with neighboring groups, unstable harvests, and centrifugal tendencies among local nobles.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于伊梅里纳高地的山丘聚落和稻作谷地，分布较为密集。由于农业定居程度较高，梅里纳社会的人口聚合度明显高于一般部落共同体。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in hill settlements and rice-growing valleys of Imerina, with relatively dense distribution. Because of its stronger agricultural sedentism, Merina society showed greater population concentration than a typical tribal community.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与安德里安纳贵族、自由平民、依附劳役人口、战俘与早期奴役群体。
socialClasses.en: Royalty and Andriana nobles, free commoners, dependent laborers, war captives, and early servile groups.

allies.zh: 主要盟友是通过婚姻与贡纳联系的高地亲缘聚落和附属贵族集团。
allies.en: Its main allies were kin-linked highland settlements and subordinate noble groups connected by marriage and tribute.

enemies.zh: 敌对者主要为争夺高地控制权的周边聚落联盟与地方竞争王系。
enemies.en: Enemies were chiefly neighboring settlement coalitions and rival royal lineages contesting control of the highlands.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以统一伊梅里纳高地、控制山丘要塞和以联姻吸纳邻近势力为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on unifying the Imerina highlands, controlling fortified hills, and absorbing nearby powers through dynastic marriage.

era.zh: 马达加斯加高地早期铁器王国阶段
era.en: Early Iron Age highland kingdom stage of Madagascar

keyInnovations.zh: 高地稻田经营、山丘防御工事、铁器锻造、牲畜财富管理与口传王系治理传统。
keyInnovations.en: Highland rice-field management, hill fortifications, iron forging, livestock wealth management, and oral dynastic governance traditions.

strengths.zh: 拥有较稳定的高地农业基础、可积累的牲畜财富和逐步成形的王权结构，利于持续扩张。山丘防御体系增强了其在区域冲突中的生存能力。
strengths.en: It possessed a relatively stable highland agricultural base, accumulable livestock wealth, and an increasingly coherent royal structure that favored continued expansion. Its hill-defense system improved survivability in regional conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 国家整合仍不完全，行政依赖亲族与地方贵族，缺乏书面行政和远距离控制能力。经济剩余有限，也制约了更复杂的专业官僚与军备发展。
weaknesses.en: State integration remained incomplete, administration depended on kinship and local nobles, and there was no written administration or strong long-distance control. Limited surplus also constrained more complex bureaucracy and armament.

outlook.zh: 在1280年前后，梅里纳王国处于上升整合期，只要王室能压制贵族离心并稳定稻作生产，其区域主导地位有望增强。
outlook.en: Around 1280, the Kingdom of Merina was in a rising phase of consolidation; if the crown could restrain noble fragmentation and stabilize rice production, its regional predominance was likely to grow.

description.zh: 1280年前后的梅里纳王国是马达加斯加高地上一个依靠稻作、山丘防御和王族整合而兴起的早期王国。
description.en: Around 1280, the Kingdom of Merina was an emerging highland Malagasy kingdom built on rice cultivation, hill defense, and royal consolidation.

governmentForm.zh: 以港口王都为核心、通过附庸港邦与海峡节点维系的松散海上王国联盟。
governmentForm.en: A loose maritime mandala polity centered on a port-capital and sustained through tributary ports and strategic strait nodes.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与港口贵族、佛教僧团、商人、船主、手工业者以及沿河与沿海村社居民构成。内陆与海口地区联系密切，但政治整合已较早期减弱。外来商人如马来人、爪哇人、泰米尔人与华商在经济生活中占有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house and port aristocracy, Buddhist clergy, merchants, shipowners, artisans, and riverine and coastal village communities. Inland and estuarine zones remained linked, though political integration was weaker than in Srivijaya’s earlier height. Foreign merchants, including Malays, Javanese, Tamils, and Chinese traders, played an important role in economic life.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是巴邻旁王室、港口首领与控制航运税收的地方豪族。佛教寺院网络也为统治合法性提供支持。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class was mainly the Palembang royal house, port chiefs, and local magnates who controlled shipping dues. Buddhist monastic networks also supported political legitimacy.

succession.zh: 王位继承以王族血统与港口精英承认为主，并常受地方势力平衡影响。到13世纪后期，继承秩序更易受到外部强权与区域竞争干扰。
succession.en: Succession relied on royal lineage and recognition by port elites, and was often shaped by local power balances. By the late thirteenth century, succession had become more vulnerable to external powers and regional competition.

structure.zh: 政权以王廷和港口管理机构为中心，实际控制力主要集中在巴邻旁及周边河口。外围港口多通过朝贡、婚姻与贸易义务维系，而非严密官僚直辖。随着爪哇势力上升，中央号令的执行力下降。
structure.en: The polity centered on the royal court and port administration, with effective control concentrated around Palembang and nearby estuaries. Outlying ports were bound more by tribute, marriage ties, and trade obligations than by strict bureaucratic rule. As Javanese power rose, the center’s ability to enforce commands declined.

function.zh: 处理王室仪礼、附庸联络、使节接待与对外封赠。
function.en: Managed royal ceremony, vassal relations, reception of envoys, and external investiture and tribute.

function.zh: 征收船税、货税，监管泊位、仓储与过境贸易。
function.en: Collected shipping and cargo duties and supervised berths, warehousing, and transit trade.

function.zh: 负责河运、堤岸维护、粮食调配与王室供给。
function.en: Oversaw river transport, embankment maintenance, grain distribution, and royal provisioning.

localAdmin.zh: 地方多由港口首领、河口酋长和依附王室的地方豪族管理。中央通常通过税契、贡品和贸易特许维持影响力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was usually exercised by port chiefs, estuarine leaders, and regional magnates tied to the court. The center maintained influence through tax arrangements, tribute, and commercial privileges.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以王命、港口惯例、海商习俗与佛教道德规范并行为主。商贸纠纷多由港口长官与商人团体调解。
legalSystem.en: Law rested on royal decrees, port custom, maritime practice, and Buddhist moral norms. Commercial disputes were often mediated by harbor officials and merchant communities.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠港口关税、过境税、附庸贡纳与王室垄断收益。农村征收较轻，国家收入高度依赖海峡贸易流量。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended chiefly on port customs, transit dues, tributary payments, and royal monopoly income. Rural taxation was relatively light, making state finances highly dependent on strait trade volumes.

religion.zh: 佛教

culturalAchievements.zh: 三佛齐长期作为佛教学习与海上交流中心，对马来世界宗教传播和港市文化形成影响深远。其港口文化促进了多语、多族商贸社群的融合。
culturalAchievements.en: Srivijaya long served as a center of Buddhist learning and maritime exchange, profoundly influencing religious transmission and port-city culture across the Malay world. Its harbor culture fostered multilingual and multiethnic commercial communities.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系（马来语支）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian language family (Malayic branch)

philosophy.zh: 以大乘佛教与密教传统为主，并融合马来王权神圣观念与海洋祭祀习俗。
philosophy.en: Primarily Mahayana Buddhism with tantric elements, blended with Malay sacral kingship and maritime ritual traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 古马来语与梵语并用，采用婆罗米系字母的地方变体，亦见碑铭传统延续。
writingSystem.en: Old Malay and Sanskrit were used, written in local Brahmic-derived scripts, with an enduring inscriptional tradition.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以转口贸易、港口服务、航运税收与周边稻米、林产和香料集散为主。到1280年前后，其总量仍可观，但已明显低于全盛时期。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was driven by entrepot trade, port services, shipping dues, and the aggregation of rice, forest products, and spices from surrounding regions. Around 1280 its overall scale remained significant, though clearly below its peak.

mainIndustries.zh: 转口贸易、航运、仓储、林产采集、稻作、造船
mainIndustries.en: Entrepot trade, shipping, warehousing, forest extraction, rice cultivation, shipbuilding

tradeGoods.zh: 樟脑、沉香、金、锡、蜂蜡、龟甲、象牙、香料、稻米、陶瓷与纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Camphor, aloeswood, gold, tin, beeswax, tortoiseshell, ivory, spices, rice, ceramics, and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 港口商人和船主家产较丰，内陆与渔农村社财富较低且差异明显。
householdWealth.en: Harbor merchants and shipowners held comparatively substantial wealth, while inland and fishing-farming communities possessed much less, with marked inequality.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王权控制关键港口与关税的海上贸易经济，兼具地方贡纳与市场交换特征。
economicSystem.en: A maritime trade economy in which kingship controlled key ports and customs, combined with local tribute and market exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银、金、香料与高价值货物储备为主，流动性受贸易季节影响较大。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted mainly of silver, gold, spices, and other high-value stores, with liquidity strongly affected by trade seasons.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政策略偏向维持港口运转与海上威望，但在贸易受阻时容易出现赤字。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized sustaining port operations and maritime prestige, but deficits emerged easily when trade was disrupted.

description.zh: 以轻快桨帆船为主，负责海峡巡逻、护航与河口防御。
description.en: Built around light oared and sailed vessels, responsible for strait patrol, convoy protection, and estuarine defense.

description.zh: 由王都卫队、港口守军及附庸提供的步兵组成，装备矛、盾、弓与短刃。
description.en: Composed of palace guards, port garrisons, and infantry supplied by dependents, equipped with spears, shields, bows, and short blades.

description.zh: 来自沿河与沿海社群，在战争或治安需要时短期征集。
description.en: Raised from riverine and coastal communities for short-term service in war or security operations.

technology.zh: 擅长热带海域航行、轻型战船运用与港口防御，但在攻城器械、重装部队和持久大会战方面有限。
technology.en: It was adept at tropical seafaring, light warcraft, and port defense, but limited in siege equipment, heavy troops, and sustained large-scale campaigning.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自爪哇势力扩张、马六甲海峡航线竞争以及附庸港口离心化。
threats.en: Main threats came from expanding Javanese power, competition over Malacca Strait routes, and the centrifugal drift of tributary ports.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于苏门答腊东南部河口、沿海港市与周边村社。王都及主要港口人口混杂，包含本地马来人和多族外来商人。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in southeastern Sumatran estuaries, coastal ports, and nearby villages. The capital and major ports were ethnically mixed, including local Malays and many foreign merchants.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与港口贵族、僧侣、商人与船主、工匠、自由村民、依附劳作者与奴婢。
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and port aristocracy, clergy, merchants and shipowners, artisans, free villagers, dependent laborers, and slaves.

allies.zh: 与部分马来港邦、海峡地方首领及往来商人团体保持务实合作。
allies.en: Maintained pragmatic cooperation with some Malay port polities, local strait chiefs, and merchant communities.

enemies.zh: 与爪哇崛起政权存在结构性竞争，并受区域海上强权挤压。
enemies.en: Faced structural rivalry with rising Javanese states and pressure from other regional maritime powers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持航道中介地位、争取朝贡承认与保护商路为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving its intermediary role on sea routes, securing tributary recognition, and protecting commerce.

era.zh: 13世纪海上东南亚港市技术阶段
era.en: 13th-century maritime Southeast Asian port-state technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 季风航海经验、河海联运体系、热带造船与港口仓储组织较为成熟。
keyInnovations.en: Monsoon navigation knowledge, integrated river-sea transport, tropical shipbuilding, and organized port warehousing were relatively mature.

strengths.zh: 地处战略海峡、港口传统深厚、商业网络广泛且文化吸引力仍存。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic strait position, had a deep port tradition, broad trade networks, and lingering cultural prestige.

weaknesses.zh: 中央控制力衰退、财政过度依赖贸易、军力有限且附庸忠诚不稳。
weaknesses.en: Central control had weakened, finances were overly dependent on trade, military capacity was limited, and subordinate loyalty was unstable.

outlook.zh: 若无法重建对关键港口和海峡通道的控制，其区域主导地位将继续下滑。
outlook.en: Unless it regained control over key ports and strait passages, its regional primacy would continue to decline.

description.zh: 1280年前后的三佛齐仍是重要海上贸易中心，但已由昔日霸权转入衰落守成阶段。
description.en: Around 1280, Srivijaya remained an important maritime trade center, but had shifted from former hegemony into a phase of defensive decline.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，诸部族传统长老制
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; diverse tribal elder traditions

governmentForm.zh: 由众多语言群与氏族共同构成、以长老协商和仪式权威维系的分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal society composed of many language groups and clans, held together by elder consensus and ritual authority.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲属、氏族、图腾和婚姻规则组织，人与土地、祖灵和仪式网络关系密切。多数群体以季节性移动为生，沿海、河谷、沙漠和林地生计模式差异极大。社会地位通常取决于年龄、知识、仪式资格与调解能力，而非固定官阶。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized through kinship, clan ties, totems, and marriage rules, with close ties among people, land, ancestral beings, and ritual networks. Most groups lived seasonally mobile lives, with great variation among coastal, riverine, desert, and woodland subsistence regimes. Status usually depended on age, knowledge, ritual qualification, and mediating skill rather than fixed office.

rulingClass.zh: 并无统一意义上的统治阶层，较有影响者为长老、仪式知识持有者和善于协调交换关系的首领人物。其权威通常建立在传统、口述知识与人际威望之上。
rulingClass.en: There was no ruling class in a centralized sense; the most influential figures were elders, custodians of ritual knowledge, and leaders skilled at coordinating exchange relations. Their authority rested on tradition, oral knowledge, and personal prestige.

succession.zh: 领导权多非世袭王位，而是在家族资历、仪式资格和群体认可中逐步形成。某些职责可在亲属线中传递，但仍需获得长老与社群承认。
succession.en: Leadership was generally not a hereditary throne but emerged through family seniority, ritual standing, and group recognition. Some responsibilities could pass through kin lines, but still required acceptance by elders and the community.

structure.zh: 不存在统一中央政府，各地群体按照生态区和传统法共同体自我治理。重要事务如婚配、领地使用、争端处理与仪式活动由长老会议或相关亲属群体决定。跨地区联系依靠贸易路径、仪式聚会与婚姻联盟维持。
structure.en: There was no unified central government; groups governed themselves according to ecological region and customary-law communities. Important matters such as marriage arrangements, land use, dispute settlement, and ritual activities were decided by elder councils or relevant kin groups. Interregional ties were maintained through trade routes, ceremonial gatherings, and marriage alliances.

function.zh: 处理争端、主持迁徙与领地协商、维护婚姻和仪式规范。
function.en: Handled disputes, coordinated movement and territorial negotiation, and upheld marriage and ritual norms.

function.zh: 保存歌线、圣地知识、成年礼程序与跨部族仪式联系。
function.en: Preserved songlines, sacred-site knowledge, initiation procedures, and intertribal ceremonial links.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由各部族、氏族与营地首领自行处理，没有固定州县体制。资源丰富地区的季节性集会点往往承担临时协调中心作用。
localAdmin.en: Local governance was handled by individual tribes, clans, and camp leaders, without fixed provincial structures. Seasonal gathering places in resource-rich areas often served as temporary coordination centers.

legalSystem.zh: 法律即习惯法和祖灵秩序，围绕领地权、婚姻禁忌、赎偿与仪式责任运作。惩处通常以调解、赔偿、公开仪式或有限报复实现。
legalSystem.en: Law consisted of customary law and ancestral order, operating around territorial rights, marriage taboos, compensation, and ritual obligations. Enforcement usually took the form of mediation, restitution, public ritual, or limited retaliatory action.

taxationSystem.zh: 无固定税制，资源分享、馈赠交换和仪式性再分配承担类似财政功能。战时或大型集会时可临时集中食物、器物与劳力。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed taxation system; resource sharing, gift exchange, and ritual redistribution fulfilled comparable functions. In wartime or major gatherings, food, goods, and labor could be concentrated temporarily.

religion.zh: 梦幻时代

culturalAchievements.zh: 澳大利亚原住民在歌线地理、生态知识、火管理、仪式舞蹈与岩画传统方面成就卓著。其复杂口述体系能在无文字条件下长期保存法律、历史和地景记忆。
culturalAchievements.en: Australian Aboriginal peoples achieved remarkable sophistication in songline geography, ecological knowledge, fire management, ceremonial dance, and rock art traditions. Their complex oral systems preserved law, history, and landscape memory over long periods without writing.

languageFamily.zh: 澳大利亚诸语言，尤以帕马-恩永甘语系分布最广
languageFamily.en: Australian languages, especially the widespread Pama-Nyungan family

philosophy.zh: 以“梦时”宇宙观为核心，强调祖灵造世、土地神圣性、歌线传承与人与自然的责任关系。
philosophy.en: Centered on Dreaming cosmology, emphasizing ancestral creation, the sacredness of land, the transmission of songlines, and reciprocal responsibilities between people and nature.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字体系，以口述传统、岩画、身体彩绘、雕刻与仪式符号传承知识。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, rock art, body painting, carving, and ritual symbols.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以采集、渔猎、小规模定点资源管理与区域交换为主，不适合用国家农业税收模型衡量。若按银价折算，其物质产出不高，但生态适应能力极强。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on foraging, fishing, hunting, small-scale fixed-resource management, and regional exchange, and is not well measured by agrarian tax-state models. Converted into silver equivalents, material output was modest, but ecological adaptation was extremely strong.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、渔猎、石器制作、木器与纤维工艺、赭石与贝壳交换
mainIndustries.en: Foraging, fishing and hunting, stone-tool production, wood and fiber crafts, ochre and shell exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 赭石、石斧石料、贝壳、木矛、篮具、树脂、皮革、仪式物品
tradeGoods.en: Ochre, stone for axe heads, shells, wooden spears, basketry, resin, hides, and ritual goods

name.zh: 无铸币，以物易物与礼物交换
name.en: No coinage; barter and gift exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为对土地和资源点的使用权、工具质量、亲属网络和仪式知识，而非可积累货币。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through access rights to land and resource sites, quality of tools, kin networks, and ritual knowledge rather than accumulated money.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲属共享、习俗再分配和长距离交换网络为基础的采猎型经济体系。
economicSystem.en: A hunter-forager economic system based on kin sharing, customary redistribution, and long-distance exchange networks.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

source.en: Exchange of tools and specialty goods

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中金库，所谓储备主要体现为储存食物、工具、仪式物品和可动员的亲属义务网络。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves consisted mainly of stored food, tools, ritual objects, and mobilizable kin obligations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 经济分配重在保障群体生存与仪式连续性，而非积累财政盈余。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource allocation prioritized group survival and ritual continuity rather than accumulating fiscal surpluses.

description.zh: 以矛、投矛器、棍棒和盾牌作战，多用于部族冲突、报复和领地防卫。
description.en: Fought with spears, spear-throwers, clubs, and shields, mainly in tribal conflict, reprisal, and territorial defense.

description.zh: 依靠地形熟悉、追踪技术与隐蔽移动执行警戒和侦察。
description.en: Used terrain knowledge, tracking skill, and stealth movement for warning and reconnaissance.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各部族长老与战时首领协商指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander; direction was coordinated by tribal elders and temporary war leaders

technology.zh: 军事技术以木石骨器和投矛器为主，远程杀伤与地形运用能力较强，但缺乏金属武器、攻城与重装体系。
technology.en: Military technology centered on wood, stone, and bone weapons and spear-throwers, with strong ranged skill and terrain use but no metal arms, siege systems, or heavy equipment.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要风险来自局部部族冲突、资源波动与生态压力，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: Major risks came from local tribal conflict, resource fluctuation, and ecological pressure rather than large-scale state warfare.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口分散于澳大利亚大陆各生态区，沿海、河系和资源稳定地带较为密集。不存在统一城市，但有若干季节性大型聚会地和长期营地。
populationDescription.en: Population was dispersed across Australia’s ecological zones, denser along coasts, river systems, and resource-stable areas. There were no true cities, but there were several large seasonal gathering places and long-used camps.

name.zh: 穆雷河下游大型聚会地

socialClasses.zh: 长老与仪式知识持有者、成年战猎者、普通家户成员、青年受训者；社会分层较弱但资历与知识差异明显。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual knowledge holders, adult hunters-warriors, ordinary household members, and youths in training; social stratification was limited but distinctions of seniority and knowledge were clear.

allies.zh: 联盟以邻近婚姻集团、共同仪式伙伴和交换网络成员为主。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly with neighboring marriage groups, ceremonial partners, and members of exchange networks.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多为局部和临时性的邻近部族冲突，并无统一外敌概念。
enemies.en: Hostile relationships were mostly local and temporary conflicts with neighboring groups; there was no single unified external enemy concept.

foreignPolicy.zh: 其“对外关系”本质上是跨部族交换、通婚、圣地访问与边界协商。
foreignPolicy.en: Their 'foreign policy' was essentially intertribal exchange, intermarriage, sacred-site visitation, and boundary negotiation.

era.zh: 13世纪澳大利亚采猎社会技术阶段
era.en: 13th-century Australian hunter-forager technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 投矛器、精细石器、火管理、鱼坝与季节性资源调度体系体现出高度环境适应性。
keyInnovations.en: The spear-thrower, refined stone tools, fire management, fish traps, and seasonal resource scheduling reflected highly developed environmental adaptation.

strengths.zh: 对复杂环境适应极强，知识传承稳定，社会韧性高，跨地区交换网络广泛。
strengths.en: They possessed exceptional adaptation to varied environments, stable knowledge transmission, strong social resilience, and wide exchange networks.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权、集中储备和金属技术，难以进行大规模持久军事动员。
weaknesses.en: They lacked unified state structures, concentrated reserves, and metal technology, making large and sustained military mobilization difficult.

outlook.zh: 在1280年前后，此类社会仍能长期稳定延续，但其发展路径与欧亚农业国家并不相同。
outlook.en: Around 1280 these societies could continue stably for long periods, though their developmental trajectory differed fundamentally from Eurasian agrarian states.

description.zh: 1280年的澳大利亚原住民社会由众多分散部族组成，以梦时信仰、口述法律与生态知识维系广阔大陆上的生活秩序。
description.en: In 1280, Australian Aboriginal society consisted of many dispersed tribal groups whose order across the continent was sustained by Dreaming belief, oral law, and ecological knowledge.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，诸游群与牧猎社群传统
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; various band and pastoral-forager traditions

governmentForm.zh: 由南部非洲多支采猎与早期牧养群体构成、以小规模游群共识治理为主的分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal society of southern African foraging and early pastoral communities governed mainly through small-band consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 社会基本单位为游群、亲属群和区域性会聚网络，成员通过血缘、婚姻与共享水源和猎场相联系。部分群体维持采猎生活，部分科伊人群兼营牧养。社会分层较浅，威望主要来自年长、狩猎能力、谈判技巧和知识传承。
socialStructure.en: The basic social units were bands, kin groups, and regional networks of aggregation, linked by descent, marriage, and shared water sources and hunting grounds. Some groups remained primarily foragers, while certain Khoikhoi communities combined herding with foraging. Social stratification was shallow, and prestige came mainly from age, hunting ability, negotiating skill, and knowledge transmission.

rulingClass.zh: 没有稳定的贵族阶层，较有影响者是首领、长老、猎队领头人和牲畜较多的牧养家户。其权威通常有限，需依赖说服和馈赠维持。
rulingClass.en: There was no stable aristocratic class; the most influential figures were headmen, elders, hunting leaders, and herding households with more livestock. Their authority was usually limited and depended on persuasion and generosity.

succession.zh: 领导职能往往在亲属网络中延续，但并非绝对世袭。若首领失去支持，游群成员可迁离或转附他人。
succession.en: Leadership roles often continued within kin networks but were not strictly hereditary. If a headman lost support, band members could leave or attach themselves to another leader.

structure.zh: 并无中央国家结构，各游群在水源、牧地和狩猎范围内独立协商治理。重大事项如迁徙、婚配、赔偿和与邻群关系由长老和有威望者共同决定。区域间联络依靠婚姻、交换与季节性会聚。
structure.en: There was no central state structure; each band governed itself through negotiation over water sources, grazing, and hunting ranges. Major matters such as migration, marriage, compensation, and relations with neighboring groups were decided collectively by elders and respected figures. Interregional contact relied on marriage, exchange, and seasonal aggregation.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、水源使用、争端解决与对外交涉。
function.en: Coordinated migration, water use, dispute resolution, and dealings with outsiders.

function.zh: 组织放牧、集体狩猎，并在营地内分配肉食与日用品。
function.en: Organized herding, collective hunting, and the distribution of meat and daily necessities within camps.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由各营地或游群自行处理，没有固定行政边界。对水井、季节牧场和采集区的使用权通过惯例和协商界定。
localAdmin.en: Local affairs were handled by each camp or band, without fixed administrative borders. Rights to wells, seasonal pasture, and gathering areas were defined by custom and negotiation.

legalSystem.zh: 法律依赖习惯法、亲属责任与赔偿机制，强调调解与恢复群体平衡。严重冲突可能导致分群、决斗或有限报复。
legalSystem.en: Law relied on customary norms, kin responsibility, and compensation mechanisms, emphasizing mediation and restoration of group balance. Serious conflict could lead to fission, duel, or limited retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税收，首领可在集会、婚礼、狩猎或危机时期集中牲畜、食物与劳力。馈赠和再分配是维系权威的重要方式。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; headmen could concentrate livestock, food, and labor during gatherings, marriages, hunts, or crises. Gift-giving and redistribution were essential to maintaining authority.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰

culturalAchievements.zh: 科伊桑诸群体在岩画艺术、追踪技术、药用植物知识和适应干旱环境的生计智慧方面极为突出。其音乐节奏、口弦与治病舞传统具有鲜明特色。
culturalAchievements.en: Khoisan groups were especially distinguished in rock art, tracking skill, medicinal plant knowledge, and subsistence strategies adapted to arid environments. Their musical rhythms, mouth-bow traditions, and healing dances were highly distinctive.

languageFamily.zh: 科伊桑相关语言群与部分早期科伊人语言传统
languageFamily.en: Khoisan-related language groups and early Khoikhoi linguistic traditions

philosophy.zh: 信仰以泛灵观、祖灵观念、治病舞与人与动物世界之间的灵性联系为核心。
philosophy.en: Belief centered on animism, ancestral ideas, healing dances, and spiritual connections between people and the animal world.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字体系，以口述叙事、歌舞、岩画和身体记忆传承传统。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; tradition was transmitted through oral narrative, song, dance, rock art, and embodied memory.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济基础为采集、狩猎、局部牧养和邻近群体间交换。物质总量有限，但在干旱与半干旱地区具有较高生存效率。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on gathering, hunting, localized herding, and exchange with neighboring groups. Material output was limited, but survival efficiency in arid and semi-arid environments was high.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、狩猎、牧养、皮革加工、骨木器制作、颜料与珠饰制作
mainIndustries.en: Gathering, hunting, herding, hide processing, bone and wooden toolmaking, pigment and bead production

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、肉干、牲畜、鸵鸟蛋壳珠、赭石、药材、骨器
tradeGoods.en: Hides, dried meat, livestock, ostrich eggshell beads, ochre, medicinal materials, and bone tools

name.zh: 无统一货币，以牲畜与物品交换
name.en: No unified currency; livestock and goods exchange

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要表现为牲畜数量、可靠水源联系、工具储备与婚姻交换网络。采猎群体财富积累较少，牧养群体差异更明显。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through livestock holdings, access to dependable water, tool stocks, and marriage-exchange networks. Foraging groups accumulated less, while pastoral groups showed greater differentiation.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per person per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以亲属共享、营地再分配和小范围交换为核心的采猎—牧养混合经济。
economicSystem.en: A mixed forager-pastoral economy centered on kin sharing, camp redistribution, and limited exchange.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中金库，储备主要表现为牲畜、干肉、珠饰、工具和互助义务。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves were embodied chiefly in livestock, dried meat, beads, tools, and mutual obligations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 资源分配重在减少饥荒风险、维持社会联结与确保迁徙灵活性。
fiscalPolicy.en: Resource allocation aimed primarily at reducing famine risk, maintaining social ties, and preserving mobility.

description.zh: 以弓箭、投矛和短棒为主，擅长追踪、伏击与小规模冲突。
description.en: Used bows, throwing spears, and short clubs, excelling in tracking, ambush, and small-scale combat.

description.zh: 在水源地、营地和牲畜附近承担警戒与护卫任务。
description.en: Provided watch and protection near water points, camps, and livestock.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一统帅，由各游群首领与资深猎手临时指挥
commanderInChief.en: No single commander; direction was given ad hoc by headmen and senior hunters

technology.zh: 军事技术以轻型弓箭、毒箭、追踪术和地形熟悉见长，但缺乏金属铠甲与攻城能力。
technology.en: Military technology emphasized light bows, poison arrows, tracking, and terrain familiarity, but lacked metal armor and siege capacity.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要压力来自邻近群体竞争、水源稀缺、牲畜争夺以及更强势农牧社群的边缘扩张。
threats.en: Main pressures came from neighboring competition, water scarcity, livestock raiding, and the frontier expansion of stronger agro-pastoral communities.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布于南部非洲干旱和半干旱地带，多为小型营地和季节性聚群。不存在真正城市，只有临时会聚中心与较大的牧猎营地。
populationDescription.en: Population was sparsely distributed across arid and semi-arid southern Africa, mostly in small camps and seasonal aggregations. There were no true cities, only temporary meeting centers and somewhat larger herding-foraging camps.

socialClasses.zh: 首领与长老、熟练猎手和牧者、普通家户成员、青年与依附亲属；总体分层较弱。
socialClasses.en: Headmen and elders, skilled hunters and herders, ordinary household members, youths, and dependent kin; overall stratification was weak.

allies.zh: 与通婚对象、交换伙伴和相邻友好营群维持松散联盟。
allies.en: Maintained loose alliances with marriage partners, exchange counterparts, and neighboring friendly bands.

enemies.zh: 敌对多表现为局部水源与牲畜纠纷，也面临邻近扩张群体的压力。
enemies.en: Hostility usually took the form of local disputes over water and livestock, alongside pressure from expanding neighboring groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 其对外关系以避免冲突、确保水源通行、维系婚姻交换和控制报复循环为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on avoiding conflict, ensuring access to water, maintaining marriage exchange, and limiting cycles of retaliation.

era.zh: 13世纪南部非洲采猎—牧养社会技术阶段
era.en: 13th-century southern African forager-pastoral technological stage

keyInnovations.zh: 毒箭技术、精细追踪术、干旱环境水源知识、鸵鸟蛋壳珠加工和轻便迁徙装备尤具代表性。
keyInnovations.en: Representative innovations included poison-arrow technology, fine tracking skills, arid-zone water knowledge, ostrich eggshell beadworking, and lightweight mobile equipment.

strengths.zh: 机动性高、环境知识深厚、社会弹性强，适应干旱地区能力突出。
strengths.en: They were highly mobile, possessed deep environmental knowledge, and had strong social flexibility, making them notably well adapted to dry regions.

weaknesses.zh: 人口稀少、组织分散、物资储备有限，难以抵抗更大规模农牧社会的持续挤压。
weaknesses.en: Sparse population, dispersed organization, and limited material reserves made sustained resistance to larger agro-pastoral societies difficult.

outlook.zh: 在1280年前后仍能维持区域性稳定生存，但长期将承受来自更强邻群与生态波动的双重压力。
outlook.en: Around 1280 they could still maintain stable regional survival, but in the long run they would face dual pressures from stronger neighbors and ecological fluctuation.

description.zh: 1280年的科伊桑诸群体是南部非洲分散而坚韧的采猎—牧养社会，以高度机动性和精深环境知识维系生存。
description.en: In 1280, the Khoisan peoples were dispersed yet resilient forager-pastoral societies of southern Africa, sustaining themselves through high mobility and deep environmental knowledge.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，诸草原部族联盟
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; confederated savanna tribal communities

governmentForm.zh: 以部族、氏族与长老会议为核心的分散性部落治理体系，战时由有威望的首领临时协调。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal order centered on clans and councils of elders, with respected leaders coordinating temporarily in times of war.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以亲族、年龄等级和氏族归属为组织基础，村落与季节性营地共同构成生活网络。多数人口从事采集、狩猎、渔猎与小规模园圃耕作，劳作按性别、年龄与季节分工。祭司、医者与占卜者在精神生活中拥有重要地位。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around kinship, age grades, and clan affiliation, with both villages and seasonal camps forming the social landscape. Most people relied on gathering, hunting, fishing, and small-scale garden cultivation, with labor divided by gender, age, and season. Ritual specialists, healers, and diviners held important places in spiritual life.

rulingClass.zh: 统治层并非固定贵族，而是由部族长老、战时首领与宗教权威共同构成。权威主要来自年长资历、慷慨分配、狩猎战功与祖灵认可。
rulingClass.en: The ruling stratum was not a fixed nobility but a combination of elders, war leaders, and ritual authorities. Authority derived mainly from seniority, generosity in redistribution, hunting and military prestige, and perceived ancestral sanction.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常在特定家系中推举产生，但须获长老与成年成员认可。若首领失去威望，部族可转而拥戴他人。
succession.en: Leadership usually emerged from particular lineages but required recognition by elders and adult community members. If a leader lost prestige, the tribe could shift support to another candidate.

structure.zh: 政治结构松散，决策多在长老会议、氏族代表与祭仪集会中达成。跨部族事务如迁徙路线、婚盟、狩猎区与复仇调停，则由若干首领共同协商。
structure.en: The political structure was loose, with decisions usually made in councils of elders, clan representatives, and ritual gatherings. Intertribal matters such as migration routes, marriage alliances, hunting grounds, and feud settlement were negotiated jointly by several leaders.

function.zh: 处理迁徙、纠纷调解、婚姻安排与部族间协商。
function.en: Handled migration decisions, dispute mediation, marriage arrangements, and negotiations between communities.

function.zh: 主持祭祀、管理公共储粮与救济分配。
function.en: Oversaw rituals, managed communal food stores, and organized relief distribution.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理以村落长者、家族首领和营地负责人为主。不存在严格层级官僚，权力随季节性聚散而变化。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested with village elders, lineage heads, and camp leaders. There was no rigid bureaucratic hierarchy, and authority shifted with seasonal movement and settlement.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要是习惯法，以赔偿、调解、誓言与祖灵见证维系秩序。严重冲突可引发血亲复仇，但长老通常会推动和解。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly customary, maintained through compensation, mediation, oaths, and ancestral witness. Serious conflicts could provoke blood-feud, but elders usually sought reconciliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无固定货币税制，首领通过贡献猎获物、季节性劳役与公共储藏分配获得支持。战时或歉收时，部族会追加集体供给。
taxationSystem.en: There was no fixed monetary taxation; leaders were supported through contributions of game, seasonal labor, and redistribution from communal stores. In war or famine, additional collective provisioning was expected.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰

culturalAchievements.zh: 擅长口述史诗、节奏乐舞、面饰与身体装饰，以及适应热带草原生态的物候知识。其狩猎、采集与药用植物知识高度精细，构成重要文化遗产。
culturalAchievements.en: They excelled in oral epic, rhythmic dance, facial and body adornment, and environmental knowledge adapted to tropical savanna ecologies. Their expertise in hunting, gathering, and medicinal plants formed a major cultural legacy.

languageFamily.zh: 多属尼日尔-刚果语系及邻近地方语群
languageFamily.en: Mostly Niger-Congo language families and neighboring local language groups

philosophy.zh: 以万物有灵、祖灵庇佑、自然循环与群体互惠为核心观念。
philosophy.en: A worldview centered on animism, ancestral protection, natural cycles, and reciprocal obligations within the community.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土成文文字，历史、谱系与仪式知识主要靠口传与歌谣记忆。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; history, genealogy, and ritual knowledge were preserved mainly through oral tradition and song.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性采集、渔猎、狩猎和小规模农园生产为主，市场交换有限。财富多体现为食物储备、工具、牲畜与婚姻交换资源，而非金属货币。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based chiefly on subsistence gathering, fishing, hunting, and small-scale garden production, with limited market exchange. Wealth was expressed more in food reserves, tools, livestock, and marriage exchange goods than in metallic currency.

mainIndustries.zh: 采集、狩猎、渔猎、小规模园圃农业、编织、制皮
mainIndustries.en: Gathering, hunting, fishing, small-scale horticulture, weaving, leatherworking

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、蜂蜜、羽毛、染料木、药草、象牙、小牲畜
tradeGoods.en: Hides, honey, feathers, dyewood, medicinal herbs, ivory, small livestock

name.zh: 物品交换与贝饰价值体系

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富通常以储粮、工具、渔猎器具、家畜与亲族支持网络衡量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was generally measured in stored food, tools, hunting and fishing gear, livestock, and the strength of kin support networks.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家户自给、亲族互助和首领再分配为主的部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A tribal economy based on household subsistence, kin reciprocity, and chiefly redistribution.

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要表现为储粮、工具、珠饰和少量贵重交换品，而非集中金库。
treasuryDescription.en: The “treasury” consisted mainly of stored food, tools, beads, and a small stock of prestige exchange goods rather than a centralized bullion reserve.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持基本储备、灾荒救济和首领威望分配为主，避免对各部族征索过重。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining reserves, famine relief, and prestige redistribution by leaders, while avoiding excessive demands on constituent groups.

description.zh: 以矛、弓、棍棒和小型盾牌作战，擅长伏击与地形机动。
description.en: Fought with spears, bows, clubs, and small shields, excelling in ambush and terrain-based mobility.

description.zh: 负责聚居点与储藏区防卫，多由成年男子轮值。
description.en: Protected settlements and storage areas, often through rotational service by adult men.

commanderInChief.zh: 无固定统帅，由战时首领会议推举主战者
commanderInChief.en: No permanent commander; a wartime chief was selected by council

technology.zh: 军事技术较为简朴，以木矛、骨角器、弓箭和皮盾为主，依赖熟悉草原、林地与水域环境的机动能力。
technology.en: Military technology was relatively simple, centered on wooden spears, bone and horn implements, bows, and hide shields, relying on mobility and knowledge of savanna, woodland, and riverine terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族争夺猎场、水源与人口掠夺，以及旱灾引发的迁徙冲突。
threats.en: The main threats came from neighboring groups competing over hunting grounds, water, and captives, as well as migration conflicts triggered by drought.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布稀疏，集中于水源、季节性渔场和较稳定的采集区域。多数人生活在小型村落或流动营地中，人口密度低。
populationDescription.en: Population was sparse and concentrated near water sources, seasonal fisheries, and reliable gathering zones. Most people lived in small villages or mobile camps, with low overall population density.

socialClasses.zh: 长老与祭司、战士与熟练猎手、普通家户、依附者与俘获人口。
socialClasses.en: Elders and ritual specialists, warriors and skilled hunters, ordinary households, dependents, and captives.

allies.zh: 以邻近通婚与互市部族为主，联盟多随季节与资源状况变化。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring intermarrying and trading tribes, with alliances shifting according to season and resource conditions.

enemies.zh: 无长期固定外敌，但与争夺猎场、河道与人口的部族常有敌对关系。
enemies.en: There were no permanent large-scale enemies, but hostility often arose with groups competing over hunting grounds, river access, and captives.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在维持迁徙通道、婚盟平衡与交换网络，避免长期消耗战。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy emphasized preserving migration corridors, balancing marriage alliances, and maintaining exchange networks while avoiding prolonged conflict.

era.zh: 晚期石器—早期铁器接触并存的部落技术阶段
era.en: Late Stone Age to early Iron Age contact-era tribal technology

keyInnovations.zh: 弓箭狩猎、火管理、独木舟与渔网、植物药知识、季节性储藏技术。
keyInnovations.en: Bow hunting, fire management, dugout canoes and nets, botanical medicine, and seasonal storage techniques.

strengths.zh: 生态适应力强、亲族网络稳固、机动性高，能在环境波动中维持生存。
strengths.en: Strong ecological adaptability, resilient kin networks, and high mobility allowed survival under environmental fluctuation.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏统一政权与大规模储备体系，难以长期集中兵力或组织远程贸易。
weaknesses.en: The lack of unified authority and large centralized reserves limited prolonged military mobilization and long-distance trade organization.

outlook.zh: 若气候稳定并维持区域互市，可长期保持平衡；若旱灾加剧，部族冲突与人口流动将上升。
outlook.en: If climate conditions remained stable and regional exchange persisted, the society could remain balanced for a long time; if drought intensified, conflict and migration would increase.

description.zh: 一个以采集、渔猎和亲族协作为基础、由长老与祭司维系秩序的热带草原部族世界。
description.en: A savanna tribal world rooted in gathering, fishing, hunting, and kin cooperation, with order maintained by elders and ritual specialists.

dynasty.zh: 无统一王朝，潘帕斯诸游群
dynasty.en: No unified dynasty; the Pampas bands and tribes

governmentForm.zh: 以游群首领、亲族长者和战士声望为核心的松散部落组织。
governmentForm.en: A loose tribal organization centered on band leaders, kin elders, and the prestige of warriors.

socialStructure.zh: 社会主要由小型流动群体组成，依靠狩猎、采集与季节性聚会维持联系。家庭与亲族是基本单位，婚姻交换在群体间建立联盟。具有狩猎能力与慷慨分配能力者往往享有更高声望。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted mainly of small mobile bands connected by hunting, gathering, and seasonal assemblies. The family and kin group were the basic units, while marriage exchange created alliances between bands. Those with strong hunting ability and generous redistributive habits often enjoyed higher prestige.

rulingClass.zh: 并无制度化贵族，领袖多为善于狩猎、作战和调解纠纷的首领。萨满或祭仪人物在精神与治病层面具有影响力。
rulingClass.en: There was no institutionalized nobility; leaders were typically skilled hunters, fighters, and mediators. Shamans or ritual figures held influence in spiritual and healing matters.

succession.zh: 首领职位通常不严格世袭，而是在若干有声望家系中选择。若首领无法保护群体或分配猎获，其追随者可能转投他人。
succession.en: Leadership was not strictly hereditary and usually emerged from a few prestigious families. If a leader failed to protect the band or redistribute game, followers could move to another leader.

structure.zh: 治理方式极度分散，决策依靠首领协商、家长会议与仪式集会。大多数事务在游群内部解决，跨群合作多限于婚盟、狩猎和防御。
structure.en: Government was highly decentralized, with decisions made through chief consultation, household councils, and ritual gatherings. Most affairs were handled within each band, and cooperation beyond the band was usually limited to marriage, hunting, and defense.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、狩猎地使用和群体间争端。
function.en: Coordinated migration, hunting-ground use, and disputes between groups.

function.zh: 主持季节性集会、祭礼和猎获物分享。
function.en: Managed seasonal assemblies, ritual observances, and the sharing of hunting yields.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政基本不存在固定机构，每个游群由一至数名首领和长者管理。营地随资源移动，行政权也随之流动。
localAdmin.en: There was essentially no fixed local administration; each band was managed by one or several leaders and elders. As camps moved with resources, authority moved with them.

legalSystem.zh: 习惯法建立在亲族责任、赔偿、婚姻安排与首领调解上。严重暴力事件有时以决斗、分离迁徙或报复解决。
legalSystem.en: Customary law rested on kin responsibility, compensation, marriage arrangements, and chiefly mediation. Serious violence was sometimes resolved through duel, separation of bands, or retaliation.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，首领通过猎获物分成、礼物与追随者劳务维持地位。大型集会时，各家户需贡献食物与物资。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders were supported through shares of game, gifts, and follower labor. During large gatherings, households were expected to contribute food and supplies.

religion.zh: 万物有灵信仰

culturalAchievements.zh: 形成适应草原环境的追猎技术、皮革加工、披衣与帐幕制作，并保存丰富的口头传统。季节性集会兼具婚配、交换与宗教功能，是文化凝聚的重要机制。
culturalAchievements.en: They developed pursuit-hunting techniques suited to grassland environments, leatherworking, cloaks and tent-making, and rich oral traditions. Seasonal gatherings combined marriage exchange, trade, and religion, serving as a key mechanism of cultural cohesion.

languageFamily.zh: 多为南美南锥体地方语群，含前马普切与潘帕斯相关语支
languageFamily.en: Local Southern Cone language groups, including pre-Mapuche and Pampas-related branches

philosophy.zh: 崇尚自然灵力、祖先记忆、梦兆与勇武荣誉。
philosophy.en: A worldview valuing natural spiritual forces, ancestral memory, dreams, and martial honor.

writingSystem.zh: 无文字，传统依靠口述叙事、歌唱、纹饰与仪式传承。
writingSystem.en: No writing system; traditions were transmitted through oral narratives, song, body design, and ritual.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以狩猎、采集和低强度交换为主，物质积累有限。财富主要体现为皮革、工具、食物储备与婚姻网络，而非持续性市场收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on hunting, gathering, and low-intensity exchange, with limited material accumulation. Wealth lay mainly in hides, tools, food stores, and marriage networks rather than steady market income.

mainIndustries.zh: 狩猎、采集、制皮、骨角器制作、季节性交换
mainIndustries.en: Hunting, gathering, leatherworking, bone and horn craft, seasonal exchange

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、羽毛、石器、骨器、颜料、干肉
tradeGoods.en: Hides, feathers, stone tools, bone implements, pigments, dried meat

householdWealth.zh: 家户财富多以皮张数量、工具质量、配偶联盟和可迁徙装备衡量。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was measured largely by the number of hides, quality of tools, marriage alliances, and portable equipment.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以流动家户生产、互赠与季节性物品交换为主的草原部落经济。
economicSystem.en: A grassland tribal economy based on mobile household production, gift exchange, and seasonal barter.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财库规模很小，主要是皮张、颜料、骨器、干肉和少量珍贵石料等可携带财物。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was very small, consisting mainly of portable wealth such as hides, pigments, bone items, dried meat, and a small quantity of valued stone materials.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政强调轻征与共享，维持首领权威所需的馈赠能力比积累储金更重要。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized light extraction and sharing; the ability of leaders to give gifts mattered more than accumulating stored bullion.

description.zh: 以步行为主，使用投枪、长矛、弓箭和石制武器，擅长快速袭扰。
description.en: Primarily foot-based fighters using throwing spears, lances, bows, and stone weapons, skilled in rapid raids.

description.zh: 负责保护妇孺、帐幕和储备物资，多由成年成员临时承担。
description.en: Protected families, tents, and stored provisions, usually through temporary service by adult members.

commanderInChief.zh: 无常设总指挥，由最具威望首领统率联队
commanderInChief.en: No standing supreme commander; coalition forces followed the most prestigious chief

technology.zh: 军事技术处于步行猎战阶段，未形成骑兵传统，装备以木、石、骨和皮革制品为主。
technology.en: Military technology remained in a pedestrian hunting-war stage, without a cavalry tradition; equipment was mainly made of wood, stone, bone, and leather.

threats.zh: 威胁主要来自邻近草原群体对猎场、河岸与季节营地的竞争，以及偶发性复仇袭击。
threats.en: Threats came chiefly from neighboring grassland groups competing over hunting grounds, riverbanks, and seasonal camps, as well as episodic revenge raids.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口规模较小，分散在广阔草原与河谷地带。聚居点多为临时或半固定营地，季节迁徙频繁。
populationDescription.en: The population was relatively small and dispersed across broad grasslands and river valleys. Settlements were mostly temporary or semi-fixed camps, with frequent seasonal mobility.

socialClasses.zh: 首领与长者、萨满与医者、成年战士与猎手、普通家户、俘获依附者。
socialClasses.en: Chiefs and elders, shamans and healers, adult warriors and hunters, ordinary households, and captive dependents.

allies.zh: 主要是通过婚配、会盟和交换维系的邻近游群。
allies.en: Allies were mainly neighboring bands linked through marriage, assemblies, and exchange.

enemies.zh: 无恒久国家级敌人，但与竞争资源的部族常有周期性冲突。
enemies.en: There were no permanent state-scale enemies, but recurring conflicts occurred with tribes competing for resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系注重维持迁徙空间与互不干扰的狩猎边界，必要时通过结盟共同防御。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on preserving mobility space and nonintrusive hunting boundaries, with alliances formed when necessary for common defense.

era.zh: 前金属或低金属使用的草原部落技术阶段
era.en: A grassland tribal technological stage with little or no metallurgy

keyInnovations.zh: 群体围猎、皮革加工、石骨复合工具、季节性营地组织、口头导航知识。
keyInnovations.en: Group drive hunting, leatherworking, composite stone-and-bone tools, seasonal camp organization, and oral navigation knowledge.

strengths.zh: 机动灵活、适应草原环境、社会成本低，能迅速重组游群。
strengths.en: Highly mobile, well adapted to grassland conditions, and socially flexible, the bands could reorganize quickly.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、组织分散、物资积累有限，不利于长期战争或大型工程。
weaknesses.en: Small population, fragmented organization, and limited material accumulation hindered prolonged war or large-scale construction.

outlook.zh: 在生态资源尚可的情况下可以长期延续，但一旦遭遇气候波动或外来更强势群体，脆弱性较高。
outlook.en: The society could endure for long periods under stable ecological conditions, but it was vulnerable to climatic shocks or intrusion by stronger external groups.

description.zh: 一个分散流动、以狩猎采集和亲族会盟维持生存与秩序的潘帕斯草原部族社会。
description.en: A dispersed and mobile Pampas grassland tribal society sustained by hunting, gathering, and kin-based alliances.

governmentForm.zh: 以神圣君主为中心、由贵族与地方酋长共同支撑的海洋王国。
governmentForm.en: A maritime kingdom centered on a sacred monarch and supported by nobles and local chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 汤加社会等级分明，王族、贵族、酋长、自由民与依附劳作者之间有清晰差序。血缘、神圣性与馈赠关系共同决定地位，女性在高贵血统传承中亦具重要象征意义。群岛之间通过朝贡、婚姻与航海联系维系整体秩序。
socialStructure.en: Tongan society was strongly stratified, with clear distinctions among the royal line, nobles, chiefs, commoners, and dependent laborers. Status was shaped by lineage, sacred prestige, and gift relations, while women also held important symbolic value in transmitting high-ranking bloodlines. Order across the islands was maintained through tribute, marriage, and seafaring links.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层包括图伊汤加王室、高级贵族与地方酋长，他们控制土地分配、贡赋征收与远洋动员。宗教仪式权威与世俗征调权在上层高度结合。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class comprised the Tu'i Tonga royal house, high nobles, and local chiefs, who controlled land allocation, tribute extraction, and oceanic mobilization. Ritual authority and secular command were closely intertwined at the top.

succession.zh: 王位通常在王族核心支系内继承，但需贵族与宗教传统认可。地方酋长职位多具世袭性，同时受中央王室关系网络制约。
succession.en: The throne usually passed within the core royal lineage, but required recognition under noble and ritual conventions. Local chiefships were often hereditary as well, though constrained by ties to the central royal house.

structure.zh: 王国以穆阿为礼仪与政治中心，由王室近臣、高级贵族和岛屿酋长构成统治网络。中央对外岛的控制并非统一官僚制，而是通过朝贡、婚盟、祭祀义务与航海联系实现。
structure.en: The kingdom centered politically and ritually on Mu'a, governed through a network of royal attendants, high nobles, and island chiefs. Central control over outer islands did not rest on a uniform bureaucracy but on tribute, marriage alliances, ritual obligations, and seafaring connections.

function.zh: 主持王室祭祀、陵墓礼仪、等级次序与重要典礼。
function.en: Oversaw royal worship, tomb rites, rank order, and major ceremonies.

function.zh: 征收外岛贡品、协调酋长义务并安排再分配。
function.en: Collected tribute from outer islands, coordinated chiefly obligations, and organized redistribution.

function.zh: 组织独木舟建造、远航联络、公共工程与战时动员。
function.en: Managed canoe construction, long-distance communication, public works, and wartime mobilization.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由各岛酋长与其家臣管理，负责土地、劳役和贡品征集。中央通常不直接介入村社细务，而通过酋长层级施加影响。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was carried out by island chiefs and their retainers, who managed land, labor, and tribute collection. The center generally did not intervene directly in village routine, but exerted influence through the chiefly hierarchy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要基于习惯、王命与神圣禁忌，社会秩序依赖等级礼仪和首领裁断。对土地、婚姻与冒犯尊贵者的案件尤为重视。
legalSystem.en: Law was grounded mainly in custom, royal command, and sacred taboo, with social order enforced through rank etiquette and chiefly judgment. Cases involving land, marriage, and offenses against high-ranking persons were especially important.

taxationSystem.zh: 税赋以实物贡纳和劳役为主，包括食物、细席、鲸齿、贝饰、木材与独木舟服务。财政征收与仪式性馈赠紧密相连。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation consisted mainly of tribute in goods and labor, including food, fine mats, whale teeth, shell valuables, timber, and canoe service. Fiscal extraction was closely tied to ceremonial gift exchange.

religion.zh: 波利尼西亚多神教

culturalAchievements.zh: 汤加在远洋航海、独木舟制造、石构陵墓与精细席垫织造方面成就突出。其跨岛礼仪网络与谱系传统，使王权在广阔海域中保持文化影响力。
culturalAchievements.en: Tonga excelled in oceanic navigation, canoe building, stone tomb construction, and fine mat weaving. Its inter-island ritual network and genealogical tradition gave royal power cultural reach across a wide maritime zone.

languageFamily.zh: 南岛语系—波利尼西亚语支（汤加语）
languageFamily.en: Austronesian, Polynesian branch (Tongan)

philosophy.zh: 以波利尼西亚多神信仰、祖先神圣性、等级秩序与互惠馈赠伦理为核心。
philosophy.en: A worldview centered on Polynesian polytheism, ancestral sacredness, social hierarchy, and the ethics of reciprocal gift exchange.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，王系谱牒、航海知识和礼仪规范主要靠口传记忆。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; royal genealogies, navigational knowledge, and ritual norms were preserved through oral memory.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以芋头、山药、面包果、椰子等农业与渔业为基础，并依赖王室组织的贡赋再分配。跨岛交换活跃，财富常以食物盈余、细席、贝饰与船只能力体现。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture and fishing, including taro, yams, breadfruit, and coconuts, and depended heavily on royal tribute redistribution. Inter-island exchange was active, and wealth was often expressed in food surplus, fine mats, shell valuables, and seafaring capacity.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、渔业、席垫织造、独木舟制造、石构工程、贝饰工艺
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, fishing, mat weaving, canoe construction, stoneworks, shell ornament craft

tradeGoods.zh: 细席、树皮布、贝饰、鲸齿、木材、干鱼、椰油、工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Fine mats, barkcloth, shell valuables, whale teeth, timber, dried fish, coconut oil, crafted goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要由土地使用权、作物产量、席垫与树皮布储备、船只和贵重礼品决定。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended mainly on land-use rights, crop yields, stores of mats and barkcloth, canoes, and valuable ceremonial gifts.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year

economicSystem.zh: 以王室朝贡、地方生产和跨岛再分配结合的海洋王国经济。
economicSystem.en: A maritime kingdom economy combining royal tribute, local production, and inter-island redistribution.

unit.en: taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
taels of silver equivalent per year
