银里弗尔图尔努瓦等值

treasuryDescription.zh: 公爵金库相对稳健，但受封臣忠诚、法王压力和沿海贸易波动影响较大。
treasuryDescription.en: The ducal treasury was relatively solid, but it was highly affected by vassal loyalty, pressure from the French crown, and fluctuations in coastal trade.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政上倾向保护公爵领地收益、维持港口税源，并在必要时通过临时征收筹措军费。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored safeguarding ducal domain revenues, preserving port-based income, and raising military funds through extraordinary levies when necessary.

description.zh: 由公爵直属骑士和地方封臣提供，是机动打击核心。
description.en: Supplied by the duke and local vassals, they formed the core mobile striking force.

description.zh: 由乡村和城镇征发的矛兵、弩手和杂役组成，适合防御和围城。
description.en: Made up of spearmen, crossbowmen, and support personnel raised from countryside and towns, suitable for defense and siege work.

description.zh: 用于护渔、运输和近岸巡逻，战斗力有限但具地方价值。
description.en: Used for fishery protection, transport, and nearshore patrols, with limited combat value but clear local utility.

commanderInChief.zh: 布列塔尼公爵约翰二世

technology.zh: 军事技术与13世纪法兰西相近，以重骑兵、长矛步兵、弩和石制城堡体系为主。
technology.en: Military technology was broadly similar to that of thirteenth-century France, centered on heavy cavalry, spear infantry, crossbows, and stone castle systems.

unit.zh: 银里弗尔图尔努瓦等值/年

threats.zh: 主要风险来自法王加强宗主控制、邻近封建冲突以及海峡沿岸的海盗和海上竞争。
threats.en: The main risks came from stronger royal French suzerain control, neighboring feudal conflicts, and piracy and maritime competition along the Channel coast.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于乡村，西部布列塔尼语区与东部加洛语区并存。城市化水平不高，但港口与主教座堂城市是区域经济和宗教中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly rural, with western Breton-speaking and eastern Gallo-speaking zones coexisting. Urbanization was limited, but ports and cathedral towns served as regional economic and religious centers.

socialClasses.zh: 公爵与大贵族、骑士和教士、城市商人与工匠、自由农、佃农与附庸农。
socialClasses.en: The duke and high nobility, knights and clergy, urban merchants and craftsmen, free peasants, tenants, and dependent cultivators.

allies.zh: 与法国王室保持名义臣属关系，并通过婚姻和贸易同英吉利海峡两岸势力往来。
allies.en: It maintained a nominal subordinate relationship with the French crown and cultivated ties across the Channel through marriage and trade.

enemies.zh: 无固定宿敌，但与法王、邻近封建领主或海上掠夺者可能发生紧张关系。
enemies.en: It had no permanent archenemy, but could face tensions with the French king, neighboring feudal lords, or maritime raiders.

foreignPolicy.zh: 其外交重心在于维护自治、平衡法王影响，并利用海上联系扩大贸易利益。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy focused on preserving autonomy, balancing royal French influence, and leveraging maritime links to expand commercial interests.

era.zh: 13世纪西欧封建—城市发展阶段
era.en: Thirteenth-century Western European feudal-urban developmental stage

keyInnovations.zh: 石造防御工事、弩、轮作农业、盐田生产技术、海岸航运经验。
keyInnovations.en: Stone fortifications, crossbows, crop rotation, salt-pan production techniques, and coastal navigation experience.

strengths.zh: 地理边缘性赋予其较强自治空间，农业与海洋资源稳定，地方认同鲜明。
strengths.en: Its peripheral geography gave it room for autonomy, its agrarian and maritime resources were steady, and local identity was strong.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权有限，地方贵族势力强，财政和军力在大国竞争中偏弱。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was limited, local nobles were powerful, and its finances and military were comparatively weak in great-power competition.

outlook.zh: 短期内布列塔尼可保持稳定，但长期将不断面对法国王权整合带来的政治压力。
outlook.en: In the short term Brittany could remain stable, but in the long run it would face mounting political pressure from the consolidation of French royal power.

description.zh: 1280年前后的布列塔尼是一个以封建自治、海岸经济和地方文化传统著称的法兰西西部公国。
description.en: Around 1280, Brittany was a western French principality noted for feudal autonomy, a coastal economy, and strong local cultural traditions.

dynasty.zh: 科穆宁王朝（大科穆宁家族）

governmentForm.zh: 这是一个以皇帝为核心、兼具拜占庭官僚传统与地方贵族色彩的希腊化君主制帝国。
governmentForm.en: This was a Greek imperial monarchy centered on the emperor, combining Byzantine bureaucratic traditions with strong local aristocratic features.

socialStructure.zh: 特拉比松社会由皇室、军政贵族与高级教士居于顶层，城市商人与手工业者构成重要中间群体。农村人口多为希腊语或拉兹、亚美尼亚背景的农民和牧民，山区地方势力保留较强自治性。黑海贸易吸引了热那亚、威尼斯及高加索商人长期活动。
socialStructure.en: Trebizondian society was headed by the imperial family, military-administrative aristocracy, and senior clergy, while urban merchants and artisans formed an important middle stratum. The rural population included Greek-speaking cultivators as well as Laz and Armenian peasants and pastoralists, with upland districts retaining considerable local autonomy. Black Sea trade drew sustained activity from Genoese, Venetian, and Caucasian merchants.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要由大科穆宁皇室、宫廷官员、军区贵族和东正教主教团组成。他们依靠土地、税权、海港收入和宫廷任命维系统治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted chiefly of the Grand Komnenian imperial family, court officials, military aristocrats, and the Orthodox episcopate. They sustained rule through landed resources, fiscal rights, port revenues, and imperial appointments.

succession.zh: 皇位原则上由科穆宁王朝世袭，但宫廷政变、婚姻联盟和贵族支持常对实际继承造成影响。皇族内部竞争是政治不稳的重要潜在因素。
succession.en: The throne was in principle hereditary within the Komnenos dynasty, but palace intrigue, marriage alliances, and aristocratic backing often affected actual succession. Rivalry within the imperial house was a major latent source of instability.

structure.zh: 帝国政府沿袭拜占庭式宫廷结构，由皇帝、宫廷大臣、财政与军事官员共同运作。中央统治在特拉比松沿海地带较有效，对内陆山区和边远封邑则更多依赖地方贵族与附庸关系。
structure.en: The empire retained a Byzantine-style court structure operated by the emperor together with high ministers and fiscal and military officials. Central rule was more effective along the coast around Trebizond, while the interior mountains and remote lordships relied more on local aristocrats and dependent ties.

function.zh: 起草敕令、管理宫廷礼仪、记录任命并处理皇帝文书。
function.en: Drafted decrees, managed court ceremonial, recorded appointments, and handled imperial documentation.

function.zh: 征收关税、地租、市场税并管理黑海贸易收益。
function.en: Collected customs, rents, market dues, and managed revenues from Black Sea trade.

function.zh: 组织宫廷卫队、边防驻军、雇佣兵与地方军役。
function.en: Organized the court guard, frontier garrisons, mercenaries, and local military obligations.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托城镇总督、边区军事长官和地方豪族。山区与边境区域的治理往往建立在个人忠诚、联姻和附庸协定之上，而非严密官僚渗透。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on town governors, frontier military commanders, and regional magnates. Governance in mountain and border zones often rested on personal loyalty, marriage alliances, and dependent agreements rather than dense bureaucratic penetration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系承袭拜占庭罗马法传统，并受东正教教会法和地方习惯法补充。商业案件尤其重视港口惯例和跨文化交易安排。
legalSystem.en: The legal system drew on the Byzantine Roman-law tradition, supplemented by Orthodox canon law and local custom. Commercial cases especially relied on port usages and cross-cultural transactional arrangements.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、土地税、市场税和附庸贡赋为主。由于贸易地位重要，关税收入在财政中占比尤高。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based mainly on port customs, land taxes, market dues, and tribute from dependent communities. Because of the empire’s trading position, customs revenues formed an especially large part of state finance.

religion.zh: 东正教

culturalAchievements.zh: 特拉比松保存了拜占庭晚期的宫廷礼仪、宗教艺术与历史记述传统。其教堂壁画、手抄本和贸易都市文化使其在黑海世界中具有鲜明特色。
culturalAchievements.en: Trebizond preserved late Byzantine court ceremony, religious art, and historical writing traditions. Its church frescoes, manuscripts, and trading-urban culture gave it a distinctive place in the Black Sea world.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系：希腊语支为主，并有亚美尼亚语、格鲁吉亚语及高加索语言接触。
languageFamily.en: Indo-European: primarily Greek, with Armenian, Georgian, and Caucasian language contact.

philosophy.zh: 以东正教神学、拜占庭皇权观和黑海边疆多民族文化交融为核心。
philosophy.en: Its core lay in Orthodox theology, Byzantine concepts of imperial rule, and the cultural blending of the Black Sea frontier.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用希腊字母，行政和宗教文书以中世纪希腊语为主，并接触亚美尼亚文、格鲁吉亚文和意大利商人文书。
writingSystem.en: The Greek alphabet predominated, with medieval Greek used in administration and religion, alongside contacts with Armenian, Georgian, and Italian mercantile writing.

unit.zh: 银海佩尔庇伦等值/人/年

gdpDescription.zh: 特拉比松经济规模不大，但因掌握黑海东南岸转口贸易而相对富裕。其财富更多来自商税、港口服务和丝绸香料转运，而非广阔农业腹地。
gdpDescription.en: Trebizond’s economy was not large, but it was relatively wealthy because it controlled entrepôt trade on the southeastern Black Sea coast. Its wealth came more from customs, port services, and the transit of silk and spices than from an extensive agrarian hinterland.

mainIndustries.zh: 港口贸易、航运、园艺农业、葡萄酒、畜牧、手工业
mainIndustries.en: port trade, shipping, horticulture, wine, pastoralism, handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 丝绸、香料转口、蜡、毛皮、葡萄酒、坚果、金属制品
tradeGoods.en: silk, re-exported spices, wax, furs, wine, nuts, metal goods

name.zh: 海佩尔庇伦与地方银币

householdWealth.zh: 港口商人、税农和宫廷贵族家庭财富较高，山区农牧民和普通城镇居民则多处于中低水平。
householdWealth.en: Port merchants, tax farmers, and courtly aristocratic households were relatively wealthy, while mountain peasants, pastoralists, and ordinary townspeople mostly occupied lower to middling levels.

unit.zh: 银海佩尔庇伦等值/劳动者/年

economicSystem.zh: 以拜占庭式税收国家和港口中介贸易相结合的区域经济体系。
economicSystem.en: A regional economy combining a Byzantine-style fiscal state with intermediary port trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 帝国金库依赖贸易繁荣，现金流相对灵活，但若商路受阻或边境冲突升级，财政抗压能力有限。
treasuryDescription.en: The imperial treasury depended on commercial prosperity and thus had relatively flexible cash flow, but its resilience was limited if trade routes were disrupted or border warfare intensified.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策重在保障关税收入、维护商人信心，并以有限土地税支撑宫廷与防务开支。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on securing customs revenues, maintaining merchant confidence, and using limited land taxation to support court and defense spending.

description.zh: 由皇帝直属近卫和贵族骑兵组成，是帝国机动作战骨干。
description.en: Composed of the emperor’s guard and aristocratic cavalry, they were the backbone of mobile operations.

description.zh: 驻守沿海城镇、山地通道和边境据点，承担防御与治安职责。
description.en: Stationed in coastal towns, mountain passes, and frontier strongpoints, they handled defense and security duties.

description.zh: 规模较小，主要用于港口防卫、护航和近岸行动。
description.en: Small in scale, they were used mainly for harbor defense, convoy protection, and near-coastal action.

commanderInChief.zh: 特拉比松皇帝约翰二世

technology.zh: 军事技术承续晚期拜占庭传统，兼用骑兵、弓弩、山地要塞和有限海军力量。
technology.en: Military technology continued late Byzantine traditions, using cavalry, bows and crossbows, mountain fortresses, and limited naval forces.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自安纳托利亚突厥诸势力、黑海海上竞争，以及高加索与内陆边疆的不稳定。
threats.en: Major threats came from Turkic powers in Anatolia, Black Sea maritime competition, and instability along the Caucasian and inland frontiers.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于特拉比松及沿海带，内陆山地分布较疏。民族与语言构成复杂，以希腊人居主导，同时有拉兹人、亚美尼亚人、格鲁吉亚人及外来商人。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in Trebizond and the coastal belt, while the inland mountains were more sparsely settled. Ethnic and linguistic composition was complex, led by Greeks alongside Laz, Armenians, Georgians, and foreign merchants.

socialClasses.zh: 皇室与军政贵族、东正教教士、城市商人与工匠、农民与牧民、边疆附庸群体。
socialClasses.en: The imperial family and military-administrative aristocracy, Orthodox clergy, urban merchants and artisans, peasants and pastoralists, and frontier dependent groups.

allies.zh: 与部分格鲁吉亚和高加索势力保持联姻或合作，并与意大利商人维持务实关系。
allies.en: It maintained marital or cooperative ties with certain Georgian and Caucasian powers, and pragmatic relations with Italian merchants.

enemies.zh: 常见对手包括安纳托利亚突厥政权、地区性海上竞争者及潜在的内部贵族反对派。
enemies.en: Frequent adversaries included Turkic Anatolian polities, regional maritime rivals, and potential internal aristocratic opposition.

foreignPolicy.zh: 其外交目标是通过贸易让利、王室婚姻与边疆妥协来维持黑海口岸帝国的独立。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy aimed to preserve the independence of a Black Sea port empire through trade concessions, dynastic marriages, and frontier compromise.

era.zh: 晚期拜占庭—黑海贸易时代
era.en: Late Byzantine–Black Sea commercial era

keyInnovations.zh: 港口税务管理、黑海远程贸易网络、山地要塞防御、手抄本和宗教艺术传统。
keyInnovations.en: Port-based fiscal management, long-distance Black Sea trade networks, mountain-fortress defense, and manuscript and religious art traditions.

strengths.zh: 港口贸易地位优越、宫廷传统完备、地形利于防御，是其核心优势。
strengths.en: Its core strengths were its favorable port-trade position, developed court tradition, and terrain well suited for defense.

weaknesses.zh: 腹地狭小、兵源有限、对贸易通道和贵族忠诚依赖过深，是帝国长期弱点。
weaknesses.en: Its long-term weaknesses were a narrow hinterland, limited manpower, and deep dependence on trade corridors and aristocratic loyalty.

outlook.zh: 若黑海贸易持续繁荣且边疆局势可控，特拉比松仍能维持稳定；但一旦外部军事压力上升，其独立性将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: If Black Sea trade remained prosperous and the frontier manageable, Trebizond could stay stable; but any rise in external military pressure would seriously test its independence.

description.zh: 1280年前后的特拉比松帝国是一个依靠黑海贸易、拜占庭宫廷传统和山海防御维系生存的希腊化小帝国。
description.en: Around 1280, the Empire of Trebizond was a small Hellenic empire sustained by Black Sea trade, Byzantine court traditions, and a defensive mountain-and-sea setting.

dynasty.zh: 萨丁尼亚诸朱迪卡托与比萨—阿拉贡过渡时期
dynasty.en: Judicati of Sardinia in the Pisan–Aragonese transitional era

governmentForm.zh: 1280年前后的萨丁尼亚并非统一王国，而是由数个朱迪卡托政权、比萨与热那亚势力共同影响的分裂君主制岛屿政体。
governmentForm.en: Around 1280 Sardinia was not a unified kingdom but a divided insular polity shaped by several giudicati and by the competing influence of Pisa and Genoa.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以乡村农民、牧民和小城镇居民为主体，贵族、教会与自由民共同构成地方社会骨架。修道院、主教区与地主家族在土地控制和司法中占据重要位置。沿海港市与内陆村社之间存在明显差异，前者更深受意大利海上共和国影响。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on rural peasants, pastoralists, and small-town inhabitants, with nobles, clergy, and free commoners forming the main local framework. Monasteries, bishoprics, and landed families held major roles in land control and justice. A clear divide existed between coastal ports, more influenced by the Italian maritime republics, and inland village communities.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由朱迪卡托王室、地方贵族、主教与意大利商人集团组成。比萨人和热那亚人常通过港口、堡垒和婚姻关系介入岛上政治。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of giudical dynasties, local nobles, bishops, and Italian merchant interests. Pisans and Genoese often intervened in island politics through ports, fortresses, and marriage alliances.

succession.zh: 继承通常依王室家族血统与贵族承认而定，但常因外部势力支持不同继承人而发生争议。女性继承与婚姻联姻在萨丁尼亚政治中也具有实际重要性。
succession.en: Succession generally followed dynastic bloodline and noble recognition, though disputes were frequent when outside powers backed rival claimants. Female inheritance and marriage alliances also had real political significance in Sardinia.

structure.zh: 岛上行政高度分散，各朱迪卡托保有自己的宫廷、法庭与土地管理体系。沿海城市和关键港口往往受比萨或热那亚顾问、商人和驻军影响。教会行政与世俗政权交织，是治理的重要组成部分。
structure.en: Administration on the island was highly decentralized, with each giudicato maintaining its own court, tribunals, and land-management system. Coastal cities and strategic ports were often influenced by Pisan or Genoese advisers, merchants, and garrisons. Ecclesiastical administration overlapped with secular power and formed a major part of governance.

function.zh: 处理君主命令、贵族纠纷、土地诉讼与刑事审判。
function.en: Handled princely orders, noble disputes, land suits, and criminal judgments.

function.zh: 登记庄园、征收实物地租、监督农牧产出与港口课税。
function.en: Registered estates, collected rents in kind, and supervised agrarian output and port dues.

function.zh: 管理海港、盐场、航运税与对比萨、热那亚商人的许可。
function.en: Managed harbors, saltworks, shipping taxes, and licenses for Pisan and Genoese merchants.

function.zh: 与主教区、修道院协调征收什一税、婚姻法与地方仲裁。
function.en: Coordinated with bishoprics and monasteries on tithes, marriage law, and local arbitration.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以各地区领主、村社首领和教区神职人员为基础。山区与内陆地区更依赖习惯法和家族权威，沿海地区则更受文书行政影响。
localAdmin.en: Local administration rested on regional lords, village heads, and parish clergy. Mountainous and inland zones relied more on customary law and kin authority, while coastal districts were more shaped by documentary administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律兼具萨丁尼亚地方习惯法、拉丁教会法与意大利城市法影响。土地、婚姻和继承案件常由教俗双方共同裁断。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Sardinian customary rules with Latin canon law and influences from Italian urban law. Cases concerning land, marriage, and inheritance were often judged through both secular and ecclesiastical channels.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠地租、港口税、盐税和什一税性质的教会征收。许多负担以谷物、奶酪、牲畜和劳役形式缴纳，而非完全以货币支付。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income relied chiefly on rents, port dues, salt taxes, and church-like tithe extractions. Many obligations were paid in grain, cheese, livestock, and labor rather than entirely in coin.

religion.zh: 天主教

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨丁尼亚在中世纪以教会建筑、修道院网络和具有地方特色的法律文书传统著称。比萨罗马式建筑风格在岛上多处主教座堂和教堂中留下明显印记。
culturalAchievements.en: Medieval Sardinia was noted for its ecclesiastical architecture, monastic networks, and distinctive documentary legal traditions. Pisan Romanesque style left a visible mark on many cathedrals and churches across the island.

languageFamily.zh: 罗曼语族，以萨丁尼亚语诸方言为主，兼用拉丁文与意大利城邦语言
languageFamily.en: Romance family, primarily Sardinian dialects, alongside Latin and Italian urban languages

philosophy.zh: 拉丁基督教世界观与地方习惯传统并存
philosophy.en: Latin Christian worldview combined with local customary tradition

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母，用于拉丁文、萨丁尼亚地方文书及意大利语文书
writingSystem.en: Latin script, used for Latin, Sardinian documentary usage, and Italian-language records

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以谷物种植、放牧、盐业和沿海贸易为主，整体规模中等偏小。岛屿资源并不贫乏，但政治分裂和外部干涉限制了财富集中。
gdpDescription.en: The economy relied on grain cultivation, pastoralism, salt production, and coastal trade, with a modest overall scale. The island was not resource-poor, but political fragmentation and outside intervention limited wealth concentration.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、盐业、渔业、港口贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, salt production, fishing, and port commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、盐、奶酪、羊毛、皮革、蜡、珊瑚
tradeGoods.en: Grain, salt, cheese, wool, hides, wax, and coral

name.zh: 比萨与热那亚银币并用
name.en: Pisan and Genoese silver coinage in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富以土地使用权、羊群、牛只和粮食储备体现，货币持有量有限。沿海商人和教会机构的财富显著高于普通农户。
householdWealth.en: Most household wealth was held in land-use rights, sheep, cattle, and grain stores, with limited coin holdings. Coastal merchants and church institutions were markedly wealthier than ordinary peasant households.

economicSystem.zh: 以庄园租佃、村社生产和港口交换并存的封建型海岛经济
economicSystem.en: A feudal insular economy combining estate tenancy, village production, and port exchange

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备有限，往往分散于城堡、主教座堂和港口账簿之中，难以迅速集中动员。战时常需依赖外来贷款或贵族额外供役。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were limited and often dispersed across castles, cathedrals, and port accounts, making rapid mobilization difficult. In wartime, rulers often relied on outside credit or extra noble service.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏保守，以维持港口收入和土地贡赋稳定为核心，避免对村社施加过重现金税负。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, aiming to preserve port income and land dues while avoiding excessive cash burdens on village communities.

description.zh: 由贵族与其随从组成，数量不多，但在突袭和地方威慑中有价值。
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers; not numerous, but valuable for raids and local coercion.

description.zh: 主力来自村社和领主辖地，装备以矛、盾、弩和短剑为主。
description.en: The main force came from villages and lordly districts, armed chiefly with spears, shields, crossbows, and short blades.

name.en: Harbor Guards and Light Naval Force

description.zh: 负责沿岸巡逻、港口防御和反海盗行动，多依赖雇佣水手与商船改装。
description.en: Responsible for coastal patrols, port defense, and anti-piracy work, often relying on hired sailors and adapted merchant craft.

commanderInChief.zh: 各朱迪卡托君主或其指定执政官
commanderInChief.en: The giudicato ruler or his appointed regent

technology.zh: 军备属典型地中海拉丁中世纪水平，使用锁子甲、弩、矛与石木城防工事，海上力量有限。
technology.en: Military equipment was typical of the medieval Latin Mediterranean, using mail, crossbows, spears, and stone-and-timber fortifications, with limited naval strength.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自比萨与热那亚竞争、岛内继承冲突、海盗袭扰以及未来阿拉贡扩张。
threats.en: Main threats came from Pisan-Genoese rivalry, internal succession conflicts, piracy, and looming Aragonese expansion.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

populationDescription.zh: 人口以农村为主，分布于农业平原、丘陵牧区和少数港口城市。内陆社区较为保守，沿海地区人口流动性更高，并有意大利商人与神职人员常驻。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly rural, spread across agricultural plains, pastoral hills, and a few ports. Inland communities were more conservative, while coastal areas were more mobile and hosted resident Italian merchants and clergy.

socialClasses.zh: 王室与贵族、主教与修士、城市商人和手工业者、自由农民、佃农与牧民
socialClasses.en: Royal and noble elites, bishops and monastics, urban merchants and artisans, free peasants, tenants, and pastoralists

allies.zh: 部分朱迪卡托与教廷、比萨或热那亚维持阶段性同盟。
allies.en: Some giudicati maintained temporary alliances with the Papacy, Pisa, or Genoa.

enemies.zh: 敌对关系多为岛内诸政权间竞争，以及比萨与热那亚势力对港口和继承权的争夺。
enemies.en: Hostilities were mainly internal rivalries and the contest between Pisa and Genoa over ports and succession rights.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以在意大利海上共和国之间维持平衡、保护港口利益和争取教廷认可为重点。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on balancing the Italian maritime republics, protecting port interests, and securing papal recognition.

era.zh: 中世纪后期地中海封建时代
era.en: Late medieval Mediterranean feudal era

keyInnovations.zh: 改良港口设施、弩兵运用、罗马式石造教堂与拉丁文文书行政。
keyInnovations.en: Improved port facilities, crossbow use, Romanesque stone church building, and Latin documentary administration.

strengths.zh: 海岛位置重要，农业与牧业基础稳固，港口可连接西地中海贸易网络。
strengths.en: Its island position was strategic, its agrarian-pastoral base was solid, and its ports connected it to western Mediterranean trade.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分裂严重，外来势力干预深，财政与军力难以统一调动。
weaknesses.en: Political fragmentation was severe, outside influence was deep, and finances and military forces were hard to unify.

outlook.zh: 若无强有力整合，萨丁尼亚将继续沦为比萨、热那亚与阿拉贡角逐的对象。
outlook.en: Without strong internal consolidation, Sardinia would continue to be an object of rivalry among Pisa, Genoa, and Aragon.

description.zh: 1280年前后的萨丁尼亚是一个由地方朱迪卡托、教会权威和意大利海上势力共同塑造的分裂性海岛政区。
description.en: Around 1280, Sardinia was a divided island polity shaped by local giudicati, ecclesiastical authority, and the power of Italian maritime states.

dynasty.zh: 热那亚支配下的科西嘉地方领主时期
dynasty.en: Corsica under Genoese domination and local lordship

governmentForm.zh: 1280年的科西嘉实为受热那亚控制的岛屿属地，由总督、城堡守备与地方豪族共同治理。
governmentForm.en: In 1280 Corsica functioned as an island dependency under Genoese control, governed jointly by a governor, fortress garrisons, and local magnates.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以山地村社、氏族网络和少量沿海城镇为基础。贵族家族、教会与热那亚商人构成上层，普通居民多为农民、牧民和小规模渔民。岛内区域差异显著，沿海受拉丁商贸影响较深，内陆更保留强烈地方自治传统。
socialStructure.en: Society rested on mountain villages, kin networks, and a small number of coastal towns. Noble families, clergy, and Genoese merchants formed the upper stratum, while most inhabitants were peasants, herders, and small-scale fishers. Regional differences were pronounced, with coastal areas more deeply affected by Latin commerce and inland zones retaining stronger local autonomy.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由热那亚任命的官员、驻岛商人寡头和科西嘉地方豪族共同组成。实际控制往往依赖港口和堡垒，而非对全岛的直接官僚渗透。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of Genoese-appointed officials, resident mercantile oligarchs, and Corsican local lords. Real control often depended more on ports and fortresses than on direct bureaucratic penetration of the entire island.

succession.zh: 总督由热那亚方面任命，不采本地世袭原则。地方领主领地则常按家族继承，但易因氏族争斗与外部支持而生变。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by Genoa rather than chosen by local hereditary principle. Local lordships usually followed family succession, but were vulnerable to clan conflict and external backing for rivals.

structure.zh: 科西嘉行政核心集中在沿海据点，尤其是巴斯蒂亚等热那亚控制港口。总督依靠少量书记员、税务员和守备队维持秩序，广大内陆仍通过地方首领与教区网络间接统治。行政能力较弱，许多政策须借助盟约和人质制度落实。
structure.en: Corsican administration was centered on coastal strongpoints, especially Genoese-controlled ports such as Bastia. The governor relied on a small cadre of clerks, tax agents, and garrisons to maintain order, while much of the interior was ruled indirectly through local chiefs and parish networks. Administrative capacity was weak, and many policies had to be enforced through alliances and hostage arrangements.

function.zh: 负责政令发布、与热那亚本土通信及港口文书管理。
function.en: Handled proclamations, communication with Genoa, and port documentation.

function.zh: 征收港口关税、渔业费、地产贡赋与罚金。
function.en: Collected port customs, fishery fees, landed dues, and fines.

function.zh: 管理守军、海岸警戒和对地方叛乱的快速镇压。
function.en: Managed garrisons, coastal watch, and rapid suppression of local unrest.

localAdmin.zh: 地方管理主要依赖村社长老、主教区神职人员和与热那亚合作的豪族。内陆地区常保留较强自治性，对中央命令的执行并不稳定。
localAdmin.en: Local administration depended mainly on village elders, diocesan clergy, and magnates willing to cooperate with Genoa. Inland districts often preserved substantial autonomy and implemented central orders inconsistently.

legalSystem.zh: 法律结合热那亚商法、教会法和科西嘉习惯法。涉商与港口案件偏向书面裁决，内陆纠纷则常通过调停、血亲赔偿与地方仲裁解决。
legalSystem.en: Law combined Genoese commercial law, canon law, and Corsican custom. Commercial and port disputes favored written adjudication, while inland conflicts were often settled by mediation, kin compensation, and local arbitration.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以港口关税、地产贡赋、渔业和盐务收费为主，辅以罚金和临时征发。山区居民常以实物和劳役承担义务。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on port customs, landed dues, and charges on fisheries and salt, supplemented by fines and extraordinary levies. Highland communities often met obligations through goods and labor service.

religion.zh: 天主教

culturalAchievements.zh: 科西嘉虽非大型文化中心，但在修道院、教区组织和地方口述传统方面保存了鲜明特色。岛上宗教建筑与防御塔楼反映了其长期面对海上竞争和袭扰的环境。
culturalAchievements.en: Corsica was not a major cultural center, but it preserved distinctive monastic, diocesan, and oral local traditions. Its religious buildings and defensive towers reflected a long environment of maritime rivalry and raiding.

languageFamily.zh: 罗曼语族，科西嘉语早期形态并存于拉丁文与利古里亚—托斯卡纳语影响之下
languageFamily.en: Romance family, with early Corsican forms alongside Latin and Ligurian-Tuscan influences

philosophy.zh: 拉丁天主教秩序与山地氏族荣誉传统并存
philosophy.en: Latin Catholic order coexisting with mountain clan honor traditions

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母，用于拉丁文和意大利语文书
writingSystem.en: Latin script, used for Latin and Italian documentary records

gdpDescription.zh: 岛上经济体量较小，以自给农业、放牧、林木资源和有限海运贸易为主。地形破碎和政治控制薄弱限制了市场整合。
gdpDescription.en: The island economy was small, based on subsistence farming, herding, timber resources, and limited maritime trade. Rugged terrain and weak political control constrained market integration.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、林业、渔业、沿海贸易
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, forestry, fishing, and coastal trade

tradeGoods.zh: 木材、蜂蜡、皮革、奶酪、葡萄酒、橄榄油、鱼制品
tradeGoods.en: Timber, beeswax, hides, cheese, wine, olive oil, and fish products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富多体现为羊群、山地梯田、栗林与简单房舍，现金流通有限。沿海商人和关税承包者拥有相对更高财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mostly held in flocks, terraced plots, chestnut groves, and simple dwellings, with limited cash circulation. Coastal merchants and customs farmers possessed relatively greater wealth.

economicSystem.zh: 以村社自给、领主贡赋和热那亚港口贸易抽成为特征的边缘型海岛经济
economicSystem.en: A peripheral island economy characterized by village subsistence, lordly dues, and Genoese port extraction

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模较小，多用于维持驻军与海岸据点。若发生叛乱或海盗加剧，常需热那亚本土额外拨款。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was modest and mainly used to sustain garrisons and coastal strongholds. In the event of rebellion or intensified piracy, extra subventions from Genoa were often required.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以确保港口税收和低成本维持统治为目标，避免在内陆投入过多行政支出。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to secure port revenue and maintain rule at low cost, avoiding excessive administrative spending in the interior.

description.zh: 驻守巴斯蒂亚及其他据点，负责城防、巡逻和征税护卫。
description.en: Stationed at Bastia and other strongpoints, they handled urban defense, patrols, and tax escort duty.

description.zh: 由合作村社和地方豪族提供，适合山地作战，但纪律和持续性较弱。
description.en: Provided by cooperating communities and local lords, they were suited to mountain warfare but weaker in discipline and endurance.

description.zh: 规模很小，用于沿岸通信、反海盗和护送税船。
description.en: Very small in scale, used for coastal communication, anti-piracy, and escorting revenue vessels.

technology.zh: 军事技术与热那亚本土接近，使用弩、长矛、锁甲、石砌堡垒和轻型桨帆船，但资源有限。
technology.en: Military technology was broadly similar to that of Genoa, using crossbows, spears, mail, stone fortifications, and light oared-sail craft, though resources were limited.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括地方豪族叛乱、山地反抗、海盗袭扰以及比萨残余影响。
threats.en: Main threats included revolts by local magnates, mountain resistance, piracy, and residual Pisan influence.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

description.zh: 铁环编织甲衣，灵活且防护可靠
description.en: Iron ring-linked armor, flexible and reliably protective

populationDescription.zh: 科西嘉人口稀疏，多居山地村落和沿海小镇。巴斯蒂亚等港口虽小，却是政治与商业控制的关键节点。
populationDescription.en: Corsica was sparsely populated, with most people living in mountain villages and small coastal towns. Ports such as Bastia were small but crucial nodes of political and commercial control.

socialClasses.zh: 热那亚官员与商人、地方豪族、神职人员、自由农民与牧民、依附民与贫民
socialClasses.en: Genoese officials and merchants, local magnates, clergy, free peasants and herders, dependents, and poor laborers

allies.zh: 科西嘉名义上依附热那亚，其主要外部支持来自热那亚海军和商人集团。
allies.en: Corsica was effectively dependent on Genoa, whose navy and merchant interests formed its principal external support.

enemies.zh: 敌对者包括反热那亚地方势力、海盗，以及任何试图恢复比萨影响的派系。
enemies.en: Enemies included anti-Genoese local forces, pirates, and any faction seeking to restore Pisan influence.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策基本服从热那亚利益，核心在于保港口、控航道并防止岛上政局被竞争海权利用。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy largely followed Genoese interests, centered on securing ports, controlling sea lanes, and preventing rival maritime powers from exploiting island politics.

era.zh: 中世纪后期拉丁海洋边疆时代
era.en: Late medieval Latin maritime frontier era

keyInnovations.zh: 港口城堡化、弩兵运用、热那亚式海关与航海文书制度。
keyInnovations.en: Port fortification, crossbow deployment, and Genoese customs and maritime documentation systems.

strengths.zh: 地处西地中海要冲，山地防御性强，具木材与牧产资源。
strengths.en: It occupied a strategic western Mediterranean position, had defensible highlands, and possessed timber and pastoral resources.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、财政薄弱、内陆控制困难，对宗主海权依赖极大。
weaknesses.en: It had a small population, weak finances, difficult interior control, and heavy dependence on its maritime overlord.

outlook.zh: 短期内只要热那亚海权稳固，科西嘉大体可维持稳定，但难以形成强有力的统一岛内政权。
outlook.en: So long as Genoese sea power remained firm, Corsica could stay broadly stable in the short term, but it was unlikely to develop a strong unified island state.

description.zh: 1280年的科西嘉是一个由热那亚海权、沿海堡垒与山地地方势力共同维系的边缘型岛屿属地。
description.en: In 1280 Corsica was a peripheral island dependency sustained by Genoese sea power, coastal fortresses, and highland local forces.

dynasty.zh: 丹巴德尼耶时期的僧伽罗王统
dynasty.en: The Sinhalese monarchy of the Dambadeniya period

governmentForm.zh: 1280年前后的僧伽罗王国为佛教君主制，王权在名义上继承古代阿努拉德普勒—波隆纳鲁沃传统，但实际上已趋于衰弱与区域化。
governmentForm.en: Around 1280 the Sinhalese kingdom was a Buddhist monarchy that claimed continuity with the old Anuradhapura–Polonnaruwa tradition, though in practice royal power had become weakened and regionalized.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族官僚、僧团、村社农民和服役群体组成。灌溉农业社会仍是国家基础，寺院在土地、教育和合法性上具有核心作用。由于南印度入侵与都城迁移，许多地区出现人口流动和地方军政势力增强。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal house, noble officials, the sangha, village cultivators, and service groups. Irrigation-based agrarian society remained the foundation of the state, and monasteries held a central role in landholding, learning, and legitimacy. Because of South Indian invasions and royal relocations, many regions saw population movement and stronger local military-political power.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层由国王、王族、地方大臣、军职贵族和高级僧侣构成。王权合法性高度依赖护持佛法、维系寺院和掌控稻田水利系统。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the king, royal kin, regional ministers, military nobles, and senior monks. Royal legitimacy depended heavily on protecting Buddhism, sustaining monasteries, and controlling the rice-irrigation system.

succession.zh: 王位继承常在王族内部选择，并受大臣、军方和僧团支持影响。因王室分支众多，摄政、兄终弟及和地方拥立都可能出现，继承并不总是平稳。
succession.en: Succession was often decided within the royal family and shaped by support from ministers, military elites, and the sangha. With many royal branches, regencies, fraternal succession, and regional backing for claimants were all possible, making succession far from smooth.

structure.zh: 王国行政沿袭古代僧伽罗宫廷传统，以国王、首席大臣、地方领主和寺院网络共同运作。虽然名义都城可指波隆纳鲁沃，但这一时期实际政治重心已更频繁转向丹巴德尼耶等较安全地区。中央对边远地区和北部的控制明显减弱。
structure.en: The kingdom's administration inherited older Sinhalese court traditions and operated through the king, chief ministers, provincial lords, and monastic networks. Although Polonnaruwa could still be cited as the capital, the real political center in this period had increasingly shifted toward safer locations such as Dambadeniya. Central control over peripheral zones and the north had clearly weakened.

function.zh: 起草王命、保管敕令、处理宫廷文书与对地方的行政指令。
function.en: Drafted royal commands, preserved edicts, and managed court records and provincial orders.

function.zh: 管理稻田赋税、劳役征发、蓄水池和渠道修缮及王田收入。
function.en: Managed paddy taxation, corvée obligations, repair of tanks and canals, and income from crown lands.

function.zh: 统筹近卫军、地方兵役、象队调配及对南印度威胁的防务准备。
function.en: Coordinated the guard forces, provincial levies, elephant deployments, and defenses against South Indian threats.

function.zh: 管理寺院赐田、宗教捐献、僧团协调和佛教典礼事务。
function.en: Managed temple land grants, religious donations, coordination with the sangha, and Buddhist ceremonial affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依靠拉塔和科拉莱等区域层级、村长、地方豪族及寺院代理人。中央在稻作核心区仍有影响，但对边远地区往往需通过盟附关系而非直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on regional divisions, village headmen, local magnates, and temple agents. The center retained influence in the rice-growing core, but in peripheral areas it often governed through subordinate alliances rather than direct control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合王命、僧伽罗习惯法和佛教伦理规范。土地、水利、婚姻与役务案件常由地方官、长老与僧侣共同调处。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined royal decree, Sinhalese customary law, and Buddhist ethical norms. Cases involving land, irrigation, marriage, and service obligations were often mediated by local officials, elders, and monks together.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以稻米田赋、劳役、园林税、贸易税与王田收入为主。实物征收占比很高，尤其是在稻谷、油料、椰产和服役义务方面。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on paddy dues, labor service, garden taxes, trade tolls, and revenue from crown lands. Collection in kind remained very important, especially in rice, oil products, coconut produce, and service obligations.

religion.zh: 上座部佛教

culturalAchievements.zh: 这一时期僧伽罗文化仍以佛教文学、史书编纂、寺院教育和灌溉文明遗产为核心。尽管政治衰退，巴利与僧伽罗文献传统、佛塔修缮和寺院艺术仍持续发展。
culturalAchievements.en: In this period Sinhalese culture remained centered on Buddhist literature, chronicle writing, monastic education, and the legacy of irrigation civilization. Despite political decline, Pali and Sinhala literary traditions, stupa restoration, and temple art continued to develop.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系印度-雅利安语族，以僧伽罗语为主，宗教学术广用巴利语
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Indo-Aryan branch; primarily Sinhala, with Pali widely used in religious scholarship

philosophy.zh: 上座部佛教王权观与巴利学术传统
philosophy.en: Theravada Buddhist kingship and the Pali scholastic tradition

writingSystem.zh: 僧伽罗字母，用于僧伽罗语与巴利语经论抄写
writingSystem.en: Sinhala script, used for Sinhala and Pali manuscripts

gdpDescription.zh: 王国经济以稻作灌溉农业为核心，并辅以椰子、园林作物、象牙、宝石和海上贸易。虽仍具较高农业生产潜力，但都城迁徙、战争和水利衰败已削弱总体动员能力。
gdpDescription.en: The kingdom's economy was centered on irrigated rice agriculture, supplemented by coconuts, garden crops, ivory, gems, and maritime trade. It still possessed considerable agricultural potential, but royal relocations, warfare, and deterioration in irrigation had weakened overall mobilization.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻作农业、水利工程、椰产与园林种植、宝石采集、海贸
mainIndustries.en: Rice agriculture, irrigation works, coconut and garden cultivation, gem extraction, and maritime trade

tradeGoods.zh: 稻米、椰子制品、香料、宝石、象牙、肉桂、纺织品、象只
tradeGoods.en: Rice, coconut products, spices, gems, ivory, cinnamon, textiles, and elephants

name.en: Silver tankas and copper coinage in circulation

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要体现为稻田使用权、水牛、椰树、粮仓和村社地位。寺院与王室赐田机构掌握大量土地财富，城市商人和海港中间商则持有较多可流通金银。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was chiefly held in paddy-use rights, buffaloes, coconut trees, grain stores, and village status. Monasteries and royal grant-holders controlled large landed wealth, while urban merchants and port middlemen held more liquid silver and gold.

economicSystem.zh: 以灌溉稻作、王田贡赋、寺院土地和港口交换相结合的佛教君主制农业经济
economicSystem.en: A Buddhist monarchical agrarian economy combining irrigated rice cultivation, crown dues, temple estates, and port exchange

source.en: Paddy taxes and commuted labor obligations
Crown lands and irrigated estate income

source.zh: 林园税、罚金与附庸贡物
source.en: Garden taxes, fines, and tributary goods

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库仍保有一定积蓄，但受持续防务、水利修缮和政局不稳影响，财政缓冲正在缩小。相当部分财富以粮仓、寺院储藏和地方赋役承诺形式存在，而非纯现金。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury still retained meaningful reserves, but continuing defense costs, irrigation repairs, and political instability were shrinking fiscal buffers. A considerable share of wealth existed in granaries, temple stores, and provincial service obligations rather than in pure cash.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持稻田税源、水利系统和佛教正统支持为核心，但因政权衰退已更频繁依赖临时征发与地方协商。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on preserving paddy revenue, irrigation infrastructure, and Buddhist orthodox support, but state decline forced more reliance on extraordinary levies and negotiation with regional powers.

description.zh: 直属国王与都城，装备较好，承担王宫护卫与核心战斗任务。
description.en: Directly attached to the king and court, better equipped, and tasked with palace security and core combat operations.

description.zh: 主要兵力来源于地方领主和村社征发，适合守御、行军和围垒作战。
description.en: The main force came from provincial lords and village levies, suited to defense, marching, and siege-field operations.

description.zh: 象队兼具威慑和突破作用，骑兵规模有限，多用于侦察、传令和追击。
description.en: Elephant corps provided shock and prestige, while cavalry remained limited and was used mainly for reconnaissance, dispatch, and pursuit.

technology.zh: 军制结合南亚传统与海上交流影响，使用弓、矛、剑、甲胄、战象、土石工事和有限攻城器械。海军并非强项，但沿海交通和港口防务具一定能力。
technology.en: Military practice combined South Asian traditions with influences from maritime exchange, using bows, spears, swords, armor, war elephants, earth-and-stone fortifications, and limited siege equipment. Naval power was not a major strength, but the kingdom retained some coastal transport and port defense capacity.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自南印度泰米尔势力、北部政治分裂、都城转移造成的控制真空以及财政紧张导致的兵源不稳。
threats.en: Main threats came from South Indian Tamil powers, northern political fragmentation, control vacuums caused by shifting capitals, and unstable recruitment caused by fiscal strain.

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于西南湿润区、稻作村社和仍维持灌溉系统的平原地带。北中部旧都区域因战乱与水利失修而出现相对衰退，但宗教中心仍吸引人口与资源。
populationDescription.en: Population was concentrated in the wetter southwest, rice-growing villages, and plains where irrigation systems still functioned. The old north-central capital zone had relatively declined because of war and irrigation decay, but religious centers continued to attract people and resources.

socialClasses.zh: 王族与大臣、军职贵族、僧侣与寺院团体、村社农民、工匠商人、服役与依附群体
socialClasses.en: Royal kin and ministers, military nobility, monks and temple corporations, village cultivators, artisans and merchants, and service-dependent groups

allies.zh: 主要依托岛内佛教僧团、南部与西部地方势力，并在海贸上与阿拉伯和南印度商人保持务实关系。
allies.en: The kingdom relied chiefly on the island's Buddhist sangha, southern and western regional powers, and maintained pragmatic trade ties with Arab and South Indian merchants.

enemies.zh: 主要敌手为南印度泰米尔军事势力及岛内割据者，尤其是对北部和古都区有觊觎的力量。
enemies.en: Its main enemies were South Indian Tamil military powers and internal separatist forces, especially those contesting the north and the old royal zones.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策重在守住海港贸易、争取佛教正统地位并避免南印度势力进一步深入内陆。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve port commerce, uphold Buddhist legitimacy, and prevent deeper South Indian penetration into the island interior.

era.zh: 南亚中世纪灌溉王国时代
era.en: Medieval South Asian irrigation-kingdom era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型蓄水池与渠道工程、巴利—僧伽罗文献抄写、寺院教育体系、战象组织与稻作管理技术。
keyInnovations.en: Large tank-and-canal engineering, Pali-Sinhala manuscript culture, monastic education systems, war-elephant organization, and rice-field management techniques.

strengths.zh: 拥有深厚的佛教合法性、成熟的灌溉农业传统和连接印度洋的港口贸易优势。
strengths.en: It possessed deep Buddhist legitimacy, a mature irrigation-agricultural tradition, and port access to Indian Ocean trade.

weaknesses.zh: 中央权威衰退、北部控制薄弱、都城多次迁移且水利维护成本沉重。
weaknesses.en: Central authority was declining, control of the north was weak, capitals had shifted repeatedly, and irrigation maintenance was costly.

outlook.zh: 若不能恢复稳定都城与水利税基，王国将继续向区域化和防御性统治方向发展。
outlook.en: If it could not restore a stable capital and irrigation tax base, the kingdom would continue drifting toward regionalization and defensive rule.

description.zh: 1280年前后的僧伽罗王国是一个仍具佛教文明与灌溉农业底蕴、但已因外患与内部分裂而走向衰落的南亚王国。
description.en: Around 1280, the Sinhalese kingdom remained a South Asian state rich in Buddhist civilization and irrigation agriculture, yet was moving into decline under external pressure and internal fragmentation.

governmentForm.zh: 以亲族和猎队为核心、由地方首领协商领导的分散部族社会。
governmentForm.en: A decentralized tribal society led through kin groups and local hunting-band consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 图勒社会以扩展家庭、季节性营地和猎队为基本单位，社会分层较弱。熟练猎手、船主、萨满与年长者享有较高威望，但财富积累有限。婚姻、互赠与共同捕猎维系群体关系。
socialStructure.en: Thule society was organized around extended families, seasonal camps, and hunting bands, with relatively weak stratification. Skilled hunters, boat owners, shamans, and elders held greater prestige, but long-term wealth accumulation was limited. Marriage ties, gift exchange, and cooperative hunting bound communities together.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定贵族阶层，实际领导权掌握在成功猎手、营地主人和有声望的长者手中。萨满在精神事务上亦具有重要影响力。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; practical authority rested with successful hunters, camp leaders, and respected elders. Shamans also exercised major influence in spiritual matters.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常依靠声望、资历和资源调配能力形成，而非严格世袭。若某位首领失去支持，家庭与猎队可转而追随他人。
succession.en: Leadership usually emerged from prestige, experience, and the ability to organize resources rather than strict heredity. If a leader lost support, families and hunting bands could shift their allegiance elsewhere.

structure.zh: 图勒文化不存在统一国家机器，各地营地由季节性会议和长者协商管理。公共事务主要围绕迁徙路线、猎场分配、食物储备与纠纷调停展开。
structure.en: The Thule world lacked a centralized state apparatus; local camps were governed through seasonal meetings and elder consensus. Public affairs centered on migration routes, allocation of hunting grounds, food storage, and mediation of disputes.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、狩猎安排、资源共享与纠纷调解。
function.en: Coordinated migration, hunting schedules, resource sharing, and dispute settlement.

function.zh: 主持祭仪、治病、占兆并维系群体精神秩序。
function.en: Conducted rituals, healing, divination, and maintained spiritual order.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理由若干家庭组成的营地自主管理。冬夏营地之间的关系依赖亲族联系与口头协定。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was handled autonomously by camps composed of several families. Relations between winter and summer camps depended on kinship ties and oral agreements.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法和调解为主，强调赔偿、和解与群体和谐。严重冲突有时通过分离迁徙或报复解决。
legalSystem.en: Law was primarily customary and conciliatory, emphasizing compensation, reconciliation, and group harmony. Serious conflicts were sometimes resolved by separation of camps or revenge.

taxationSystem.zh: 不存在正式税制，首领通过分配猎获、组织劳作和接受馈赠来维持权威。大型共同捕猎后的再分配具有准财政性质。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; leaders sustained authority through allocation of catches, labor coordination, and receipt of gifts. Redistribution after large communal hunts served a quasi-fiscal role.

religion.zh: 萨满教

culturalAchievements.zh: 图勒人发展出适应北极环境的犬拉雪橇、乌米亚克大船、复合鱼叉和冬季居住技术。其骨、象牙与石制器物体现出高度实用性与审美结合。
culturalAchievements.en: The Thule developed dog sled transport, large umiak boats, composite harpoons, and winter housing techniques suited to the Arctic. Their bone, ivory, and stone artifacts show a strong blend of utility and aesthetic design.

languageFamily.zh: 爱斯基摩-阿留申语系（因纽特分支前身）
languageFamily.en: Eskimo–Aleut language family (ancestral Inuit branch)

philosophy.zh: 泛灵信仰、祖灵观念与人与海兽环境平衡的伦理
philosophy.en: Animism, ancestral belief, and an ethic of balance between humans and the marine-animal world

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，知识主要依靠口述传统、歌谣与器物技艺传承
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; knowledge was transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and craft practice

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 图勒经济以自给性海猎、渔猎和季节性采集为主，市场化程度极低。其财富主要体现为食物储备、舟具、雪橇、犬只和象牙等耐用资源。
gdpDescription.en: The Thule economy was based on subsistence marine hunting, fishing, and seasonal gathering, with very low marketization. Wealth was expressed mainly in food stores, boats, sleds, dogs, and durable materials such as ivory.

mainIndustries.zh: 海兽捕猎、渔业、骨角牙器制造、皮革加工、雪橇与舟船制作
mainIndustries.en: Marine mammal hunting, fishing, bone-and-ivory toolmaking, hide processing, sled and boat construction

tradeGoods.zh: 海象牙、兽皮、骨角器、燧石与陨铁制品、鲸脂
tradeGoods.en: Walrus ivory, animal hides, bone tools, flint and meteoritic-iron items, whale oil

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要取决于猎具、犬队、舟船和食物储藏能力。贫富差异存在，但远小于农业国家。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth depended chiefly on hunting gear, dog teams, boats, and food-storage capacity. Inequality existed, but it was far lower than in agrarian states.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以家庭生产、共享分配和礼物交换为基础的狩猎采集经济
economicSystem.en: A hunting-gathering economy based on household production, redistribution, and gift exchange

category.en: Maintenance of hunting gear and boats

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: “国库”主要体现为首领与营地共同掌握的食物、皮革、象牙和工具储备，而非铸币储藏。
treasuryDescription.en: The 'treasury' consisted mainly of food, hides, ivory, and tools held collectively by camp leaders rather than coined money.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政取向偏向储备与再分配，以提高冬季生存能力并稳定群体关系。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized storage and redistribution to improve winter survival and stabilize social ties.

description.zh: 由成年猎手组成，平时狩猎，必要时承担部落防御与袭击任务。
description.en: Composed of adult hunters who normally hunted but could defend camps or conduct raids when needed.

description.zh: 依托皮船沿海快速机动，适合巡查海岸与短程冲突。
description.en: Used skin boats for rapid coastal movement, suitable for shoreline patrol and short-range conflict.

commanderInChief.zh: 各地猎队首领协同指挥

technology.zh: 武备以鱼叉、弓箭、长矛、骨石刃器和皮甲为主，机动能力强但杀伤力有限。
technology.en: Armament centered on harpoons, bows, spears, bone-and-stone blades, and hide armor, with strong mobility but limited striking power.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自资源竞争、营地间冲突、极端气候和食物短缺，而非大规模国家战争。
threats.en: Main threats came from resource competition, inter-camp conflict, extreme climate, and food shortages rather than large-scale state warfare.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 骑士标准双刃直剑，十字形护手
description.en: Knight's standard double-edged straight sword with cruciform guard

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布极为稀疏，沿北极海岸和岛屿呈带状散居。大多数人生活在小型季节性聚落中，流动性很高。
populationDescription.en: Population was extremely sparse, scattered in bands along Arctic coasts and islands. Most people lived in small seasonal settlements with high mobility.

socialClasses.zh: 猎手、舟船所有者、萨满、普通家庭成员与依附亲属
socialClasses.en: Hunters, boat owners, shamans, ordinary household members, and dependent kin

allies.zh: 以邻近因纽特前身群体和互通婚姻的营地网络为主要合作对象。
allies.en: Their main partners were neighboring proto-Inuit groups and camp networks linked by marriage.

enemies.zh: 无固定国家敌人，主要摩擦对象是争夺猎场的邻近群体。
enemies.en: They had no permanent state enemies; friction was mainly with neighboring groups competing for hunting grounds.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外关系以互市、通婚、迁徙协商和避免高成本冲突为主。
foreignPolicy.en: External relations focused on barter, intermarriage, migration arrangements, and avoidance of costly conflict.

era.zh: 北极晚期石骨器—海猎技术体系
era.en: Late Arctic stone-bone maritime hunting technological complex

keyInnovations.zh: 复合鱼叉、犬拉雪橇、乌米亚克与单人皮艇、雪屋与半地下冬屋技术
keyInnovations.en: Composite harpoons, dog sleds, umiaks and kayaks, and snow-house and semi-subterranean winter dwelling techniques

strengths.zh: 环境适应能力极强，海猎技术成熟，社会协作灵活。
strengths.en: Highly adapted to the environment, with mature marine-hunting techniques and flexible social cooperation.

weaknesses.zh: 人口少、政治分散、剩余产品有限，难以长期承受灾荒或外来压力。
weaknesses.en: Small population, political fragmentation, and limited surplus made sustained resilience against famine or external pressure difficult.

outlook.zh: 在1280年前后整体仍具扩张与繁荣潜力，但未来将受气候波动和资源变化显著影响。
outlook.en: Around 1280 the culture still had room for expansion and prosperity, but its future would be strongly shaped by climate and resource fluctuations.

description.zh: 图勒文化是北极地区以海猎和迁徙营地为基础、技术适应性极强的部族社会。
description.en: The Thule Culture was a highly adaptive Arctic tribal society centered on marine hunting and mobile camp life.

governmentForm.zh: 以希达共同体为核心、由长老和家族代表协商治理的游动社会。
governmentForm.en: A mobile society centered on siida communities and governed by elders and family representatives through consensus.

socialStructure.zh: 萨米社会以希达为基本政治与经济单位，由若干家庭共同管理渔场、猎场和迁徙路线。社会分层有限，但富有驯鹿、渔猎资源和贸易联系的家庭更具影响力。诺艾迪萨满、长老和熟练猎手在共同体中地位较高。
socialStructure.en: Sámi society was organized around the siida, the basic political and economic unit through which several families jointly managed fishing waters, hunting grounds, and migration routes. Stratification was limited, but families with larger reindeer holdings, better access to fishing and hunting resources, or trade links held greater influence. Noaidi shamans, elders, and skilled hunters enjoyed elevated status within the community.

rulingClass.zh: 不存在正式贵族，实际领导者是希达长老、富裕家庭首领与具宗教威望的诺艾迪。其权威建立在协调能力、传统知识和资源控制上。
rulingClass.en: There was no formal aristocracy; effective leaders were siida elders, heads of wealthy households, and religiously influential noaidi. Their authority rested on coordination skill, traditional knowledge, and control over resources.

succession.zh: 领导地位通常在家族内部延续，但须获得共同体认可。若长老无力维持共识，希达可推举其他资深成员主持事务。
succession.en: Leadership often continued within influential families, but it required community acceptance. If an elder could no longer maintain consensus, the siida could elevate another experienced member.

structure.zh: 萨米人并无统一国家，而是由许多地方希达组成的松散网络。政治权力集中于资源使用规则、争端调解和对外贸易协商，而非常设官僚机构。
structure.en: The Sámi had no unified state, but rather a loose network of local siidas. Political authority focused on regulating resource use, mediating disputes, and negotiating external trade rather than maintaining a permanent bureaucracy.

function.zh: 决定迁徙、渔猎分配、边界协调与内部调解。
function.en: Decided migration, distribution of fishing and hunting rights, boundary coordination, and internal mediation.

function.zh: 与挪威、瑞典和诺夫哥罗德商人或征收者往来，组织皮毛与鱼产交换。
function.en: Handled dealings with Norwegian, Swedish, and Novgorodian traders or tribute collectors, and organized exchange of furs and fish.

localAdmin.zh: 地方事务由各希达自理，管理范围通常围绕湖泊、河谷、森林和山地季节性资源。跨区域事务多依靠会议、婚姻关系和习惯边界处理。
localAdmin.en: Local affairs were managed by each siida, usually within a seasonal resource zone of lakes, river valleys, forests, and uplands. Interregional matters were handled through meetings, marriage ties, and customary boundaries.

legalSystem.zh: 法律主要为口头习惯法，强调资源共享规则、赔偿和群体调解。外来王权施加的税务与司法影响在沿海和边缘地区逐渐增强。
legalSystem.en: Law was chiefly oral customary law, emphasizing resource-sharing rules, compensation, and communal mediation. External royal influence in taxation and justice was gradually increasing in coastal and frontier areas.

taxationSystem.zh: 内部没有统一成文税制，但共同体成员需承担共享劳动和礼物义务。部分地区开始向周边王国或商贸势力缴纳皮毛、鱼干等贡赋。
taxationSystem.en: Internally there was no unified formal tax code, but community members bore obligations of shared labor and gift contributions. In some areas, tribute in furs, dried fish, and similar goods was increasingly paid to neighboring kingdoms or trading powers.

religion.zh: 萨满教

culturalAchievements.zh: 萨米人形成了适应北方森林—苔原环境的迁徙、渔猎和小规模驯鹿管理体系。其约伊克、鼓纹象征和皮革骨角工艺具有鲜明地方特色。
culturalAchievements.en: The Sámi developed migration, fishing-hunting, and small-scale reindeer management systems adapted to the northern forest-tundra environment. Their joik tradition, drum symbolism, and leather-bone craftwork possessed a distinctive regional character.

languageFamily.zh: 乌拉尔语系萨米语支
languageFamily.en: Uralic language family, Sámi branch

philosophy.zh: 万物有灵、土地与水域神圣性、祖灵崇敬与实用伦理并重
philosophy.en: Animism, the sacredness of land and waters, ancestral reverence, and a pragmatic moral order

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，传统以口传、约伊克歌唱和象征性鼓纹传播
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; traditions were transmitted through oral memory, joik singing, and symbolic drum imagery

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 萨米经济兼具自给性与边贸性，核心为渔猎、采集、毛皮交换和早期驯鹿经营。由于同北欧和俄罗斯商路相连，其商品化程度略高于纯狩猎部族。
gdpDescription.en: The Sámi economy combined subsistence and frontier trade, centered on fishing, hunting, gathering, fur exchange, and early reindeer management. Because it was connected to Nordic and Russian trade routes, it was somewhat more commercialized than purely hunting tribal societies.

mainIndustries.zh: 渔业、毛皮狩猎、驯鹿放牧与运输、皮革加工、干鱼生产
mainIndustries.en: Fishing, fur hunting, reindeer herding and transport, hide processing, dried-fish production

tradeGoods.zh: 毛皮、驯鹿制品、鱼干、海兽产品、角骨器、羽绒
tradeGoods.en: Furs, reindeer products, dried fish, marine products, antler and bone goods, down

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富由驯鹿数量、渔场控制、皮毛积累和对外贸易渠道决定。沿海与边贸节点家庭通常较内陆家庭更富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was determined by reindeer holdings, control of fishing sites, accumulated furs, and access to external trade channels. Coastal and frontier-trade households were generally wealthier than inland ones.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以希达共享资源、家庭生产和边境贸易结合的混合型经济
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy combining siida-managed shared resources, household production, and frontier trade

source.zh: 共同体贡献与实物分摊

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政储备多以毛皮、鱼干、驯鹿产品和少量银重形式保存，分散掌握于主要希达与贸易节点。
treasuryDescription.en: Fiscal reserves were held mainly in furs, dried fish, reindeer products, and small quantities of weighed silver, dispersed among major siidas and trade points.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重心在维持季节生计、支持边贸和应对外部征收压力，强调弹性而非集中征敛。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice focused on maintaining seasonal subsistence, supporting frontier trade, and coping with external tribute demands, favoring flexibility over centralized extraction.

description.zh: 由成年男子临时集结，负责营地防卫、护送和边境争端。
description.en: Temporarily assembled adult men responsible for camp defense, escort duties, and frontier disputes.

description.zh: 依托雪地交通快速移动，适合侦察、传讯和冬季袭扰。
description.en: Used snow transport for rapid movement, suited to reconnaissance, message carrying, and winter raiding.

technology.zh: 武器以弓箭、矛、刀斧和狩猎器具改装兵器为主，善于雪地行军与伏击，但缺乏重装与攻城能力。
technology.en: Weapons consisted mainly of bows, spears, knives, axes, and militarized hunting tools; they were effective in snow travel and ambush but lacked heavy equipment and siege capacity.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北欧王国税权扩张、边地冲突、资源争夺和恶劣气候引发的生计危机。
threats.en: Main threats came from expanding Nordic royal taxation, frontier conflicts, resource competition, and livelihood crises triggered by harsh climate.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 人口分布于芬诺斯堪的亚北部广大森林、湖区和苔原，聚落规模小且具有明显季节流动性。沿海、河口和内陆渔猎节点是人口较密集区域。
populationDescription.en: The population was spread across the forests, lake districts, and tundra of northern Fennoscandia, in small settlements with marked seasonal mobility. Coastal zones, estuaries, and inland fishing-hunting nodes were the more concentrated population areas.

socialClasses.zh: 希达长老、富裕驯鹿或渔猎家庭、诺艾迪萨满、普通家庭成员与依附者
socialClasses.en: Siida elders, wealthy reindeer or fishing-hunting households, noaidi shamans, ordinary household members, and dependents

allies.zh: 多依赖邻近希达网络，并与部分挪威、瑞典、卡累利阿和诺夫哥罗德商人保持务实合作。
allies.en: They relied chiefly on neighboring siida networks and maintained pragmatic cooperation with some Norwegian, Swedish, Karelian, and Novgorodian traders.

enemies.zh: 无统一宿敌，但对周边王权扩张、征税者与竞争性边地群体保持警惕。
enemies.en: They had no single permanent enemy, but remained wary of neighboring state expansion, tribute collectors, and competing frontier groups.

foreignPolicy.zh: 采取平衡周边强权、保全迁徙空间和扩大贸易渠道的务实策略。
foreignPolicy.en: Their foreign policy was pragmatic: balancing neighboring powers, preserving migratory space, and widening trade opportunities.

era.zh: 北方森林—苔原铁器边地文化
era.en: Northern forest-tundra Iron Age frontier culture

keyInnovations.zh: 雪橇交通、寒地渔猎技术、皮革毛皮加工、早期驯鹿运输与资源分区管理
keyInnovations.en: Sled transport, cold-region fishing and hunting techniques, leather and fur processing, early reindeer transport, and resource-zone management

strengths.zh: 环境适应力强，机动性高，熟悉北地资源并连接多方贸易网络。
strengths.en: They possessed strong environmental adaptation, high mobility, detailed knowledge of northern resources, and links to multiple trade networks.

weaknesses.zh: 政治分散，人口较少，易受外部征税和边境压力影响。
weaknesses.en: They were politically fragmented, relatively few in number, and vulnerable to external taxation and frontier pressure.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后仍能维持稳定，但未来将 increasingly 面临北欧王权、教会与边疆贸易体系更深层渗透。
outlook.en: Around 1280 they could still maintain stability, but would increasingly face deeper penetration by Nordic monarchies, churches, and frontier trade systems.

description.zh: 萨米人是北欧北部以希达共同体、渔猎与早期驯鹿经营为基础的游动社会。
description.en: The Sámi were a mobile northern society built around siida communities, fishing-hunting, and early reindeer management.

dynasty.zh: 留里克王朝治下的诺夫哥罗德共和国
dynasty.en: The Novgorod Republic under the Rurikid dynasty

governmentForm.zh: 由市民议会、贵族家族和受邀大公共同构成的寡头共和政体。
governmentForm.en: An oligarchic republican polity combining a civic assembly, boyar families, and an invited prince.

socialStructure.zh: 诺夫哥罗德社会由大贵族、商人、教士、手工业者、自由民和农民构成，城市上层在政治上占据主导。乡村地区依附关系增强，但仍保留部分自由社群传统。教会既是精神权威，也是重要的土地和财富拥有者。
socialStructure.en: Novgorodian society consisted of high boyars, merchants, clergy, artisans, freemen, and peasants, with the urban elite dominating politics. Rural dependency was increasing, though some traditions of freer communities remained. The Church was both a spiritual authority and a major holder of land and wealth.

rulingClass.zh: 统治阶层主要是诺夫哥罗德贵族家族、大商人集团与高级教士。受邀大公拥有军事与司法作用，但其权力受市民议会和权贵集团显著制衡。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted mainly of Novgorodian boyar families, major merchant interests, and senior clergy. The invited prince had military and judicial functions, but his power was strongly checked by the civic assembly and oligarchic elites.

succession.zh: 大公并非严格世袭于本地，而是由诺夫哥罗德按照政治需要邀请、罢黜或更换留里克王公。城市内部官职如波萨德尼克和千人长则由本地精英推举或在寡头集团间轮替。
succession.en: The prince was not strictly hereditary within Novgorod itself, but was invited, dismissed, or replaced from among Rurikid princes according to political need. Internal offices such as posadnik and tysyatsky were selected by local elites or rotated among oligarchic factions.

structure.zh: 诺夫哥罗德的核心政治机构是维彻市民议会、波萨德尼克行政体系、大主教教会机构以及受邀大公的军事司法班底。国家治理兼具共和议政、贵族寡头控制与王公传统权威。其广阔北方领地通过属地、税站和地方社群网络维系。
structure.en: Novgorod's core institutions were the veche civic assembly, the posadnik administrative system, the archbishop's ecclesiastical establishment, and the invited prince's military-judicial retinue. Governance combined republican deliberation, boyar oligarchy, and princely authority. Its vast northern lands were held together through dependent territories, tribute points, and local community networks.

name.zh: 市民议会与波萨德尼克署

function.zh: 处理城市政务、对外协商、任官与重大裁决。
function.en: Managed civic administration, foreign negotiation, appointments, and major decisions.

name.zh: 大主教府与教会财产署

function.zh: 管理教产、档案、教会法事务、慈善和部分财政保管。
function.en: Managed church lands, archives, canon-law matters, charity, and part of fiscal custody.

function.zh: 监督商人事务、市场秩序、关税与民兵动员。
function.en: Supervised merchant affairs, market order, customs duties, and militia mobilization.

function.zh: 负责军事统率、部分刑事审判和边境防务。
function.en: Handled military command, portions of criminal justice, and frontier defense.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖属城、乡区、税站和河道节点，由总督、地方长官及社群长者共同运作。北方森林与皮毛产区常通过贡赋和商站控制，而非密集官僚直接统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on subordinate towns, rural districts, tribute posts, and river nodes, operated by governors, local officials, and community elders. Northern forest and fur-producing zones were often controlled through tribute and trading stations rather than dense direct bureaucracy.

legalSystem.zh: 法律基础包括《罗斯法典》传统、地方习惯法、商法惯例和教会法。审判权由市政官、大公代表和教会法庭分掌，商业纠纷处理相对成熟。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on the Russkaya Pravda tradition, local customary law, commercial practice, and canon law. Jurisdiction was shared by civic officials, princely representatives, and church courts, with comparatively developed handling of mercantile disputes.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自关税、市场税、土地与属地贡赋、司法罚金和商路征收。由于诺夫哥罗德的商业性质，贸易税和皮毛贡赋尤为重要。
taxationSystem.en: State income came from customs duties, market taxes, land and territorial tribute, judicial fines, and route tolls. Because Novgorod was highly commercial, trade duties and fur tribute were especially important.

religion.zh: 东正教

culturalAchievements.zh: 诺夫哥罗德以桦树皮信札、圣索菲亚大教堂、木构城市文化和圣像艺术著称。其识字率在中世纪东欧城市中相对较高，商业文书与私人通信极为发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Novgorod was notable for its birch-bark letters, Saint Sophia Cathedral, wooden urban culture, and icon art. Literacy was relatively high for a medieval East European city, and commercial documentation and private correspondence were unusually developed.

languageFamily.zh: 斯拉夫语系东斯拉夫语支
languageFamily.en: Slavic language family, East Slavic branch

philosophy.zh: 东正教信仰、罗斯城市共和传统与实用商人精神并存
philosophy.en: A combination of Eastern Orthodox belief, Rus' urban republican tradition, and pragmatic mercantile outlook

writingSystem.zh: 基里尔字母书写的古东斯拉夫文与教会斯拉夫文
writingSystem.en: Cyrillic writing in Old East Slavic and Church Slavonic

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per person per year

gdpDescription.zh: 诺夫哥罗德是东欧北部最重要的商贸与转运中心之一，经济基础包括农业、手工业、皮毛贸易和跨波罗的海—伏尔加体系交换。其人均产出高于多数同区域农本公国，原因在于城市商业密度和远程贸易收益。
gdpDescription.en: Novgorod was one of northern Eastern Europe's leading trade and transit centers, with an economic base in agriculture, crafts, fur trade, and exchange across the Baltic-Volga system. Its per capita output was higher than that of most neighboring agrarian principalities because of urban commercial density and long-distance trade gains.

mainIndustries.zh: 皮毛贸易、蜂蜡与蜂蜜、亚麻与麻布、金属加工、木工造船、渔业、农业
mainIndustries.en: Fur trade, wax and honey, flax and linen, metalworking, woodworking and boatbuilding, fishing, agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 貂皮、松鼠皮、蜂蜡、蜂蜜、麻布、鱼、盐、金属器、银锭、进口布匹与酒类
tradeGoods.en: Sable and squirrel furs, beeswax, honey, linen, fish, salt, metal goods, silver ingots, imported cloth, and wine

name.zh: 格里夫纳银锭与库纳记账体系
name.en: Grivna silver ingots and kuna accounting system

householdWealth.zh: 城市贵族和大商人拥有显著财富，控制地产、商路和仓储。普通手工业者与农民生活较为稳定，但与上层差距明显。
householdWealth.en: Urban boyars and major merchants held substantial wealth, controlling estates, trade routes, and storage facilities. Ordinary artisans and peasants had more modest but often stable livelihoods, with a clear gap from the elite.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per person per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

economicSystem.zh: 以城市商人资本、属地贡赋和乡村生产相结合的中世纪商业共和经济
economicSystem.en: A medieval commercial republican economy combining urban merchant capital, territorial tribute, and rural production

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent per year

kg of silver equivalent per year

kg of silver equivalent per year

kg of silver equivalent per year
kg of silver equivalent in reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以银锭、皮毛、蜡、谷物储备和教会保管财物构成，部分由大主教府承担保管与信用功能。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of silver ingots, furs, wax, grain reserves, and assets held in ecclesiastical custody, with the archbishopric serving partly as a custodial and credit institution.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策注重维持贸易畅通、保障边防和控制税率，以免削弱商人利益与城市竞争力。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized keeping trade routes open, maintaining frontier defense, and restraining tax burdens so as not to undermine merchant interests and urban competitiveness.

description.zh: 由大公及贵族随从组成，训练和装备优于普通民兵，是野战核心力量。
description.en: Composed of the prince's and boyars' retainers, better trained and equipped than common militia, forming the core field force.

description.zh: 由城区和属城居民征集，适合守城、防御与短期战役。
description.en: Raised from urban residents and subordinate towns, suitable for city defense and short campaigning.

description.zh: 依托河流和湖泊运输作战，兼具护运、巡逻和突袭功能。
description.en: Operated on rivers and lakes for transport, escort, patrol, and raiding functions.

commanderInChief.en: The invited Grand Prince of Novgorod

technology.zh: 军备包括锁子甲、圆盾、长矛、剑、弓弩和攻城器具，具备成熟城防与河运后勤能力，但相较草原军仍欠缺战略机动性。
technology.en: Military equipment included mail armor, round shields, spears, swords, bows and crossbows, and siege tools, with mature fortification and river-logistics capability, though strategic mobility lagged behind steppe armies.

unit.en: kg of silver equivalent per year

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自条顿与瑞典方向的波罗的海竞争、罗斯诸公国内斗争，以及金帐汗国秩序对东北欧政治的间接压力。
threats.en: Main threats came from Baltic competition involving Teutonic and Swedish forces, internal struggles among Rus' princes, and indirect political pressure from the Golden Horde order in northeastern Europe.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 绞盘上弦重弩，可破重甲
description.en: Windlass-drawn heavy crossbow capable of piercing heavy armor

description.zh: 伊斯兰世界弯刀，轻便利于骑战劈斩
description.en: Islamic curved sword, light and ideal for mounted slashing

populationDescription.zh: 诺夫哥罗德人口由城市居民、周边农业人口及广大北方属地居民组成，族群以东斯拉夫人为主，同时包含芬诺-乌戈尔成分与外来商人。城市社会高度商业化，农村地区则保持农业与贡赋混合结构。
populationDescription.en: Novgorod's population included urban inhabitants, surrounding agricultural populations, and residents of vast northern territories; East Slavs were dominant, with Finno-Ugric elements and foreign merchants also present. Urban society was highly commercialized, while the countryside preserved a mixed agricultural and tribute-based structure.

description.zh: 中世纪俄罗斯商业共和国
description.en: Medieval Russian commercial republic

socialClasses.zh: 贵族、商人、教士、手工业者、自由民、农民、属地贡民
socialClasses.en: Boyars, merchants, clergy, artisans, freemen, peasants, and tribute-paying dependent populations

allies.zh: 与部分罗斯公国、波罗的海商人网络及按利益变化结盟的地方势力保持合作。
allies.en: Novgorod cooperated with certain Rus' principalities, Baltic merchant networks, and local powers aligned by changing interests.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌手包括瑞典、条顿骑士团及威胁其贸易和边疆的竞争势力。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included Sweden, the Teutonic Order, and any rival forces threatening its trade and frontiers.

foreignPolicy.zh: 奉行以贸易安全、边疆缓冲和灵活结盟为中心的现实主义外交，同时尽量避免与草原强权直接对撞。
foreignPolicy.en: Its foreign policy was realist and centered on trade security, frontier buffers, and flexible alliances, while generally avoiding direct confrontation with steppe great powers.

era.zh: 中世纪东欧高峰期城市—商贸技术体系
era.en: High medieval East European urban-commercial technological system

keyInnovations.zh: 桦树皮信札文书、成熟木构城防、河运物流网络、金属和皮革手工业、规范化市场度量
keyInnovations.en: Birch-bark written documentation, advanced wooden fortifications, riverine logistics networks, metal and leather crafts, and more standardized market measures

strengths.zh: 贸易网络广、财政弹性较强、城市识字与文书能力突出，且具备稳固的城防和北方资源腹地。
strengths.en: It possessed wide trade networks, comparatively flexible finances, notable literacy and documentary capacity, and strong fortifications backed by a vast northern resource hinterland.

weaknesses.zh: 政治受寡头内斗制约，农村统合有限，军事动员虽强但持续野战能力不如高度集中政权。
weaknesses.en: Politics were constrained by oligarchic factionalism, rural integration was limited, and although mobilization was strong, sustained field warfare was weaker than in more centralized states.

outlook.zh: 1280年前后诺夫哥罗德仍处于稳定与繁荣阶段，但长期将面临西方军事压力与东北罗斯新兴强权的挑战。
outlook.en: Around 1280 Novgorod remained stable and prosperous, but in the long run it would face western military pressure and the rise of stronger northeastern Rus' rivals.

description.zh: 诺夫哥罗德是13世纪末东欧北部以商业、皮毛贸易和寡头共和制度著称的强盛城市共和国。
description.en: Novgorod was a powerful late thirteenth-century northern East European city-republic renowned for commerce, fur trade, and oligarchic republican institutions.

dynasty.zh: 无王朝的因纽人猎群联盟

governmentForm.zh: 以季节性猎群为核心、由资深猎手与家族长者协商决策的部落治理形式。
governmentForm.en: A tribal system centered on seasonal hunting bands, with decisions made through consensus among experienced hunters and family elders.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以若干流动性很强的亲族猎群组成，冬夏随驯鹿、河谷渔场与海岸资源而迁徙。家庭与扩展亲族是生产和分配的基本单位，声望主要来自狩猎能力、慷慨分配与灵力声誉。萨满在精神事务上具有重要影响，但通常不形成脱离亲族网络的正式统治阶层。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of highly mobile kin-based hunting bands that moved seasonally with caribou herds, river fisheries, and coastal resources. The household and extended kin group formed the basic units of production and distribution, while prestige came chiefly from hunting skill, generosity, and spiritual reputation. Shamans held major influence in ritual matters but usually did not constitute a separate formal ruling estate.

rulingClass.zh: 并无固定贵族阶层，实际领导者多为成功猎手、长者与能协调婚姻和馈赠关系的人物。萨满在危机、疾病与狩猎仪式中可左右公共决策。
rulingClass.en: There was no fixed hereditary nobility; effective leaders were usually successful hunters, elders, and figures able to manage marriage and gift relationships. Shamans could strongly shape communal decisions in times of crisis, illness, and hunting ritual.

succession.zh: 首领地位通常不完全世袭，而是在亲族内部或相邻家庭之间凭资历、能力与支持自然形成。若领导者失去声望，猎群成员可分散并追随其他带领者。
succession.en: Leadership was not strictly hereditary but emerged within kin groups or neighboring families through seniority, competence, and support. If a leader lost prestige, band members could disperse and follow another organizer.

structure.zh: 政体松散，缺乏常设首都和固定官僚机构。主要事务在季节营地中通过长者商议解决，涉及狩猎分工、迁徙路线、婚配安排与纠纷调解。
structure.en: The polity was loose, lacking a permanent capital or fixed bureaucracy. Most public matters were handled in seasonal camps through councils of elders dealing with hunting organization, migration routes, marriage arrangements, and dispute settlement.

function.zh: 协调迁徙、猎场分配、家庭纠纷与对外关系。
function.en: Coordinated migration, hunting-ground allocation, family disputes, and external relations.

function.zh: 主持治病、祭仪、禁忌解释与重大集体狩猎前的精神准备。
function.en: Oversaw healing, rituals, taboo interpretation, and spiritual preparation before major communal hunts.

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理即各猎群自理，沿河谷、森林与海岸分布的营地在资源季节上保持联系。没有正式行政区，影响力范围取决于亲族网络与习惯使用地。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was effectively self-government by individual bands distributed across river valleys, forests, and coastal areas while remaining tied by seasonal resource cycles. There were no formal districts; spheres of influence depended on kin networks and customary use areas.

legalSystem.zh: 法律以习惯法、长者仲裁和赔偿原则为主，强调恢复群体和谐。严重冲突可能通过分营、婚姻补偿或馈赠和解处理。
legalSystem.en: Law was based on custom, elder arbitration, and compensatory principles, with emphasis on restoring harmony. Serious conflicts could be resolved through band fission, marriage compensation, or gift settlement.

taxationSystem.zh: 无正式税制，公共需要通过共享猎获、礼物交换和在大型营地中的义务劳动来满足。首领与萨满依靠声望获得更多份额，而非强制征收。
taxationSystem.en: There was no formal taxation; communal needs were met through shared game, gift exchange, and obligatory labor within larger camps. Leaders and shamans received larger shares through prestige rather than coercive collection.

religion.zh: 萨满教

culturalAchievements.zh: 因纽人擅长在北方森林与苔原交界地带建立精细的季节性生计知识，尤其是驯鹿迁徙、河川渔汛与雪地行猎经验。口述叙事、仪式歌唱与皮革、骨角器制作体现出高度适应性的文化传统。
culturalAchievements.en: The Innu developed refined seasonal ecological knowledge for the northern forest-tundra transition, especially concerning caribou movements, river fisheries, and winter travel. Oral storytelling, ritual song, and skilled leather and bone-working reflected a highly adaptive cultural tradition.

languageFamily.zh: 阿尔冈昆语系
languageFamily.en: Algonquian language family

philosophy.zh: 以泛灵观、狩猎伦理与人与动物互惠关系为核心。
philosophy.en: Centered on animistic cosmology, hunting ethics, and reciprocal relations between humans and animals.

writingSystem.zh: 无本土文字，历史与知识主要通过口述传统、歌谣和地名记忆传承。
writingSystem.en: No indigenous writing system; history and knowledge were transmitted through oral tradition, songs, and place-name memory.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以自给性狩猎、捕鱼、采集和小规模物物交换为主，货币化程度极低。若折算为白银等值，总产出规模有限，但对当地生态条件具有较强适应性。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based mainly on subsistence hunting, fishing, gathering, and small-scale barter, with very low monetization. When converted into silver equivalent, total output was modest but well adapted to local ecological conditions.

mainIndustries.zh: 驯鹿与小兽狩猎、捕鱼、皮革加工、骨角器制作、采集
mainIndustries.en: Caribou and small-game hunting, fishing, hide processing, bone and antler working, gathering

tradeGoods.zh: 兽皮、骨角器、干鱼、驯鹿制品、桦树皮器物
tradeGoods.en: Furs, bone and antler tools, dried fish, caribou products, birchbark goods

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现为皮帐、衣物、雪地行具、骨角工具、犬只与储存食物，而非贵金属积累。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was expressed mainly through tents, clothing, winter travel gear, bone tools, dogs, and stored food rather than precious-metal accumulation.

unit.en: taels of silver per person per year (estimated)

economicSystem.zh: 以亲族共享、季节分配和有限交换为特征的狩猎采集经济。
economicSystem.en: A hunting-gathering economy characterized by kin-based sharing, seasonal allocation, and limited exchange.

treasuryDescription.zh: 并无集中金库，所谓储备主要是皮货、干肉、工具与少量可交换珍贵物品的等值总和。
treasuryDescription.en: There was no centralized treasury; reserves consisted mainly of the equivalent value of furs, dried meat, tools, and a small stock of exchangeable valuables.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政重在储备食物与维持群体互助，而非积累货币盈余。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal practice emphasized food reserves and mutual support rather than accumulation of monetary surplus.

description.zh: 以弓、矛、木棍和地形伏击为主，平时兼任狩猎生产。
description.en: Used bows, spears, clubs, and ambush tactics, while normally doubling as subsistence hunters.

description.zh: 负责在营地迁徙、遭遇袭扰或食物紧张时保卫家庭和储藏。
description.en: Protected households and stores during camp movement, raids, or periods of food stress.

commanderInChief.zh: 无统一总司令，由各猎队首领临时统率
commanderInChief.en: No single commander-in-chief; band leaders commanded on a temporary basis

technology.zh: 军事技术较低，主要依赖弓箭、骨角与石制或少量铜铁器、皮甲和熟悉森林地形的机动能力。
technology.en: Military technology was limited, relying chiefly on bows, bone or stone and some copper or iron tools, hide protection, and mobility in forest terrain.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自邻近部族竞争猎场、报复性冲突、饥荒年份的资源压力与严酷气候。
threats.en: Main threats came from neighboring tribal competition over hunting grounds, retaliatory conflict, resource pressure in bad years, and severe climate.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 骑兵冲锋长矛，骑士战核心武器
description.en: Cavalry charge lance, core weapon of knightly warfare

description.zh: 英格兰长弓手主力弓，射程可达300步
description.en: English longbow, effective range up to 300 paces

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀疏分布在拉布拉多与加拿大东部内陆森林地带，多为小型流动营地。人口增长受气候、猎物周期与婴幼儿存活率强烈影响。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparsely spread across Labrador and the eastern Canadian interior forests, mostly in small mobile camps. Population growth was strongly shaped by climate, game cycles, and infant survival rates.

name.zh: 无固定都邑—梅拉蒂努营地群
name.en: No fixed town - Melatinu camp cluster

name.zh: 无固定都邑—内陆驯鹿渡口营地
name.en: No fixed town - Inland caribou crossing camp

socialClasses.zh: 社会分层较弱，主要区分为长者、猎手、萨满、普通家庭成员与俘虏或边缘依附者。
socialClasses.en: Social stratification was weak, with broad distinctions among elders, hunters, shamans, ordinary household members, and occasional captives or marginal dependents.

allies.zh: 以邻近友好猎群和通婚网络为主，无稳定跨地区同盟。
allies.en: Alliances were mainly with neighboring friendly bands and marriage networks, without stable wide-ranging alliances.

enemies.zh: 与部分周边阿尔冈昆语或爱斯基摩-阿留申语群体可能因猎场和沿海资源发生冲突。
enemies.en: Conflicts could arise with some surrounding Algonquian or Eskimo-Aleut groups over hunting grounds and coastal resources.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维护迁徙通道、避免长期消耗性战争和保持交换关系为目标。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve migration corridors, avoid long attritional warfare, and maintain exchange ties.

era.zh: 北美亚寒带晚期石骨器—早期金属接触前阶段
era.en: Subarctic North American late lithic-bone toolkit to pre-intensive metal-contact stage

keyInnovations.zh: 精细的雪地旅行技术、桦树皮器具、兽皮加工、驯鹿围猎协作与口述地理记忆系统。
